   227	  public func startCapture() async throws -> AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> {
   228	    guard !isCapturing else {
   229	      return AsyncStream { $0.finish() }
   230	    }
   231	    try await startEnginePhase()
   232	    return try await beginCapturePhase()
   233	  }
   234	
   235	  public func stopCapture() async -> CaptureResult {
   236	    guard let source = activeSource else {
   237	      let samples = capturedSamples
   238	      capturedSamples = []
   239	      return CaptureResult(samples: samples)
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    // Snapshot identity BEFORE any teardown path so the pipeline's post-stop
   243	    // Sentry extras still resolve when warmEnginePolicy == .off nils activeSource
   244	    // synchronously (#285).
   245	    cachedCaptureGeneration = source.captureGeneration
   246	    cachedSourceType = source.captureSourceType
   247	
   248	    isCapturing = false
   249	    audioLevel = 0.0
   250	
   251	    // Deactivate capture but keep engine warm. The tap stays installed and the
   252	    // pre-roll ring buffer continues capturing audio. On the next recording,
   253	    // prepare() sees the engine is already running and skips startup.
   254	    // This eliminates first-word clipping by ensuring the ring buffer has
   255	    // audio from before the user pressed the key.
   256	    source.deactivateCapture()
   257	
   258	    // Keep activeSource alive — resolveSource() will reuse it if still running.
   259	    // BT state changes are handled by resolveSource() re-evaluating the route.
   260	
   261	    // Schedule full teardown after idle period. Engine stays warm for rapid-fire
   262	    // dictation but doesn't run indefinitely.
   263	    scheduleWarmEngineTeardown()
   264	
   265	    // Samples accumulated via onSamples callback -> manager.capturedSamples.
   266	    // In-process path returns empty vadSegments; pipeline owns its own SilenceDetector.
   267	    let samples = capturedSamples
   268	    capturedSamples = []
   269	    return CaptureResult(samples: samples)
   270	  }
   271	
   272	  public func rebuildEngine() {
   273	    activeSource?.rebuild()
   274	  }
   275	
   276	  public func buildEngine(noiseSuppression: Bool) {
   277	    noiseSuppressionEnabled = noiseSuppression
   278	    // buildEngine is called at app startup for VP config. Create an engine source
   279	    // for now — startEnginePhase will re-resolve if BT state requires capture session.
   280	    // If re-resolved to capture session, this engine source is discarded (no resources held —
   281	    // buildEngine only creates an AVAudioEngine object, doesn't start it or install taps).
   282	    if let engineSource = activeSource as? AVAudioEngineSource {
   283	      engineSource.buildEngine(noiseSuppression: noiseSuppression)
   284	    } else {
   285	      // Tear down any existing non-engine source before replacing
   286	      activeSource?.rebuild()
   287	      let engineSource = AVAudioEngineSource()
   288	      engineSource.buildEngine(noiseSuppression: noiseSuppression)
   289	      activeSource = engineSource
   290	    }
   291	  }
   292	
   293	  public func preWarm() async throws {
   294	    let preWarmStart = ContinuousClock.now
   295	    let source = resolveSource()
   296	    let resolveMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - preWarmStart)
   297	    guard !source.isRunning else {
   298	      Self.btRouteLog(
   299	        "COLD-START preWarm(): engine already running (warm hit) resolveSource=\(resolveMs)ms")
   300	      return
   301	    }
   302	    do {
   303	      try await source.prepare()
   304	    } catch {
   305	      Task {
   306	        await AppLogger.shared.log(
   307	          "Audio pre-warm failed: \(error.localizedDescription)",
   308	          level: .info, category: "Audio"
   309	        )
   310	      }
   311	      // Issue #289: propagate so callers can abort the start cleanly.
   312	      throw error
   313	    }
   314	    let prepareMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - preWarmStart)
   315	    let stabStart = ContinuousClock.now
   316	    let stabilized = await source.waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: 1.5, pollInterval: 0.2)
   317	    let stabMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - stabStart)
   318	    let totalMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - preWarmStart)
   319	    Self.btRouteLog(
   320	      "COLD-START preWarm(): total=\(totalMs)ms | resolve=\(resolveMs)ms prepare=\(prepareMs)ms formatStab=\(stabMs)ms stabilized=\(stabilized)"
   321	    )
   322	  }
   323	
   324	  /// Convert Duration to milliseconds for logging.
   325	  nonisolated private static func ms(_ d: Duration) -> Int {
   326	    let (seconds, attoseconds) = d.components
   327	    return Int(seconds) * 1000 + Int(attoseconds / 1_000_000_000_000_000)
   328	  }
   329	
   330	  public func abortPreWarm() {
   331	    activeSource?.abortPrepare()
   332	  }
   333	
   334	  public func waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: TimeInterval, pollInterval: TimeInterval) async
   335	    -> Bool
   336	  {
   337	    guard let source = activeSource else { return true }
   338	    return await source.waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: maxWait, pollInterval: pollInterval)
   339	  }
   340	
   341	  // MARK: - Warm Engine Management
   342	
   343	  /// Schedule engine teardown based on the current warm engine policy.
   344	  /// Called after every recording stops. Cancelled if a new recording starts.
   345	  private func scheduleWarmEngineTeardown() {
   346	    warmEngineTeardownTask?.cancel()
   347	    warmEngineTeardownTask = nil
   348	
   349	    switch warmEnginePolicy {
   350	    case .off:
   351	      performEngineTeardown()
   352	      return
   353	    case .always:
   354	      idleSince = clock.now
   355	      return
   356	    case .seconds10, .seconds30, .seconds60:
   357	      break
   358	    }
   359	
   360	    let timeout = policyTimeout(warmEnginePolicy)
   361	    idleSince = clock.now
   362	    warmEngineTeardownTask = Task { [weak self] in
   363	      try? await Task.sleep(for: timeout)
   364	      guard !Task.isCancelled, let self, !self.isCapturing else { return }
   365	      self.performEngineTeardown()
   366	    }
   367	  }
   368	
   369	  /// In-flight engine stop task. Stored so resolveSource() can cancel it
   370	  /// if a new recording starts before the stop completes, preventing two
   371	  /// engines from running simultaneously on the same hardware.
   372	  private var engineStopTask: Task<Void, Never>?
   373	
   374	  /// Tear down the warm engine. Clears activeSource synchronously before
   375	  /// awaiting stop to prevent stale async completions from clobbering new state.
   376	  private func performEngineTeardown() {
   377	    idleSince = nil
   378	    guard let source = activeSource else { return }
   379	    activeSource = nil
   380	    Self.btRouteLog("Warm engine teardown")
   381	    engineStopTask = Task { [weak self] in
   382	      _ = await source.stop()
   383	      self?.engineStopTask = nil
   384	    }
   385	  }
   386	
   387	  /// Reconcile engine state when the policy changes while idle.
   388	  /// Called automatically by the warmEnginePolicy setter.
   389	  private func reconcileWarmEnginePolicy() {
   390	    // If capturing, new policy applies on next stopCapture.
   391	    guard !isCapturing else { return }
   392	    // If engine is not warm, nothing to reconcile.
   393	    guard activeSource != nil else { return }
   394	
   395	    warmEngineTeardownTask?.cancel()
   396	    warmEngineTeardownTask = nil
   397	
   398	    switch warmEnginePolicy {
   399	    case .off:
   400	      performEngineTeardown()
   401	    case .always:
   402	      // Keep warm indefinitely, preserve idleSince.
   403	      break
   404	    case .seconds10, .seconds30, .seconds60:
   405	      let timeout = policyTimeout(warmEnginePolicy)
   406	      let elapsed = idleSince.map { clock.now - $0 } ?? .zero
   407	      if elapsed >= timeout {
   408	        performEngineTeardown()
   409	      } else {
   410	        let remaining = timeout - elapsed
   411	        warmEngineTeardownTask = Task { [weak self] in
   412	          try? await Task.sleep(for: remaining)
   413	          guard !Task.isCancelled, let self, !self.isCapturing else { return }
   414	          self.performEngineTeardown()
   415	        }
   416	      }
   417	    }
   418	  }
   419	
   420	  /// Map a timed policy case to a Duration.
   421	  private func policyTimeout(_ policy: WarmEnginePolicy) -> Duration {
   422	    switch policy {
   423	    case .seconds10: .seconds(10)
   424	    case .seconds30: .seconds(30)
   425	    case .seconds60: .seconds(60)
   426	    default: .seconds(30)
   427	    }
   428	  }
   429	
   430	  // MARK: - Source Management
   431	
   432	  /// Resolve and create the appropriate capture source based on BT state and user preference.
   433	  /// Re-evaluates on every call — BT state may change between recordings.
   434	  private func resolveSource() -> any AudioInputSource {
   435	    // Cancel idle teardown — we're about to record
   436	    warmEngineTeardownTask?.cancel()
   437	    warmEngineTeardownTask = nil
   438	    // Cancel any in-flight engine stop from a previous teardown.
   439	    engineStopTask?.cancel()
   440	    engineStopTask = nil
   441	
   442	    // If a source is already running (warm engine), check route compatibility
   443	    if let existing = activeSource, existing.isRunning {
   444	      let decision = routeResolver.resolve(
   445	        preferredInputDeviceUID: preferredInputDeviceIDOverride,
   446	        noiseSuppression: noiseSuppressionEnabled
   447	      )
   448	      let existingIsEngine = existing is AVAudioEngineSource
   449	      let wantsEngine = decision.sourceType == .audioEngine
   450	
   451	      // Check full config signature, not just source type.
   452	      // Device/VP changes between recordings must trigger rebuild.
   453	      var configMatch = existingIsEngine == wantsEngine
   454	      if configMatch, let engineSource = existing as? AVAudioEngineSource {
   455	        // Compare effective device selection: preferredInputDeviceIDOverride
   456	        // takes priority, fall back to selectedInputDeviceUID when empty.
   457	        let oldEffective =
   458	          engineSource.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride.isEmpty
   459	          ? engineSource.selectedInputDeviceUID
   460	          : engineSource.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba Sources/EnviousWisprAudio/AudioCaptureManager.swift | sed -n '461,575p'" in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
   461	        let newEffective =
   462	          preferredInputDeviceIDOverride.isEmpty
   463	          ? selectedInputDeviceUID
   464	          : preferredInputDeviceIDOverride
   465	        let deviceMatch = oldEffective == newEffective
   466	        let vpMatch = engineSource.noiseSuppressionEnabled == noiseSuppressionEnabled
   467	        configMatch = deviceMatch && vpMatch
   468	      }
   469	
   470	      if configMatch {
   471	        // Route and config unchanged, reuse warm source
   472	        lastRouteDecision = decision
   473	        Self.btRouteLog("Reusing warm \(wantsEngine ? "engine" : "capture session") source")
   474	        return existing
   475	      }
   476	      // Route changed (e.g., BT connected/disconnected) — synchronous teardown.
   477	      // Must be synchronous to avoid racing with new source's prepare() on same hardware.
   478	      Self.btRouteLog("Route changed while warm — tearing down old source")
   479	      existing.rebuild()
   480	      activeSource = nil
   481	    }
   482	
   483	    let decision = routeResolver.resolve(
   484	      preferredInputDeviceUID: preferredInputDeviceIDOverride,
   485	      noiseSuppression: noiseSuppressionEnabled
   486	    )
   487	    lastRouteDecision = decision
   488	
   489	    // Structured telemetry log
   490	    Self.btRouteLog(
   491	      "Route decision: source=\(decision.sourceType), reason=\(decision.reason.rawValue), vp=\(decision.vpAvailable), fallback=\(decision.fallbackAllowed) — \(decision.rationale)"
   492	    )
   493	    Task {
   494	      await AppLogger.shared.log(
   495	        "Capture route: \(decision.reason.rawValue) → \(decision.sourceType == .captureSession ? "AVCaptureSession" : "AVAudioEngine"), VP=\(decision.vpAvailable)",
   496	        level: .info, category: "Audio"
   497	      )
   498	    }
   499	
   500	    let source: any AudioInputSource
   501	    switch decision.sourceType {
   502	    case .captureSession:
   503	      if decision.vpAvailable == false && noiseSuppressionEnabled {
   504	        Self.btRouteLog(
   505	          "Noise suppression unavailable on AVCaptureSession path — VP requires AVAudioEngine to own input"
   506	        )
   507	      }
   508	      source = AVCaptureSessionSource()
   509	    case .audioEngine:
   510	      let engineSource = AVAudioEngineSource()
   511	      engineSource.noiseSuppressionEnabled = noiseSuppressionEnabled
   512	      engineSource.selectedInputDeviceUID = selectedInputDeviceUID
   513	      engineSource.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride = preferredInputDeviceIDOverride
   514	      source = engineSource
   515	    }
   516	
   517	    activeSource = source
   518	    return source
   519	  }
   520	
   521	  // MARK: - BT Route Logging (Step 6 instrumentation)
   522	
   523	  /// Default sink path; cross-process write target shared by main app + XPC service.
   524	  /// Phase R4: bounded by `RotatingFileSink` at 5 MB × 3 files = 15 MB ceiling.
   525	  nonisolated private static let btRouteLogURL: URL = FileManager.default
   526	    .homeDirectoryForCurrentUser
   527	    .appendingPathComponent("Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/bt-route.log")
   528	
   529	  /// Bounded sink for BT route diagnostics. `nonisolated static` matches the
   530	  /// prior surface so existing sync callers keep their call shape; the sink
   531	  /// itself uses `OSAllocatedUnfairLock` + `flock` for safety across the main
   532	  /// app and XPC service writing concurrently.
   533	  nonisolated static let btRouteSink = RotatingFileSink(
   534	    path: btRouteLogURL,
   535	    maxSize: 5 * 1_024 * 1_024,
   536	    maxFiles: 3)
   537	
   538	  /// Direct file write for BT route diagnostics. os_log info level is suppressed on macOS 26 beta,
   539	  /// and AppLogger.shared is process-local (XPC service has its own instance).
   540	  nonisolated static func btRouteLog(_ message: String) {
   541	    let timestamp = ISO8601DateFormatter().string(from: Date())
   542	    let line = "[\(timestamp)] [BTRoute] \(message)\n"
   543	    btRouteSink.append(line)
   544	  }
   545	
   546	  // MARK: - VAD Interface (Step 5)
   547	
   548	  /// No-op for in-process capture. The in-process path manages VAD entirely through
   549	  /// pipeline-owned properties (vadAutoStop, vadSensitivity, etc.) and the pipeline's
   550	  /// monitorVAD() loop. The capture manager never runs VAD itself.
   551	  /// Exists solely for AudioCaptureInterface protocol conformance.
   552	  public func configureVAD(
   553	    autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool
   554	  ) {
   555	    // Intentional no-op — see comment above.
   556	  }
   557	
   558	  /// Returns a slice of capturedSamples starting at fromIndex plus the current total count.
   559	  /// Both values are from the same snapshot moment for consistency.
   560	  public func getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: Int) async -> (samples: [Float], totalCount: Int) {
   561	    let totalCount = capturedSamples.count
   562	    let clampedIndex = max(0, min(fromIndex, totalCount))
   563	    if clampedIndex >= totalCount {
   564	      return (samples: [], totalCount: totalCount)
   565	    }
   566	    let slice = Array(capturedSamples[clampedIndex..<totalCount])
   567	    return (samples: slice, totalCount: totalCount)
   568	  }
   569	
   570	  /// Returns empty — in-process VAD segments are owned by the pipeline's SilenceDetector,
   571	  /// not by the capture manager. Only meaningful for the XPC path.
   572	  public func getVADSegments() async -> [SpeechSegment] {
   573	    return []
   574	  }
   575	}

     5	/// XPC-backed implementation of `AudioCaptureInterface`.
     6	///
     7	/// Bridges the in-process `AudioCaptureInterface` contract to XPC calls against the
     8	/// embedded `EnviousWisprAudioService`. Real audio capture runs in the service process;
     9	/// the proxy handles connection lifecycle, buffer reconstruction, and state management.
    10	///
    11	/// **Connection lifecycle (Step 1.5 design rules):**
    12	/// - `interruptionHandler`: if capturing → user-visible failure (reset state, fire onEngineInterrupted).
    13	///   If idle → transient (set needsReinit only). Always keep the same connection.
    14	/// - `invalidationHandler`: terminal — nil connection, recreate on next use.
    15	@MainActor
    16	@Observable
    17	public final class AudioCaptureProxy: AudioCaptureInterface {
    18

    79	
    80	  /// Generation counter to reject stale callbacks from previous capture sessions.
    81	  /// Incremented on beginCapturePhase, stopCapture, and interruption.
    82	  /// Checked in audioBufferCaptured (inside @MainActor Task) before yielding.
    83	  private var captureGeneration: UInt64 = 0
    84	
    85	  /// The generation that was active when the current capture session began.
    86	  /// Set in beginCapturePhase, compared in audioBufferCaptured.
    87	  private var activeCaptureGeneration: UInt64 = 0
    88

    91	  /// Private serial queue that fires the stall `DispatchWorkItem`.
    92	  private static let stallQueue = DispatchQueue(
    93	    label: "com.enviouswispr.audio.capture-stall.proxy"
    94	  )
    95	  /// Pending stall watchdog. Cancelled on stop / interrupt / invalidate /
    96	  /// on every new session's arm. Fires on MainActor via Task hop.
    97	  private var stallWorkItem: DispatchWorkItem?
    98	  /// Flips true on the MainActor inside `audioBufferCaptured` when any buffer
    99	  /// for the active session reaches us. Reset to false when arming.
   100	  private var hasReceivedBufferThisSession: Bool = false
   101	  /// Uptime-ns captured when the current watchdog armed; threaded into
   102	  /// `CaptureStallContext` for latency diagnostics.
   103	  private var stallArmedAtUptimeNs: UInt64 = 0
   104

   108	    commonFormat: .pcmFormatFloat32,
   109	    sampleRate: 16000,
   110	    channels: 1,
   111	    interleaved: false
   112	  )!

   117	  #if DEBUG

   119	    /// buffers are silently dropped before reaching the continuation or
   120	    /// `onBufferCaptured`. Decrements per drop until 0, then normal delivery
   121	    /// resumes. Drives Lane A scenario A5 ("forced audio buffer stall") via
   122	    /// the DEBUG localhost endpoint; can also be set directly from Lane C
   123	    /// tests via `@testable import EnviousWisprAudio`.
   124	    ///
   125	    /// `package` access: callable from `DebugFaultEndpoint` in the app target
   126	    /// (same SPM package). Inert in release builds — this property does not

   129	    /// See `Tests/RuntimeUAT/SCENARIOS.md` for negative-control documentation.

   137	  public func startEnginePhase() async throws {
   138	    // Derive route label app-side (same BT check as CaptureRouteResolver).
   139	    if let outID = AudioDeviceEnumerator.defaultOutputDeviceID(),
   140	      AudioDeviceEnumerator.isBluetoothDevice(outID)
   141	    {
   142	      currentAudioRoute = "capture_session_bt"
   143	    } else {
   144	      currentAudioRoute = "built_in_mic"
   145	    }
   146	
   147	    ensureConnection()
   148	    resendConfigIfNeeded()
   149	    try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { (cont: CheckedContinuation<Void, any Error>) in
   150	      let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   151	      serviceProxy { proxy in
   152	        proxy.startEnginePhase(
   153	          preferredDeviceUID: self.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride,
   154	          selectedDeviceUID: self.selectedInputDeviceUID
   155	        ) { nsError in
   156	          if let error = nsError { guard_.resume(throwing: error) } else { guard_.resume() }
   157	        }
   158	      } onProxyError: {
   159	        guard_.resume(throwing: XPCTransportError.serviceUnreachable)
   160	      }
   161	    }
   162	  }
   163	
   164	  public func beginCapturePhase() async throws -> AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> {
   165	    ensureConnection()
   166	
   167	    // Finish any stale continuation from a previous session.
   168	    bufferContinuation?.finish()
   169	    bufferContinuation = nil
   170	
   171	    captureGeneration &+= 1
   172	    activeCaptureGeneration = captureGeneration
   173	
   174	    let stream = AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> { continuation in
   175	      self.bufferContinuation = continuation
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { (cont: CheckedContinuation<Void, any Error>) in
   179	      let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   180	      serviceProxy { proxy in
   181	        proxy.beginCapture { nsError in
   182	          if let error = nsError { guard_.resume(throwing: error) } else { guard_.resume() }
   183	        }
   184	      } onProxyError: {
   185	        guard_.resume(throwing: XPCTransportError.serviceUnreachable)
   186	      }
   187	    }
   188	
   189	    isCapturing = true
   190	    hasReceivedBufferThisSession = false
   191	    armCaptureStallWatchdog()
   192	    return stream
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  /// Arm the one-shot stall watchdog for the current `activeCaptureGeneration`.
   196	  private func armCaptureStallWatchdog() {
   197	    stallWorkItem?.cancel()
   198	    let armedSession = activeCaptureGeneration
   199	    let armedAtNs = DispatchTime.now().uptimeNanoseconds
   200	    stallArmedAtUptimeNs = armedAtNs
   201	    let item = Self.makeStallWorkItem(
   202	      armedSession: armedSession, armedAtNs: armedAtNs, proxy: self)
   203	    stallWorkItem = item
   204	    Self.stallQueue.asyncAfter(
   205	      deadline: .now() + .milliseconds(TimingConstants.audioCaptureStallWindowMs),
   206	      execute: item
   207	    )
   208	  }
   209	
   210	  /// Build the watchdog closure in a nonisolated context so it does NOT inherit
   211	  /// the enclosing `@MainActor` isolation. Swift 6 otherwise inserts executor
   212	  /// checks that fire `dispatch_assert_queue_fail` when the work item runs on
   213	  /// the stall queue. Same escape-hatch pattern as `makeInterruptionHandler`.
   214	  nonisolated private static func makeStallWorkItem(
   215	    armedSession: UInt64,
   216	    armedAtNs: UInt64,
   217	    proxy: AudioCaptureProxy
   218	  ) -> DispatchWorkItem {
   219	    return DispatchWorkItem { [weak proxy] in
   220	      Task { @MainActor [weak proxy] in
   221	        proxy?.captureStallWatchdogFired(
   222	          armedSession: armedSession, armedAtNs: armedAtNs)
   223	      }
   224	    }
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  private func captureStallWatchdogFired(armedSession: UInt64, armedAtNs: UInt64) {
   228	    guard activeCaptureGeneration == armedSession else { return }
   229	    guard isCapturing else { return }
   230	    guard !hasReceivedBufferThisSession else { return }
   231	
   232	    let ctx = CaptureStallContext(
   233	      sessionID: armedSession,
   234	      armedAtUptimeNs: armedAtNs,
   235	      firedAtUptimeNs: DispatchTime.now().uptimeNanoseconds,
   236	      route: currentAudioRoute,
   237	      sourceType: "xpc_proxy",
   238	      engineStartedSuccessfully: true,
   239	      tapInstalled: true,
   240	      formatMismatchObserved: false,

 succeeded in 0ms:
   241	      inputDeviceUIDPreferred: preferredInputDeviceIDOverride.isEmpty
   242	        ? nil : preferredInputDeviceIDOverride,
   243	      inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault: AudioDeviceEnumerator.defaultInputDeviceUID()
   244	    )
   245	    onCaptureStalled?(ctx)
   246	  }
   247	
   248	  // periphery:ignore - protocol conformance (AudioCaptureInterface)
   249	  public func startCapture() async throws -> AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> {
   250	    guard !isCapturing else { return AsyncStream { $0.finish() } }
   251	    try await startEnginePhase()
   252	    return try await beginCapturePhase()
   253	  }
   254	
   255	  public func stopCapture() async -> CaptureResult {
   256	    // Cancel any pending stall watchdog before the session id rolls.
   257	    stallWorkItem?.cancel()
   258	    stallWorkItem = nil
   259	    // Snapshot the session id BEFORE the generation bump so reply-path
   260	    // failures report the session the caller was actually stopping.
   261	    let endingSession = activeCaptureGeneration
   262	    // Bump generation so stale callbacks from this session don't leak into the next.
   263	    captureGeneration &+= 1
   264	
   265	    var result = CaptureResult(samples: [])
   266	    do {
   267	      result = try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation {
   268	        (cont: CheckedContinuation<CaptureResult, any Error>) in
   269	        let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   270	        serviceProxy { proxy in
   271	          proxy.stopCapture { sampleData, vadData in
   272	            let samples = Self.dataToFloats(sampleData)
   273	            let segments = Self.decodeVADSegments(vadData)
   274	            guard_.resume(returning: CaptureResult(samples: samples, vadSegments: segments))
   275	          }
   276	        } onProxyError: { [weak self] in
   277	          self?.reportXPCReplyFailure(stage: "stop_capture", sessionID: endingSession)
   278	          guard_.resume(returning: CaptureResult(samples: []))
   279	        }
   280	      }
   281	    } catch {
   282	      // XPC error — service crashed during stopCapture. Samples are lost.
   283	      // The interruptionHandler fires independently and handles pipeline notification.
   284	      // Do NOT call onEngineInterrupted here to avoid double-firing.
   285	      Task {
   286	        await AppLogger.shared.log(
   287	          "[AudioCaptureProxy] stopCapture failed — service unreachable, samples lost: \(error)",
   288	          level: .info, category: "XPC"
   289	        )
   290	      }
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    isCapturing = false
   294	    audioLevel = 0
   295	    bufferContinuation?.finish()
   296	    bufferContinuation = nil
   297	    return result
   298	  }
   299	
   300	  public func rebuildEngine() {
   301	    serviceProxy { proxy in proxy.rebuildEngine() }
   302	  }
   303	
   304	  public func buildEngine(noiseSuppression: Bool) {
   305	    noiseSuppressionEnabled = noiseSuppression
   306	    ensureConnection()
   307	    resendConfigIfNeeded()
   308	    serviceProxy { proxy in proxy.buildEngine(noiseSuppression: noiseSuppression) }
   309	  }
   310	
   311	  public func preWarm() async throws {
   312	    let proxyStart = ContinuousClock.now
   313	
   314	    // Derive route label so Sentry telemetry is populated even when
   315	    // startEnginePhase() is skipped on the next recording (warm engine reuse).
   316	    if let outID = AudioDeviceEnumerator.defaultOutputDeviceID(),
   317	      AudioDeviceEnumerator.isBluetoothDevice(outID)
   318	    {
   319	      currentAudioRoute = "capture_session_bt"
   320	    } else {
   321	      currentAudioRoute = "built_in_mic"
   322	    }
   323	
   324	    ensureConnection()
   325	    resendConfigIfNeeded()
   326	    let connMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - proxyStart)
   327	    // Phase 1: start engine
   328	    let enginePhaseStart = ContinuousClock.now
   329	    do {
   330	      try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { (cont: CheckedContinuation<Void, any Error>) in
   331	        let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   332	        serviceProxy { proxy in
   333	          proxy.startEnginePhase(
   334	            preferredDeviceUID: self.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride,
   335	            selectedDeviceUID: self.selectedInputDeviceUID
   336	          ) { nsError in
   337	            if let error = nsError { guard_.resume(throwing: error) } else { guard_.resume() }
   338	          }
   339	        } onProxyError: {
   340	          guard_.resume(throwing: XPCTransportError.serviceUnreachable)
   341	        }
   342	      }
   343	    } catch {
   344	      Task {
   345	        await AppLogger.shared.log(
   346	          "[AudioCaptureProxy] preWarm failed: \(error)",
   347	          level: .info, category: "XPC"
   348	        )
   349	      }
   350	      // Issue #289: propagate so the pipeline / AppState can abort the
   351	      // recording cleanly instead of flipping isPreWarmed=true against a
   352	      // dead capture path.
   353	      throw error
   354	    }
   355	    let enginePhaseMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - enginePhaseStart)
   356	    // Phase 2: wait for format stabilization
   357	    let stabStart = ContinuousClock.now
   358	    _ = await waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: 1.5, pollInterval: 0.2)
   359	    let stabMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - stabStart)
   360	    let totalMs = Self.ms(ContinuousClock.now - proxyStart)
   361	    Task {
   362	      await AppLogger.shared.log(
   363	        "COLD-START [XPC proxy] preWarm: total=\(totalMs)ms | conn=\(connMs)ms enginePhase=\(enginePhaseMs)ms formatStab=\(stabMs)ms",
   364	        level: .info, category: "XPC"
   365	      )
   366	    }
   367	  }
   368	
   369	  /// Convert Duration to milliseconds for logging.
   370	  private static func ms(_ d: Duration) -> Int {
   371	    let (seconds, attoseconds) = d.components
   372	    return Int(seconds) * 1000 + Int(attoseconds / 1_000_000_000_000_000)
   373	  }
   374	
   375	  public func abortPreWarm() {
   376	    serviceProxy { proxy in proxy.abortPreWarm() }
   377	  }
   378	
   379	  public func waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: TimeInterval, pollInterval: TimeInterval) async
   380	    -> Bool
   381	  {
   382	    await withCheckedContinuation { cont in
   383	      serviceProxy { proxy in
   384	        proxy.waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: maxWait, pollInterval: pollInterval) { result in
   385	          cont.resume(returning: result)
   386	        }
   387	      } onProxyError: {
   388	        cont.resume(returning: false)
   389	      }
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	
   393	  // MARK: - Config re-send after crash
   394	
   395	  /// Replays configuration to the service after a crash/relaunch.
   396	  /// Clears needsReinit after the XPC call is dispatched. Note: buildEngine is fire-and-forget
   397	  /// (no reply handler), so we cannot detect if the service actually processed the config.
   398	  /// If the service crashes during replay, the next interruptionHandler will re-set needsReinit.
   399	  /// This is acceptable because buildEngine is idempotent — replay on next attempt is safe.
   400	  private func resendConfigIfNeeded() {
   401	    guard needsReinit else { return }
   402	    serviceProxy { [self] proxy in
   403	      proxy.buildEngine(noiseSuppression: noiseSuppressionEnabled)
   404	      // Replay VAD config so service rebuilds its SilenceDetector after crash.
   405	      if let vad = vadConfig {
   406	        proxy.configureVAD(
   407	          autoStop: vad.autoStop, silenceTimeout: vad.silenceTimeout,
   408	          sensitivity: vad.sensitivity, energyGate: vad.energyGate)
   409	      }
   410	      // Replay warm engine policy so service uses correct idle timeout.
   411	      proxy.setWarmEnginePolicy(warmEnginePolicy.rawValue)
   412	      needsReinit = false
   413	    }
   414	  }
   415	
   416	  // MARK: - XPC connection management
   417	
   418	  private func ensureConnection() {
   419	    guard connection == nil else { return }
   420	
   421	    let conn = NSXPCConnection(serviceName: XPCServiceName.audioService)
   422	    conn.remoteObjectInterface = NSXPCInterface(with: AudioServiceProtocol.self)
   423	    conn.exportedInterface = NSXPCInterface(with: AudioServiceClientProtocol.self)
   424	    conn.exportedObject = self
   425	
   426	    // DESIGN RULE: Interruption while isCapturing == true is a user-visible capture failure.
   427	    // Interruption while idle is transient — just set needsReinit.
   428	    //
   429	    // IMPORTANT(Step 3+): Step 1.5 proved kill -9 fires interruptionHandler for embedded
   430	    // XPC services. During active capture, this IS the crash signal. The connection stays
   431	    // valid — the next XPC call auto-relaunches the service via launchd.
   432	    // CRITICAL: interruptionHandler and invalidationHandler run on XPC dispatch queues,
   433	    // NOT MainActor. Closures defined inside @MainActor methods inherit that isolation in
   434	    // Swift 6, causing dispatch_assert_queue_fail when XPC calls them. Extract to
   435	    // nonisolated static to break the isolation inheritance.
   436	    conn.interruptionHandler = Self.makeInterruptionHandler(proxy: self)
   437	    conn.invalidationHandler = Self.makeInvalidationHandler(proxy: self)
   438	
   439	    conn.resume()
   440	    connection = conn
   441	
   442	    // Verify service is alive — this ping triggers launchd to spawn the service.
   443	    serviceProxy { proxy in proxy.ping { _ in } }
   444	  }
   445	
   446	  /// Gets the remote proxy with error handling.
   447	  /// `onProxyError` is called if the proxy can't be obtained (connection nil or cast fails)
   448	  /// AND if the XPC framework delivers a per-call error (service crashed mid-call).
   449	  /// This is critical: when the service dies after a call is dispatched but before it replies,
   450	  /// the XPC error handler fires but the reply handler does NOT. Without routing the error
   451	  /// to `onProxyError`, any pending continuation hangs forever.
   452	  private func serviceProxy(
   453	    _ work: (any AudioServiceProtocol) -> Void,
   454	    onProxyError: (() -> Void)? = nil
   455	  ) {
   456	    guard let conn = connection else {
   457	      onProxyError?()
   458	      return
   459	    }
   460	    let proxy = conn.remoteObjectProxyWithErrorHandler(
   461	      Self.makeXPCErrorHandler(onProxyError: onProxyError))
   462	    guard let service = proxy as? AudioServiceProtocol else {
   463	      onProxyError?()
   464	      return
   465	    }
   466	    work(service)
   467	  }
   468	
   469	  /// Build the XPC error handler in a nonisolated context.
   470	  /// Critical: closures defined inside @MainActor methods inherit that isolation.
   471	  /// When XPC calls the error handler on its dispatch queue, Swift 6 asserts
   472	  /// dispatch_assert_queue(main) and traps with EXC_BREAKPOINT. By constructing
   473	  /// the handler in a nonisolated static method, the closure is free of @MainActor.
   474	  /// Build the XPC per-call error handler in a nonisolated context.
   475	  /// This handler fires when the service crashes after a call is dispatched but before it replies.
   476	  /// It MUST call onProxyError to resume any pending continuation — otherwise the caller hangs forever.
   477	  /// The error handler is the primary recovery signal; interruption/invalidation are secondary cleanup.
   478	  nonisolated private static func makeXPCErrorHandler(onProxyError: (() -> Void)? = nil)
   479	    -> @Sendable (any Error) -> Void
   480	  {
   481	    // Capture onProxyError as nonisolated(unsafe) — it may reference @MainActor closures
   482	    // but we dispatch it via Task { @MainActor } so the actual call is safe.
   483	    nonisolated(unsafe) let proxyError = onProxyError
   484	    return { error in
   485	      Task { @MainActor in
   486	        await AppLogger.shared.log(
   487	          "[AudioCaptureProxy] XPC error: \(error.localizedDescription)",
   488	          level: .info, category: "XPC"
   489	        )
   490	        proxyError?()
   491	      }
   492	    }
   493	  }
   494	
   495	  /// Build the XPC interruptionHandler in a nonisolated context.
   496	  /// Same isolation-escape pattern as makeXPCErrorHandler.
   497	  nonisolated private static func makeInterruptionHandler(proxy: AudioCaptureProxy) -> @Sendable ()
   498	    -> Void
   499	  {
   500	    return { [weak proxy] in
   501	
   502	      Task { @MainActor [weak proxy] in
   503	        guard let proxy else { return }
   504	        let wasCapturing = proxy.isCapturing
   505	        await AppLogger.shared.log(
   506	          "[AudioCaptureProxy] XPC interruptionHandler fired — wasCapturing=\(wasCapturing)",
   507	          level: .info, category: "XPC"
   508	        )
   509	        if wasCapturing {
   510	          // Cancel pending stall watchdog before the session ends.
   511	          proxy.stallWorkItem?.cancel()
   512	          proxy.stallWorkItem = nil
   513	          let endingSession = proxy.activeCaptureGeneration
   514	          proxy.isCapturing = false
   515	          proxy.audioLevel = 0
   516	          proxy.captureGeneration &+= 1
   517	          proxy.bufferContinuation?.finish()
   518	          proxy.bufferContinuation = nil
   519	          proxy.onEngineInterrupted?()
   520	          // Fire telemetry callback — idle interruptions stay silent (§3.4 row 2).

   521	          proxy.onXPCServiceError?(
   522	            XPCErrorContext(
   523	              kind: .interruptCapturing,
   524	              sessionID: endingSession,
   525	              timestampNs: DispatchTime.now().uptimeNanoseconds
   526	            )
   527	          )
   528	        }
   529	        proxy.needsReinit = true
   530	      }
   531	    }
   532	  }
   533	
   534	  /// Build the XPC invalidationHandler in a nonisolated context.
   535	  nonisolated private static func makeInvalidationHandler(proxy: AudioCaptureProxy) -> @Sendable ()
   536	    -> Void
   537	  {
   538	    return { [weak proxy] in
   539	
   540	      Task { @MainActor [weak proxy] in
   541	        guard let proxy else { return }
   542	
   543	        proxy.connection = nil
   544	        let wasCapturing = proxy.isCapturing
   545	        let endingSession = proxy.activeCaptureGeneration
   546	        if wasCapturing {
   547	          proxy.stallWorkItem?.cancel()
   548	          proxy.stallWorkItem = nil
   549	          proxy.isCapturing = false
   550	          proxy.audioLevel = 0
   551	          proxy.captureGeneration &+= 1
   552	          proxy.bufferContinuation?.finish()
   553	          proxy.bufferContinuation = nil
   554	          proxy.onEngineInterrupted?()
   555	        }
   556	        proxy.needsReinit = true
   557	        // Invalidation is always a telemetry signal — connection is gone.
   558	        proxy.onXPCServiceError?(
   559	          XPCErrorContext(
   560	            kind: wasCapturing ? .invalidateCapturing : .invalidateIdle,
   561	            sessionID: wasCapturing ? endingSession : nil,
   562	            timestampNs: DispatchTime.now().uptimeNanoseconds
   563	          )
   564	        )
   565	      }
   566	    }
   567	  }

   569	  // MARK: - V2 fault-injection (DEBUG only, issue #291)
   570	
   571	  #if DEBUG
   572	    /// Invalidates the active XPC connection synchronously. Fires the existing
   573	    /// `invalidationHandler` path, which sets `connection = nil`, flips
   574	    /// `isCapturing = false`, finishes the buffer continuation, and emits the
   575	    /// `onXPCServiceError(.invalidateCapturing)` telemetry callback.
   576	    ///
   577	    /// Drives Lane A scenario A4 ("audio XPC service kill") via the DEBUG
   578	    /// localhost endpoint. Equivalent in effect to a real audio service crash
   579	    /// mid-stream — but deterministic and synchronous.
   580	    ///
   581	    /// `package` access: callable from `DebugFaultEndpoint` in the app target.
   582	    /// Inert in release builds.
   583	    package func forceConnectionTerminationNow() {
   584	      connection?.invalidate()
   585	    }
   586	  #endif
   587	
   588	  // MARK: - VAD Interface (Step 5)
   589	
   590	  /// Stored VAD config — forwarded to service, replayed after crash via resendConfigIfNeeded().
   591	  private var vadConfig:
   592	    (autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool)?
   593	
   594	  public func configureVAD(
   595	    autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool
   596	  ) {
   597	    vadConfig = (autoStop, silenceTimeout, sensitivity, energyGate)
   598	    serviceProxy { proxy in
   599	      proxy.configureVAD(
   600	        autoStop: autoStop, silenceTimeout: silenceTimeout, sensitivity: sensitivity,
   601	        energyGate: energyGate)
   602	    }
   603	  }
   604	
   605	  public func getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: Int) async -> (samples: [Float], totalCount: Int) {
   606	    // Use OneShotContinuation to guarantee exactly one resume — XPC error handler and
   607	    // reply handler can race, and double-resume is undefined behavior.
   608	    let sessionID = activeCaptureGeneration
   609	    do {
   610	      return try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation {
   611	        (cont: CheckedContinuation<(samples: [Float], totalCount: Int), any Error>) in
   612	        let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   613	        serviceProxy { proxy in
   614	          proxy.getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: fromIndex) { data, totalCount in
   615	            let floats = Self.dataToFloats(data)
   616	            guard_.resume(returning: (samples: floats, totalCount: totalCount))
   617	          }
   618	        } onProxyError: { [weak self] in
   619	          self?.reportXPCReplyFailure(stage: "get_samples", sessionID: sessionID)
   620	          guard_.resume(returning: (samples: [], totalCount: 0))
   621	        }
   622	      }
   623	    } catch {
   624	      return (samples: [], totalCount: 0)
   625	    }
   626	  }
   627	
   628	  public func getVADSegments() async -> [SpeechSegment] {
   629	    // Use OneShotContinuation to guarantee exactly one resume.
   630	    let sessionID = activeCaptureGeneration
   631	    do {
   632	      return try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation {
   633	        (cont: CheckedContinuation<[SpeechSegment], any Error>) in
   634	        let guard_ = OneShotContinuation(cont)
   635	        serviceProxy { proxy in
   636	          proxy.getVADSegments { data in
   637	            guard_.resume(returning: Self.decodeVADSegments(data))
   638	          }
   639	        } onProxyError: { [weak self] in
   640	          self?.reportXPCReplyFailure(stage: "get_speech_segments", sessionID: sessionID)
   641	          guard_.resume(returning: [])
   642	        }
   643	      }
   644	    } catch {
   645	      return []
   646	    }
   647	  }
   648	
   649	  /// Invoke `onXPCReplyFailed` before a reply-path swallowed empty default
   650	  /// surfaces to callers, so the pipeline can emit the correct root cause
   651	  /// instead of mis-classifying as `no_audio_captured`.
   652	  private func reportXPCReplyFailure(stage: String, sessionID: UInt64) {
   653	    onXPCReplyFailed?(
   654	      XPCReplyFailureContext(
   655	        replyStage: stage,
   656	        errorDomain: "com.enviouswispr.xpc",
   657	        errorCode: -1,
   658	        errorDescription: "XPC reply failed — service unreachable",
   659	        sessionID: sessionID
   660	      )
   661	    )
   662	  }
   663	
   664	  // MARK: - Data conversion
   665	
   666	  /// Convert raw Data to [Float]. Transport format: Float32 PCM, non-interleaved mono, 16kHz.
   667	  /// Data is raw bytes — no header, no metadata.
   668	  /// nonisolated: called from XPC reply callbacks which run on XPC dispatch queues, not MainActor.
   669	  nonisolated private static func dataToFloats(_ data: Data) -> [Float] {
   670	    guard !data.isEmpty, data.count.isMultiple(of: MemoryLayout<Float>.size) else { return [] }
   671	    return data.withUnsafeBytes { Array($0.bindMemory(to: Float.self)) }
   672	  }
   673	
   674	  /// Decode packed [Int32 start, Int32 end] pairs into SpeechSegment array.
   675	  nonisolated private static func decodeVADSegments(_ data: Data) -> [SpeechSegment] {
   676	    let pairSize = MemoryLayout<Int32>.size * 2
   677	    guard !data.isEmpty, data.count.isMultiple(of: pairSize) else { return [] }
   678	    return data.withUnsafeBytes { raw in
   679	      let int32s = raw.bindMemory(to: Int32.self)
   680	      var segments: [SpeechSegment] = []
   681	      segments.reserveCapacity(int32s.count / 2)
   682	      for i in stride(from: 0, to: int32s.count, by: 2) {
   683	        segments.append(
   684	          SpeechSegment(
   685	            startSample: Int(int32s[i]),
   686	            endSample: Int(int32s[i + 1])
   687	          ))
   688	      }
   689	      return segments
   690	    }
   691	  }
   692	}
   693	
   694	// MARK: - AudioServiceClientProtocol (service → host callbacks)
   695	
   696	/// These callbacks arrive on an XPC dispatch queue (not RT, not main).
   697	/// Each hops to @MainActor via Task before updating observable state.
   698	extension AudioCaptureProxy: AudioServiceClientProtocol {
   699	
   700	  /// Received audio buffer from service — reconstruct AVAudioPCMBuffer and deliver.
   701	  nonisolated public func audioBufferCaptured(_ data: Data, frameCount: Int, audioLevel: Float) {
   702	    // Validation guards before memcpy.
   703	    guard frameCount > 0, frameCount <= 65536 else { return }
   704	    guard data.count == frameCount * MemoryLayout<Float>.size else { return }
   705	    guard
   706	      let buffer = AVAudioPCMBuffer(
   707	        pcmFormat: Self.targetFormat,
   708	        frameCapacity: AVAudioFrameCount(frameCount)
   709	      )
   710	    else { return }
   711	
   712	    buffer.frameLength = AVAudioFrameCount(frameCount)
   713	    data.withUnsafeBytes { raw in
   714	      guard let src = raw.baseAddress, let dst = buffer.floatChannelData?[0] else { return }
   715	      memcpy(dst, src, data.count)
   716	    }
   717	
   718	    nonisolated(unsafe) let safeBuffer = buffer
   719	    // Snapshot frameCount for generation check inside the MainActor Task.
   720	    // captureGeneration is read inside the Task (MainActor-isolated), not here.
   721	
   722	    Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   723	      guard let self else { return }
   724	      // Reject stale callbacks from previous capture sessions.
   725	      guard self.isCapturing,
   726	        self.captureGeneration == self.activeCaptureGeneration
   727	      else { return }
   728	      #if DEBUG
   729	        // V2 fault-injection (issue #291): drop buffer if forced-stall is active.
   730	        // Intentionally does NOT flip `hasReceivedBufferThisSession` so the
   731	        // existing stall watchdog still fires after `audioCaptureStallWindowMs`.
   732	        if self.forceStallRemainingBuffers > 0 {
   733	          self.forceStallRemainingBuffers -= 1
   734	          return
   735	        }
   736	      #endif
   737	      self.hasReceivedBufferThisSession = true
   738	      self.audioLevel = audioLevel
   739	      self.bufferContinuation?.yield(safeBuffer)
   740	      self.onBufferCaptured?(safeBuffer)
   741	    }
   742	  }
   743	
   744	  /// Service's audio engine was interrupted (device disconnect, emergency teardown).
   745	  /// Matches interruptionHandler contract: only fires onEngineInterrupted during active capture.
   746	  /// Idle interruptions are transient — just set needsReinit.
   747	  nonisolated public func engineInterrupted() {
   748	    Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   749	      guard let self else { return }
   750	      if self.isCapturing {
   751	        self.stallWorkItem?.cancel()
   752	        self.stallWorkItem = nil
   753	        self.isCapturing = false
   754	        self.audioLevel = 0
   755	        self.captureGeneration &+= 1
   756	        self.bufferContinuation?.finish()
   757	        self.bufferContinuation = nil
   758	        self.onEngineInterrupted?()
   759	      }
   760	      self.needsReinit = true
   761	    }
   762	  }
   763	
   764	  /// Service-side VAD detected sustained silence after speech — auto-stop should trigger.
   765	  /// Stale-fire protection: generation check + pipeline state guard (in AppState handler).
   766	  nonisolated public func vadAutoStopTriggered() {
   767	    Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   768	      guard let self else { return }
   769	      guard self.isCapturing,
   770	        self.captureGeneration == self.activeCaptureGeneration
   771	      else { return }
   772	      self.onVADAutoStop?()
   773	    }
   774	  }
   775	}
   776	
   777	// MARK: - Helpers
   778	
   779	/// Thread-safe one-shot continuation guard. Ensures exactly one resume,
   780	/// preventing crashes from double-resume when XPC reply and error handler race.
   781	private final class OneShotContinuation<T: Sendable>: @unchecked Sendable {
   782	  private var continuation: CheckedContinuation<T, any Error>?
   783	  private let lock = NSLock()
   784	
   785	  init(_ continuation: CheckedContinuation<T, any Error>) {
   786	    self.continuation = continuation
   787	  }
   788	
   789	  func resume(returning value: T) {
   790	    lock.lock()
   791	    let cont = continuation
   792	    continuation = nil
   793	    lock.unlock()
   794	    cont?.resume(returning: value)
   795	  }
   796	
   797	  func resume(throwing error: any Error) {
   798	    lock.lock()
   799	    let cont = continuation
   800	    continuation = nil
   801	    lock.unlock()
   802	    cont?.resume(throwing: error)
   803	  }
   804	}
   805	
   806	/// Convenience overload for Void continuations.
   807	extension OneShotContinuation where T == Void {
   808	  func resume() {
   809	    resume(returning: ())
   810	  }
   811	}
   812	
   813	/// XPC transport errors surfaced by the proxy.
   814	enum XPCTransportError: LocalizedError {
   815	  case serviceUnreachable
   816	
   817	  var errorDescription: String? {
   818	    switch self {
   819	    case .serviceUnreachable: return "XPC audio service is unreachable."
   820	    }
   821	  }
   822	}

     1	#if DEBUG
     2	
     3	  import EnviousWisprASR
     4	  import EnviousWisprAudio
     5	  import EnviousWisprCore
     6	  import EnviousWisprPipeline
     7	  import Foundation
     8	  import Network
     9	
    10	  /// V2 fault-injection control surface (issue #291).
    11	  ///
    12	  /// A tiny localhost command listener that exists only in DEBUG builds and
    13	  /// only starts when the launching environment sets `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1`.
    14	  /// Production builds compile this entire file out — verified post-build via
    15	  /// `nm` symbol grep against the release binary.
    16	  ///
    17	  /// **Security posture:**
    18	  /// - `#if DEBUG` (entire file) — release binaries lack the symbols entirely.
    19	  /// - Env-gated: must launch with `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1` to start at all.
    20	  /// - Loopback only: binds 127.0.0.1, refuses non-loopback peers.
    21	  /// - Per-launch token: a 32-byte hex token is written atomically to
    22	  ///   `~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/fault-token-<pid>` with `0600` perms.
    23	  ///   Every command must carry the matching token in its first line.
    24	  ///   Token file is deleted on `stop()`.
    25	  /// - Fixed command set (no arbitrary RPC, no method invocation, no shell escape):
    26	  ///   `force_stall(N)`, `force_cancel`, `force_xpc_kill`,
    27	  ///   `force_audio_xpc_kill`, `query_state`.
    28	  /// - Each command dispatches to `@MainActor` via `Task { @MainActor in ... }`
    29	  ///   so command handling matches the actor isolation of the seams it drives.
    30	  ///
    31	  /// **Wire protocol** (text, line-delimited, single command per connection):
    32	  /// ```
    33	  /// <token>\n
    34	  /// <command>\n
    35	  /// ```
    36	  /// Reply is one line: `OK\n`, `OK <state>\n` (for `query_state`), or
    37	  /// `ERR <reason>\n`. Connection is closed by the listener after the reply.
    38	  ///
    39	  /// Drives Lane A scenarios in `Tests/RuntimeUAT/faultInjection.py`.
    40	  @MainActor
    41	  final class DebugFaultEndpoint {
    42	
    43	    // MARK: - Dependencies (concrete proxy/pipeline types — DEBUG seams live here)
    44	
    45	    private let audioProxy: AudioCaptureProxy?
    46	    private let asrProxy: ASRManagerProxy?
    47	    private let parakeetPipeline: TranscriptionPipeline
    48	    private let whisperKitPipeline: WhisperKitPipeline
    49	    private let activeBackend: () -> ASRBackendType
    50	
    51	    // MARK: - Listener state
    52	
    53	    private var listener: NWListener?
    54	    private let port: UInt16
    55	    private let token: String
    56	    private let tokenPath: URL
    57	    private let queue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.enviouswispr.debug.fault-endpoint")
    58	
    59	    /// Construct the endpoint. The audio/ASR proxy refs are optional so the
    60	    /// endpoint still functions in environments where the heart-path is
    61	    /// stubbed out (preview/test app builds); commands targeting absent
    62	    /// dependencies reply `ERR no_dependency`.
    63	    init(
    64	      audioProxy: AudioCaptureProxy?,
    65	      asrProxy: ASRManagerProxy?,
    66	      parakeetPipeline: TranscriptionPipeline,
    67	      whisperKitPipeline: WhisperKitPipeline,
    68	      activeBackend: @escaping () -> ASRBackendType,
    69	      port: UInt16 = 8765
    70	    ) {
    71	      self.audioProxy = audioProxy
    72	      self.asrProxy = asrProxy
    73	      self.parakeetPipeline = parakeetPipeline
    74	      self.whisperKitPipeline = whisperKitPipeline
    75	      self.activeBackend = activeBackend
    76	      self.port = port
    77	      self.token = Self.generateToken()
    78	      self.tokenPath = Self.tokenURL(pid: ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier)
    79	    }
    80	
    81	    /// Returns true when the launching environment has opted in.
    82	    static var isRequested: Bool {
    83	      ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["EW_FAULT_INJECTION"] == "1"
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    /// Bind the loopback listener and write the per-launch token file.
    87	    /// Idempotent — calling twice is a no-op after the first successful start.
    88	    func start() {
    89	      guard listener == nil else { return }
    90	
    91	      do {
    92	        try writeTokenFile()
    93	      } catch {
    94	        Task {
    95	          await AppLogger.shared.log(
    96	            "[DebugFaultEndpoint] failed to write token file: \(error)",
    97	            level: .info, category: "Debug"
    98	          )
    99	        }
   100	        return
   101	      }
   102	
   103	      let params = NWParameters.tcp
   104	      params.requiredInterfaceType = .loopback
   105	      params.acceptLocalOnly = true
   106	
   107	      do {
   108	        let listener = try NWListener(using: params, on: NWEndpoint.Port(rawValue: port)!)
   109	        listener.newConnectionHandler = { [weak self] conn in
   110	          // newConnectionHandler runs on `queue` (nonisolated). Hop to MainActor
   111	          // before touching this @MainActor type.
   112	          Task { @MainActor in
   113	            self?.accept(connection: conn)
   114	          }
   115	        }
   116	        listener.start(queue: queue)
   117	        self.listener = listener
   118	        Task {
   119	          await AppLogger.shared.log(
   120	            "[DebugFaultEndpoint] started on 127.0.0.1:\(port) (token at \(tokenPath.path))",
   121	            level: .info, category: "Debug"
   122	          )
   123	        }
   124	      } catch {
   125	        Task {
   126	          await AppLogger.shared.log(
   127	            "[DebugFaultEndpoint] failed to bind: \(error)",
   128	            level: .info, category: "Debug"
   129	          )
   130	        }
   131	        try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: tokenPath)
   132	      }
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    /// Tear down the listener and remove the token file. Safe to call multiple
   136	    /// times — wired into `applicationWillTerminate`.
   137	    func stop() {
   138	      listener?.cancel()
   139	      listener = nil
   140	      try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: tokenPath)
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    // MARK: - Connection handling
   144	
   145	    /// Maximum bytes for a single fault-injection request. Tokens are 64
   146	    /// hex chars + "\n" + a short command + "\n" — well under 512.
   147	    private static let maxRequestBytes = 512
   148	
   149	    private func accept(connection conn: NWConnection) {
   150	      conn.start(queue: queue)
   151	      readRequest(on: conn, accumulated: Data())
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    /// Drain the connection until we see the second newline (two-line
   155	    /// request) or the buffer cap. TCP can split a small request across
   156	    /// segments; the prior single `recv` parsed partial data as
   157	    /// malformed/auth-failed and closed the connection, causing
   158	    /// intermittent harness failures (Codex P2 feedback on PR #544).
   159	    private func readRequest(on conn: NWConnection, accumulated: Data) {
   160	      conn.receive(
   161	        minimumIncompleteLength: 1, maximumLength: Self.maxRequestBytes
   162	      ) { [weak self] data, _, isComplete, error in
   163	        guard let self else {
   164	          conn.cancel()
   165	          return
   166	        }
   167	        guard error == nil else {
   168	          conn.cancel()
   169	          return
   170	        }
   171	        var buffer = accumulated
   172	        if let data, !data.isEmpty {
   173	          buffer.append(data)
   174	        }
   175	
   176	        let newlineCount = buffer.reduce(0) { $0 + ($1 == 0x0A ? 1 : 0) }
   177	        let isFull = newlineCount >= 2 || buffer.count >= Self.maxRequestBytes
   178	
   179	        if isFull || isComplete {
   180	          guard !buffer.isEmpty else {
   181	            conn.cancel()
   182	            return
   183	          }
   184	          let request = String(decoding: buffer, as: UTF8.self)
   185	          Task { @MainActor in
   186	            let reply = await self.handle(request: request)
   187	            let payload = (reply + "\n").data(using: .utf8) ?? Data()
   188	            conn.send(
   189	              content: payload,
   190	              completion: .contentProcessed { _ in conn.cancel() }
   191	            )
   192	          }
   193	        } else {
   194	          // Need more data; keep draining.
   195	          self.readRequest(on: conn, accumulated: buffer)
   196	        }
   197	      }
   198	    }
   199	
   200	    /// Parse a two-line request and dispatch to the matching seam. Returns the
   201	    /// reply line (without trailing newline).
   202	    private func handle(request: String) async -> String {
   203	      let lines = request.split(separator: "\n", maxSplits: 1, omittingEmptySubsequences: false)
   204	      guard lines.count == 2 else { return "ERR malformed" }
   205	      guard String(lines[0]) == token else { return "ERR auth" }
   206	
   207	      let cmd = lines[1].trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
   208	      switch cmd {
   209	      case "force_cancel":
   210	        switch activeBackend() {
   211	        case .parakeet: await parakeetPipeline.forceCancelNow()
   212	        case .whisperKit: await whisperKitPipeline.forceCancelNow()
   213	        }
   214	        return "OK"
   215	
   216	      case "force_xpc_kill":
   217	        guard let asrProxy else { return "ERR no_dependency" }
   218	        asrProxy.forceConnectionTerminationNow()
   219	        return "OK"
   220	
   221	      case "force_audio_xpc_kill":
   222	        guard let audioProxy else { return "ERR no_dependency" }
   223	        audioProxy.forceConnectionTerminationNow()
   224	        return "OK"
   225	
   226	      case "query_state":
   227	        let p = parakeetPipeline.state
   228	        let w = whisperKitPipeline.state
   229	        return "OK parakeet=\(p) whisperkit=\(w) backend=\(activeBackend())"
   230	
   231	      default:
   232	        // force_stall(N) is the only parameterized command.
   233	        if let n = parseForceStall(cmd) {
   234	          guard let audioProxy else { return "ERR no_dependency" }
   235	          audioProxy.forceStallRemainingBuffers = n
   236	          return "OK"
   237	        }
   238	        return "ERR unknown_command"
   239	      }
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    private func parseForceStall(_ cmd: String) -> Int? {
   243	      let prefix = "force_stall("
   244	      guard cmd.hasPrefix(prefix), cmd.hasSuffix(")") else { return nil }
   245	      let inner = cmd.dropFirst(prefix.count).dropLast()
   246	      return Int(inner)
   247	    }
   248	
   249	    // MARK: - Token file management
   250	
   251	    private func writeTokenFile() throws {
   252	      let dir = tokenPath.deletingLastPathComponent()
   253	      try FileManager.default.createDirectory(
   254	        at: dir, withIntermediateDirectories: true, attributes: nil)
   255	      // Atomic write so partial token can never be observed by harness reader.
   256	      try token.data(using: .utf8)?.write(to: tokenPath, options: [.atomic])
   257	      // Tighten permissions to 0600 — owner read/write only.
   258	      try FileManager.default.setAttributes(
   259	        [.posixPermissions: NSNumber(value: Int16(0o600))], ofItemAtPath: tokenPath.path)
   260	    }
   261	
   262	    private static func tokenURL(pid: Int32) -> URL {
   263	      let logs = FileManager.default.urls(for: .libraryDirectory, in: .userDomainMask)[0]
   264	        .appendingPathComponent("Logs", isDirectory: true)
   265	        .appendingPathComponent("EnviousWispr", isDirectory: true)
   266	      return logs.appendingPathComponent("fault-token-\(pid)")
   267	    }
   268	
   269	    private static func generateToken() -> String {
   270	      var bytes = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: 32)
   271	      let result = SecRandomCopyBytes(kSecRandomDefault, bytes.count, &bytes)
   272	      precondition(result == errSecSuccess, "SecRandomCopyBytes failed in DebugFaultEndpoint")
   273	      return bytes.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
   274	    }
   275	  }
   276	
   277	#endif

/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba .claude/knowledge/v2-uat-bypass-2026-05-01.md' in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	<!-- tier: 3 -->
     2	
     3	# V2 UAT bypass — six-point cluster failure (2026-05-01)
     4	
     5	## When to read
     6	
     7	Before any plan that:
     8	- Declares `Live UAT: Y` AND introduces env-gated, DEBUG-gated, or otherwise-conditional runtime surfaces.
     9	- Adds a new gate, registry, hotkey, command channel, or runtime toggle.
    10	- Touches the heart path with infrastructure that requires a non-default build mode or non-default launch.
    11	
    12	Tracking issues: P0 #548, P1 #549, follow-up #547.
    13	
    14	## What happened
    15	
    16	PR #544 (V2 fault-injection toolkit, issue #291) merged via `gh pr merge --squash` with the runtime toolkit (Lane A scenarios + DebugFaultEndpoint) **never exercised end-to-end on a real running app even once.** When the founder asked to test it post-merge, the canonical run-the-thing path could not run the thing.
    17	
    18	Two infrastructure gaps blocked the test:
    19	- `bundle-dev.sh` hardcodes `swift build -c release` → `#if DEBUG` seams compile out → release bundle has no V2 surface.
    20	- `open(1)` does not propagate `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1` to the launched app → endpoint cannot start even on a debug bundle without manual hacks.
    21	
    22	Six bypass points all had to fire in one session for this to ship:
    23	
    24	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-1
    25	Rule: Plan-template must require a "Canonical bundle/launch path" sub-field under §11.1 Live UAT spec.
    26	Why: §11.1 names a Driver but does not require identifying which canonical script produces a build-and-launch artifact capable of running the Driver. For env-gated or DEBUG-gated runtime surfaces, that mapping is non-trivial and is the first thing that goes missing.
    27	Ref: `docs/feature-requests/TEMPLATE.md` §11.1; tracked in #548.
    28	
    29	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-2
    30	Rule: Council preamble must include a bounded "runtime path exists?" line.
    31	Why: Council reviews the plan for internal consistency. It does not currently audit whether canonical run-the-thing scripts can produce a build/launch artifact that exercises the plan's runtime surfaces. One bounded sentence in the preamble would have flagged V2 before round 1.
    32	Ref: `.claude/rules/workflow-process.md §2 council preamble`; tracked in #548.
    33	
    34	Suggested line:
    35	> "If the plan introduces new runtime surfaces (DEBUG seams, env-gated subsystems, hidden command channels, runtime toggles), confirm the canonical run-the-thing scripts can produce a build/launch artifact that exercises them. If not, that infrastructure is the first piece of work, not a follow-up."
    36	
    37	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-3
    38	Rule: Codex grounded-review template must add Q3 — does the canonical run-the-thing path support running this plan's surfaces?
    39	Why: Q1 (direction) + Q2 (council fact-check) verify the plan internally and against existing code. Neither asks whether the canonical bundling/launch flow can exercise what the plan adds. V2 grounded review verified file paths and access modifiers; it did not grep `scripts/bundle-dev.sh` against the plan's runtime requirements.
    40	Ref: `.codex/grounded-review-template.md`; tracked in #548.
    41	
    42	Q3 should activate for plans that declare `Live UAT: Y` AND introduce env-gated or DEBUG-gated runtime surfaces.
    43	
    44	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-4
    45	Rule: Phase 3 validation hook must reject third-option skip-notes; require populated `.validation/runs/<latest>/` with required artifacts before any push touching `Sources/` or `Tests/EnviousWisprTests/`.
    46	Why: `.claude/rules/workflow-process.md §1 step 9` says "Skip a step → write `skip-note.txt` BEFORE pushing OR escalate to Stage 2; **no third option**." V2 shipped with `.validation/v2-redgreen-skip-note.txt` containing forward-looking phrases ("post-merge", "founder will run", "deferred", "follow-up cycle") — exactly the third-option anti-pattern. The push hook accepted it because it does not currently parse skip-notes for forward-looking deferrals.
    47	Ref: `.claude/scripts/check-push-discipline.sh`; tracked in #548.
    48	
    49	Two complementary fixes:
    50	- Reject any skip-note containing forward-looking phrases on Code-lane PRs.
    51	- Require the skip-note to name a legitimate reason from an enumerated list (docs-only, eval-harness PR with evidence, etc.).
    52	
    53	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-5
    54	Rule: `scripts/validate-pr.sh` must be runnable AND mechanically required before push for `Sources/`-touching PRs.
    55	Why: V2 shipped without ever invoking `scripts/validate-pr.sh`. Went straight from `git commit` → `gh pr create` → `gh pr merge`. The hook would have noticed the missing artifacts (`scenario-redgreen-*.json` not present in run dir) and refused.
    56	Ref: `scripts/validate-pr.sh`, `scripts/check-validation.sh`; tracked in #548.
    57	
    58	## RULE: v2-uat-bypass-6
    59	Rule: Plan must enumerate existing equivalent infrastructure before designing a parallel gating mechanism.
    60	Why: V2 §3.5 introduced a new gate (`#if DEBUG` + `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1` localhost endpoint) without enumerating existing runtime gates of similar shape. The codebase already had `state.settings.isDebugModeEnabled` — a runtime, persistent, hidden-Cmd+Shift+D-discoverable debug surface that ships in release builds and is plumbed through `Sources/EnviousWispr/Views/Settings/DiagnosticsSettingsView.swift` + `SettingsSection.diagnostics`. V2 should have re-used the existing toggle (or nested `isFaultInjectionEndpointEnabled` under it). Grounded review verified the proposed gate compiled correctly; it did not ask whether the gate was the right one.
    61	Ref: `Sources/EnviousWispr/Views/Settings/DiagnosticsSettingsView.swift`; tracked in #549 (re-gate) and #548 (template-level fix).
    62	
    63	The bar at plan time: "why isn't the existing thing the right thing?" rather than "is the new thing well-designed?"
    64	
    65	## FACT: auto-mode-amplifier
    66	The session ran in auto-mode + the founder said "I'm not going to manually review anything." That signal got interpreted as license to relax workflow gates, when it only relaxes question-asking on routine decisions. Auto-mode policy clarification needed: **auto-mode does NOT relax workflow gates, only relaxes question-asking on routine decisions.** The current auto-mode prompt is silent on this distinction.
    67	
    68	## How to apply
    69	
    70	Before merging any PR with `Live UAT: Y` declared:
    71	1. Identify the canonical script that produces the runnable artifact (`bundle-dev.sh` for app code today).
    72	2. Confirm that script can produce a build that exercises the new surfaces. If not, file the infrastructure issue first; that's the prerequisite.
    73	3. Run `scripts/validate-pr.sh`. Read the populated run dir. Verify required artifacts.
    74	4. If a skip-note is needed: write it BEFORE pushing, name an enumerated legitimate reason, NEVER use forward-looking phrases.
    75	5. If you wrote a forward-looking skip-note, treat it as a STOP signal, not a deferral.
    76	
    77	## Why preserve this in knowledge
    78	
    79	The cluster pattern is not the V2 mistake. It's a class of bypass that any LARGE-tier plan introducing new gating mechanisms can replicate. Catching one bypass at any of the six points stops the cluster; building the muscle to catch them at plan time is what compounds. Future sessions reading this entry should treat it as a checklist for plans of similar shape, not a one-off retrospective.

     5	/// Abstraction over audio capture — enables swapping between in-process and XPC implementations.
     6	@MainActor
     7	public protocol AudioCaptureInterface: AnyObject {
     8	  // Observable state (read-only externally)
     9	  var isCapturing: Bool { get }
    10	  var audioLevel: Float { get }
    11	  var capturedSamples: [Float] { get }
    12	  /// Low-cardinality audio route label for Sentry. Set after route resolution.
    13	  var currentAudioRoute: String { get }
    14	
    15	  // Callback properties (read-write)
    16	  var onBufferCaptured: (@Sendable (AVAudioPCMBuffer) -> Void)? { get set }
    17	  var onEngineInterrupted: (() -> Void)? { get set }
    18	  var onVADAutoStop: (() -> Void)? { get set }
    19	
    20	  // Telemetry callbacks (round-4 additions for #285 heart-path Sentry coverage).
    21	  // Producers: source backends (`AVAudioEngineSource`, `AVCaptureSessionSource`,
    22	  //            `AudioCaptureProxy`). Consumers: pipeline layer + `AppState`.
    23	  // All callbacks fire on the MainActor. Conformers that don't produce a given
    24	  // signal leave the closure nil (e.g. direct sources leave XPC callbacks nil).
    25	
    26	  /// Fires once per capture session when the liveness watchdog observes zero
    27	  /// audio buffers within `Constants.audioCaptureStallWindowMs` of tap install.
    28	  /// At most one call per `currentCaptureSessionID`. Never called after a
    29	  /// subsequent `stopCapture` for that session. Telemetry-only: consumers
    30	  /// must not treat this as a control-flow signal.
    31	  var onCaptureStalled: ((CaptureStallContext) -> Void)? { get set }
    32	
    33	  /// Fires on `AVCaptureSessionWasInterrupted` / `AVCaptureSessionRuntimeError`
    34	  /// notifications from `AVCaptureSessionSource`. Carries the diagnostic
    35	  /// payload (interruption reason, runtime NSError) that the notification
    36	  /// userInfo would otherwise drop. Non-BT sources leave nil.
    37	  var onCaptureSessionInterruption: ((CaptureSessionInterruptionContext) -> Void)? { get set }
    38	
    39	  /// Fires from the proxy's XPC interruption / invalidation handlers.
    40	  /// Direct sources have no XPC layer and leave nil. Consumer emits
    41	  /// `captureError(.xpcServiceError)`. Idle interrupts do NOT invoke (silent
    42	  /// end-to-end per §3.5 Channel 2 in the round-4 plan).
    43	  var onXPCServiceError: ((XPCErrorContext) -> Void)? { get set }
    44	
    45	  /// Fires when a non-throwing XPC call (`stopCapture`, `getSamplesSnapshot`,
    46	  /// `getSpeechSegments`) swallowed a transport error in its internal catch
    47	  /// block. The proxy invokes the callback BEFORE returning its empty default
    48	  /// so the pipeline can emit the correct root cause instead of misrouting
    49	  /// into "no_audio_captured".
    50	  var onXPCReplyFailed: ((XPCReplyFailureContext) -> Void)? { get set }
    51	
    52	  /// Fires on the first route resolution and on every subsequent resolution
    53	  /// where sourceType or reason differs from the prior call. No-op on
    54	  /// warm-reuse resolutions that produce the same decision.
    55	  var onRouteResolved: ((CaptureRouteDecision, _ sourceTypeChanged: Bool) -> Void)? { get set }
    56	
    57	  /// Monotonic per-source identifier for the active capture session.
    58	  /// Increments on every `startCapture` / `beginCapturePhase`. Zero if no
    59	  /// session has started yet. Pipeline uses for correlation extras +
    60	  /// dedup-claim keying. Never persisted; not meaningful across launches.
    61	  var currentCaptureSessionID: UInt64 { get }
    62	
    63	  /// Authoritative "is a capture session in-flight right now." Backed by
    64	  /// source/proxy internal state (not pipeline UI state which lags at
    65	  /// start/stop boundaries). Used as the telemetry gate for XPC interrupt
    66	  /// classification.
    67	  var isActivelyCapturing: Bool { get }

    72	  /// (AVCaptureSession) are not mislabeled as AVAudioEngine. Delegates to the

    79	  var preferredInputDeviceIDOverride: String { get set }

     1	import Foundation
     2	
     3	/// XPC protocol: commands from host app to audio service.
     4	///
     5	/// All parameters are @objc-compatible types only: Data, NSError, Bool, Float, Double, String, Int.
     6	/// Audio samples cross as Data (raw Float32 bytes). The proxy converts Data↔[Float] at the boundary.
     7	///
     8	/// Methods are split into two categories:
     9	/// - **Configuration** (buildEngine, set*): state setters, replayed by proxy after crash reinit.
    10	/// - **Lifecycle** (start*, beginCapture, stopCapture, etc.): control flow, require config first.
    11	@objc public protocol AudioServiceProtocol {
    12	  // MARK: - Diagnostics (Steps 1-2)
    13	
    14	  /// Connection health check.
    15	  func ping(reply: @escaping (String) -> Void)
    16	
    17	  /// Report current microphone authorization status as seen by the XPC service process.
    18	  func checkMicPermission(reply: @escaping (Int, String) -> Void)
    19	
    20	  // MARK: - Configuration (Step 3)
    21	
    22	  /// Build/rebuild the audio engine with noise suppression configuration.
    23	  /// Replayed by the proxy after service crash via resendConfigIfNeeded().
    24	  func buildEngine(noiseSuppression: Bool)
    25	
    26	  /// Update noise suppression setting on the existing engine.
    27	  func setNoiseSuppressionEnabled(_ enabled: Bool)
    28	
    29	  /// Set the preferred input device UID (user's explicit choice). Empty = auto.
    30	  func setPreferredInputDeviceUID(_ uid: String)
    31	
    32	  /// Set the selected input device UID (legacy path). Empty = system default.
    33	  func setSelectedInputDeviceUID(_ uid: String)
    34	
    35	  /// Set the warm engine policy. Raw value of WarmEnginePolicy enum.
    36	  /// Replayed by the proxy after service crash via resendConfigIfNeeded().
    37	  func setWarmEnginePolicy(_ rawValue: String)
    38	
    39	  // MARK: - Lifecycle (Step 3)
    40	
    41	  /// Phase 1: start the audio engine, trigger BT codec switch, register config-change observer.
    42	  /// Device UIDs are passed inline so the proxy doesn't need separate setter replay before this call.
    43	  func startEnginePhase(
    44	    preferredDeviceUID: String, selectedDeviceUID: String, reply: @escaping (NSError?) -> Void)
    45	
    46	  /// Poll engine format until stable after BT codec negotiation.
    47	  func waitForFormatStabilization(
    48	    maxWait: Double, pollInterval: Double, reply: @escaping (Bool) -> Void)
    49	
    50	  /// Phase 2: install audio tap and begin capture. Buffers flow back via audioBufferCaptured callback.
    51	  func beginCapture(reply: @escaping (NSError?) -> Void)
    52	
    53	  /// Stop capture and return accumulated samples + finalized VAD segments atomically.
    54	  /// First Data = raw Float32 sample bytes. Second Data = packed [Int32 start, Int32 end] VAD segment pairs.
    55	  /// VAD segments are finalized BEFORE the sample buffer is cleared, preventing the call-order bug
    56	  /// where separate stopCapture/getVADSegments calls produced endSample=0 on open segments.
    57	  func stopCapture(reply: @escaping (Data, Data) -> Void)
    58	
    59	  /// Cancel a pre-warmed engine that never began capture.
    60	  func abortPreWarm()
    61	
    62	  /// Replace the audio engine with a fresh instance (lighter than buildEngine).
    63	  func rebuildEngine()
    64	
    65	  // MARK: - VAD (Step 5)
    66	
    67	  /// Configure service-side VAD. Replayed by the proxy after service crash via resendConfigIfNeeded().
    68	  func configureVAD(autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool)
    69	
    70	  /// Return a snapshot of captured samples starting at `fromIndex`.
    71	  /// Reply carries raw Float32 Data slice + totalCount at snapshot moment.
    72	  func getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: Int, reply: @escaping (Data, Int) -> Void)
    73	
    74	  /// Return speech segments detected by service-side VAD.
    75	  /// Reply carries Data of packed [Int32 startIndex, Int32 endIndex] pairs.
    76	  func getVADSegments(reply: @escaping (Data) -> Void)
    77	}
    78	
    79	/// XPC protocol: callbacks from audio service to host app.
    80	///
    81	/// These callbacks arrive on an XPC dispatch queue (not the RT audio thread, not the main thread).
    82	/// The proxy hops to @MainActor via Task before updating observable state.
    83	@objc public protocol AudioServiceClientProtocol {
    84	  /// Audio buffer captured — carries converted 16kHz mono Float32 PCM data.
    85	  /// Transport format: raw Float32 bytes, non-interleaved mono, 16kHz sample rate.
    86	  /// No header or metadata — just the raw sample bytes.
    87	  func audioBufferCaptured(_ data: Data, frameCount: Int, audioLevel: Float)
    88	
    89	  /// The audio engine was interrupted (device disconnect, emergency teardown, max-duration cap).
    90	  /// The proxy should transition pipelines to error state.
    91	  func engineInterrupted()
    92	
    93	  /// Service-side VAD detected sustained silence after speech — auto-stop should trigger.
    94	  func vadAutoStopTriggered()
    95	}
