     1	import CoreAudio
     2	
     3	/// Policy for selecting the audio capture source.
     4	/// `.automatic` decides based on output route + input intent.
     5	/// `.forceEngine` / `.forceCaptureSession` are for debugging/testing.
     6	public enum CaptureSourcePolicy: Sendable {
     7	  case automatic
     8	  case forceEngine
     9	  case forceCaptureSession
    10	}
    11	
    12	/// Which capture backend to use.
    13	public enum CaptureSourceType: Sendable {
    14	  case audioEngine
    15	  case captureSession
    16	}
    17	
    18	/// Machine-readable reason for the route decision. Used for telemetry.
    19	public enum CaptureRouteReason: String, Sendable {
    20	  case btOutputAutoInput
    21	  case btOutputUserSelectedBTMic
    22	  case btOutputUserSelectedBuiltIn
    23	  case btOutputUserSelectedWired
    24	  case noBTAutoInput
    25	  case noBTUserSelectedDevice
    26	  case forcedEngine
    27	  case forcedCaptureSession
    28	  case fallbackToEngine
    29	  case failedNoFallback
    30	}
    31	
    32	/// The result of route resolution — tells AudioCaptureManager which source to create and why.
    33	public struct CaptureRouteDecision: Sendable {
    34	  public let sourceType: CaptureSourceType
    35	  public let reason: CaptureRouteReason
    36	  public let rationale: String
    37	  public let vpAvailable: Bool
    38	  public let fallbackAllowed: Bool
    39	
    40	  public init(
    41	    sourceType: CaptureSourceType,
    42	    reason: CaptureRouteReason,
    43	    rationale: String,
    44	    vpAvailable: Bool,
    45	    fallbackAllowed: Bool
    46	  ) {
    47	    self.sourceType = sourceType
    48	    self.reason = reason
    49	    self.rationale = rationale
    50	    self.vpAvailable = vpAvailable
    51	    self.fallbackAllowed = fallbackAllowed
    52	  }
    53	}
    54	
    55	/// Decides which `AudioInputSource` to use based on output route, user input preference, and policy.
    56	///
    57	/// Ownership: source selection, device discovery, transport filtering, user preference interpretation.
    58	/// Does NOT create sources — returns a decision that AudioCaptureManager acts on.
    59	@MainActor
    60	struct CaptureRouteResolver {
    61	
    62	  var policy: CaptureSourcePolicy = .automatic
    63	
    64	  /// Resolve which capture source to use.
    65	  ///
    66	  /// - Parameters:
    67	  ///   - preferredInputDeviceUID: User's explicit device choice. Empty = Auto.
    68	  ///   - noiseSuppression: Whether noise suppression is requested (only available on engine path).
    69	  func resolve(preferredInputDeviceUID: String, noiseSuppression: Bool) -> CaptureRouteDecision {
    70	    switch policy {
    71	    case .forceEngine:
    72	      return CaptureRouteDecision(
    73	        sourceType: .audioEngine,
    74	        reason: .forcedEngine,
    75	        rationale: "Policy override: forced engine",
    76	        vpAvailable: true,
    77	        fallbackAllowed: false
    78	      )
    79	    case .forceCaptureSession:
    80	      return CaptureRouteDecision(
    81	        sourceType: .captureSession,
    82	        reason: .forcedCaptureSession,
    83	        rationale: "Policy override: forced capture session",
    84	        vpAvailable: false,
    85	        fallbackAllowed: false
    86	      )
    87	    case .automatic:
    88	      return resolveAutomatic(
    89	        preferredInputDeviceUID: preferredInputDeviceUID, noiseSuppression: noiseSuppression)
    90	    }
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  // periphery:ignore:parameters noiseSuppression - reserved for future VP-aware routing decisions
    94	  private func resolveAutomatic(preferredInputDeviceUID: String, noiseSuppression: Bool)
    95	    -> CaptureRouteDecision
    96	  {
    97	    let btOutputActive: Bool
    98	    if let outID = AudioDeviceEnumerator.defaultOutputDeviceID() {
    99	      btOutputActive = AudioDeviceEnumerator.isBluetoothDevice(outID)
   100	    } else {
   101	      btOutputActive = false
   102	    }
   103	
   104	    // No BT output — safe to use AVAudioEngine for everything
   105	    guard btOutputActive else {
   106	      if preferredInputDeviceUID.isEmpty {
   107	        return CaptureRouteDecision(
   108	          sourceType: .audioEngine,
   109	          reason: .noBTAutoInput,
   110	          rationale: "No BT output — engine with system default input",
   111	          vpAvailable: true,
   112	          fallbackAllowed: false
   113	        )
   114	      } else {
   115	        return CaptureRouteDecision(
   116	          sourceType: .audioEngine,
   117	          reason: .noBTUserSelectedDevice,
   118	          rationale: "No BT output — engine with user device \(preferredInputDeviceUID)",
   119	          vpAvailable: true,
   120	          fallbackAllowed: false
   121	        )
   122	      }
   123	    }
   124	
   125	    // BT output active — use AVCaptureSession to avoid A2DP→SCO switch.
   126	    // Do NOT re-enter AVAudioEngine path under BT output regardless of user preference.
   127	    let reason: CaptureRouteReason
   128	    let rationale: String
   129	
   130	    if preferredInputDeviceUID.isEmpty {
   131	      reason = .btOutputAutoInput
   132	      rationale = "BT output active, auto input — capture session with built-in mic"
   133	    } else {
   134	      // Check if user's preferred device is BT
   135	      let prefDeviceID = AudioDeviceEnumerator.deviceID(forUID: preferredInputDeviceUID)
   136	      let prefIsBT = prefDeviceID.map { AudioDeviceEnumerator.isBluetoothDevice($0) } ?? false
   137	
   138	      if prefIsBT {
   139	        reason = .btOutputUserSelectedBTMic
   140	        rationale =
   141	          "BT output active, user selected BT mic — override to capture session with built-in mic (crash prevention)"
   142	      } else {
   143	        // User selected built-in or wired — still use capture session under BT output
   144	        let isBuiltIn =
   145	          prefDeviceID.map {
   146	            AudioDeviceEnumerator.transportType(for: $0) == kAudioDeviceTransportTypeBuiltIn
   147	          } ?? false
   148	        reason = isBuiltIn ? .btOutputUserSelectedBuiltIn : .btOutputUserSelectedWired
   149	        rationale =
   150	          "BT output active, user selected \(isBuiltIn ? "built-in" : "wired") device — capture session"
   151	      }
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    return CaptureRouteDecision(
   155	      sourceType: .captureSession,
   156	      reason: reason,
   157	      rationale: rationale,
   158	      vpAvailable: false,
   159	      fallbackAllowed: !btOutputActive  // Only fall back if BT is not active
   160	    )
   161	  }
   162	}

   274	      case .whisperKit:

   278	      }

   280	      // sets state = .error(...), which fires onStateChange and shows the error
   281	      // overlay. Calling hide() immediately after would dismiss it before the
   282	      // user can read it. The error overlay auto-dismisses after 3 seconds.
   283	    }

    50	  func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
    51	    // Hide dock icon on launch — we're a menu bar utility.
    52	    // If onboarding is needed, stay .regular so SwiftUI creates the main window hierarchy
    53	    // and ActionWirer can wire callbacks before opening the onboarding window.
    54	    if appState.settings.onboardingState == .completed {
    55	      NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
    56	    }
    57	
    58	    // When the unified window closes, revert to .accessory immediately.
    59	    // There's only one window now, so no need for the 200ms re-check delay.
    60	    // Store token so we can remove on termination (H11 observer leak fix).
    61	    windowCloseObserver = NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
    62	      forName: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
    63	      object: nil,
    64	      queue: .main
    65	    ) { [weak self] notification in
    66	      guard let window = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
    67	      MainActor.assumeIsolated {
    68	        guard let self else { return }
    69	        // Capture the main window reference on first titled window appearance.
    70	        if self.mainWindow == nil, window.styleMask.contains(.titled),
    71	          window.title == AppConstants.appName
    72	        {
    73	          self.mainWindow = window
    74	        }
    75	        // Match by identity so status-bar/panel windows never trigger the reset.
    76	        guard window === self.mainWindow else { return }
    77	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
    78	      }
    79	    }

    89	    // Update menu bar icon whenever pipeline state or accessibility changes
    90	    appState.onPipelineStateChange = { [weak self] _ in
    91	      self?.updateIcon()
    92	    }
    93	    appState.permissions.onAccessibilityChange = { [weak self] in
    94	      self?.updateIcon()
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    // Start hotkeys now that the event loop is running.
    98	    // Carbon RegisterEventHotKey requires an active run loop for event delivery.
    99	    appState.startHotkeyServiceIfEnabled()
   100	
   101	    if appState.settings.onboardingState == .completed {
   102	      let s = appState.settings
   103	      let hasKeys =
   104	        (try? appState.keychainManager.retrieve(key: KeychainManager.openAIKeyID)) != nil
   105	        || (try? appState.keychainManager.retrieve(key: KeychainManager.geminiKeyID)) != nil
   106	      TelemetryService.shared.settingsSnapshot(
   107	        asrBackend: s.selectedBackend.rawValue,
   108	        llmProvider: s.llmProvider.rawValue,
   109	        recordingMode: s.recordingMode.rawValue,
   110	        writingStyle: s.writingStylePreset.rawValue,
   111	        fillerRemoval: s.fillerRemovalEnabled,
   112	        customWordsCount: appState.customWordsCoordinator.customWords.count,
   113	        hasApiKeys: hasKeys,
   114	        noiseSuppression: s.noiseSuppression
   115	      )
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    // Run Apple Intelligence diagnostics via coordinator.
   119	    // Handles: Sentry context, PostHog event, persistence, first-launch re-check.
   120	    let isFreshInstall = appState.settings.onboardingState != .completed
   121	    if isFreshInstall {
   122	      appState.aiAvailability.firstLaunchCheck()
   123	    } else {
   124	      Task { await appState.aiAvailability.checkAvailability(trigger: "app_launch") }
   125	    }
   126	
   127	    // Fire structured app.launched event — uses cached report (loaded from UserDefaults in coordinator init).
   128	    // No async wait needed — cached snapshot is available synchronously.
   129	    let version =
   130	      Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey: "CFBundleShortVersionString") as? String ?? "?"
   131	    let build = Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey: "CFBundleVersion") as? String ?? "?"
   132	    let osVer = ProcessInfo.processInfo.operatingSystemVersion
   133	    let cachedReport = appState.aiAvailability.latestReport
   134	    TelemetryService.shared.appLaunched(
   135	      version: version,
   136	      build: build,
   137	      osVersion: "\(osVer.majorVersion).\(osVer.minorVersion).\(osVer.patchVersion)",
   138	      hardware: cachedReport?.hardwareClass ?? "unknown",
   139	      isFreshInstall: isFreshInstall,
   140	      aiAvailable: cachedReport?.overallStatus == .available
   141	    )
   142	
   143	    // Check Accessibility permission on launch (query only — never auto-prompt).
   144	    appState.permissions.refreshOnLaunch()
   145	    updateIcon()  // Reflect accessibility warning state in menu bar icon
   146	
   147	    // Begin smart polling if Accessibility is not yet granted.
   148	    appState.permissions.startMonitoring()
   149	
   150	    // Pre-warm LLM backend with a real inference request.
   151	    LLMNetworkSession.shared.preWarmModel(
   152	      provider: appState.settings.llmProvider,
   153	      model: appState.settings.llmModel,
   154	      keychainManager: appState.keychainManager
   155	    )
   156	
   157	    // Onboarding auto-open is handled by ActionWirer inside the main Window scene.
   158	    // ActionWirer wires all callbacks first, then calls openOnboardingWindow() if needed.
   159	    // No deferred dispatch required here.
   160	
   161	    #if DEBUG
   162	      // V2 fault-injection (issue #291). Only when explicitly opted in via env var.
   163	      if DebugFaultEndpoint.isRequested {
   164	        let endpoint = DebugFaultEndpoint(
   165	          audioProxy: appState.audioCapture as? AudioCaptureProxy,
   166	          asrProxy: appState.asrManager as? ASRManagerProxy,
   167	          parakeetPipeline: appState.pipeline,
   168	          whisperKitPipeline: appState.whisperKitPipeline,
   169	          activeBackend: { [weak self] in
   170	            self?.appState.settings.selectedBackend ?? .parakeet
   171	          }
   172	        )
   173	        endpoint.start()
   174	        debugFaultEndpoint = endpoint
   175	      }
   176	    #endif
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  func applicationDidBecomeActive(_ notification: Notification) {
   180	    // Re-warm LLM backend when app comes to foreground.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba Tests/RuntimeUAT/faultInjection.py | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""
     2	V2 fault-injection harness for EnviousWispr (issue #291).
     3	
     4	Drives the DEBUG-only `DebugFaultEndpoint` in the running app via a localhost
     5	TCP listener. To run end-to-end: invoke the `wispr-rebuild-debug` skill, which
     6	compiles `-c debug` (so `#if DEBUG` seams are present) and launches the debug
     7	bundle with `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1` set via `open --env`. Without both gates
     8	satisfied (DEBUG build AND env var), the endpoint is inert. The release path
     9	(`scripts/bundle-dev.sh`) does NOT contain the endpoint — by design.
    10	
    11	Wire protocol (per `Sources/EnviousWispr/App/Debug/DebugFaultEndpoint.swift`):
    12	
    13	    <token>\\n
    14	    <command>\\n
    15	
    16	Reply: `OK\\n`, `OK <state>\\n` (for `query_state`), or `ERR <reason>\\n`.
    17	
    18	Token: per-launch random hex written by the app to
    19	`~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/fault-token-<pid>` with `0600` perms. The app
    20	deletes the file on `applicationWillTerminate`.
    21	
    22	Lane breakdown (see `Tests/RuntimeUAT/SCENARIOS.md` for the indexed menu):
    23	- Lane A (Claude-driven): A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6, A7, A8a, A8b, A9
    24	- Lane B (founder-required): B1
    25	- Lane B' (optional, programmatic device flip): not yet implemented; pending the
    26	  spike documented in the V2 plan §3.2.
    27	
    28	Each scenario function carries metadata via the `@scenario` decorator so
    29	`list_scenarios()` can render the menu without importing wispr_eyes.
    30	"""
    31	
    32	from __future__ import annotations
    33	
    34	import os
    35	import socket
    36	import subprocess
    37	import sys
    38	import time
    39	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    40	from pathlib import Path
    41	from typing import Callable, Optional
    42	
    43	ENDPOINT_HOST = "127.0.0.1"
    44	ENDPOINT_PORT = 8765
    45	TOKEN_DIR = Path("~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr").expanduser()
    46	APP_NAME = "EnviousWispr"
    47	
    48	
    49	# ──────────────────────────── scenario metadata ────────────────────────────
    50	
    51	
    52	@dataclass
    53	class ScenarioMeta:
    54	    name: str
    55	    lane: str  # "A" | "B" | "B'"
    56	    family: str  # timing | stall | xpc | settings | app-quit | model-load | backend-switch | bt-route
    57	    backends: list[str]  # ["parakeet"] | ["whisperKit"] | ["both"]
    58	    runtime_budget_seconds: float
    59	    founder_required: bool
    60	    negative_control: str
    61	    description: str
    62	
    63	
    64	_REGISTRY: dict[str, tuple[Callable, ScenarioMeta]] = {}
    65	
    66	
    67	def scenario(meta: ScenarioMeta):
    68	    """Decorator: registers a scenario function with metadata."""
    69	
    70	    def wrap(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
    71	        _REGISTRY[meta.name] = (fn, meta)
    72	        return fn
    73	
    74	    return wrap
    75	
    76	
    77	def list_scenarios() -> list[ScenarioMeta]:
    78	    """Return all registered scenarios. Use `print_scenarios()` for a menu."""
    79	    return [meta for _, meta in _REGISTRY.values()]
    80	
    81	
    82	def print_scenarios() -> None:
    83	    rows = sorted(list_scenarios(), key=lambda m: m.name)
    84	    print(f"{'NAME':<24} {'LANE':<5} {'FAMILY':<14} {'BACKENDS':<14} {'BUDGET':<8} FOUNDER")
    85	    print("-" * 80)
    86	    for m in rows:
    87	        backends = ",".join(m.backends)
    88	        print(
    89	            f"{m.name:<24} {m.lane:<5} {m.family:<14} {backends:<14} "
    90	            f"{m.runtime_budget_seconds:<8.1f} {'YES' if m.founder_required else 'no'}"
    91	        )
    92	        print(f"  {m.description}")
    93	        print(f"  negative control: {m.negative_control}")
    94	
    95	
    96	def run_scenario(name: str, **kwargs) -> dict:
    97	    """Dispatch a single scenario by name. Returns a result dict."""
    98	    if name not in _REGISTRY:
    99	        raise KeyError(f"unknown scenario: {name!r} (use print_scenarios() to list)")
   100	    fn, meta = _REGISTRY[name]
   101	    if meta.founder_required and not kwargs.get("founder_present"):
   102	        raise RuntimeError(
   103	            f"{name} is Lane B (founder-required). Re-run with founder_present=True after "
   104	            "physically performing the manual step described in SCENARIOS.md."
   105	        )
   106	    started = time.monotonic()
   107	    result = fn(**kwargs)
   108	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - started
   109	    return {"name": name, "lane": meta.lane, "elapsed_seconds": elapsed, "result": result}
   110	
   111	
   112	# ──────────────────────────── endpoint client ────────────────────────────
   113	
   114	
   115	def _find_app_pid() -> int:
   116	    """Locate the running EnviousWispr process. Raises if not running."""
   117	    try:
   118	        out = subprocess.check_output(["pgrep", "-x", APP_NAME], text=True)
   119	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   120	        raise RuntimeError(f"{APP_NAME} is not running — launch it first") from e
   121	    pids = [int(p.strip()) for p in out.split() if p.strip()]
   122	    if not pids:
   123	        raise RuntimeError(f"{APP_NAME} not found via pgrep")
   124	    return pids[0]
   125	
   126	
   127	def _read_token(pid: int) -> str:
   128	    """Read the per-launch fault token. Raises if the file is missing — that
   129	    means the app was not launched via `wispr-rebuild-debug` (or equivalent
   130	    debug-bundle path that sets `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1`)."""
   131	    path = TOKEN_DIR / f"fault-token-{pid}"
   132	    if not path.exists():
   133	        raise RuntimeError(
   134	            f"fault token not found at {path} — invoke the `wispr-rebuild-debug` "
   135	            "skill to build and launch the debug bundle with EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1 set. "
   136	            "The release path (`scripts/bundle-dev.sh`) does not contain the endpoint."
   137	        )
   138	    return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
   139	
   140	
   141	def send(command: str, timeout: float = 5.0) -> str:
   142	    """Send a command and return the reply (without trailing newline)."""
   143	    pid = _find_app_pid()
   144	    token = _read_token(pid)
   145	    payload = f"{token}\n{command}\n".encode("utf-8")
   146	    with socket.create_connection((ENDPOINT_HOST, ENDPOINT_PORT), timeout=timeout) as sock:
   147	        sock.sendall(payload)
   148	        chunks: list[bytes] = []
   149	        sock.settimeout(timeout)
   150	        while True:
   151	            try:
   152	                buf = sock.recv(4096)
   153	            except socket.timeout:
   154	                break
   155	            if not buf:
   156	                break
   157	            chunks.append(buf)
   158	            if b"\n" in buf:
   159	                break
   160	    return b"".join(chunks).decode("utf-8").strip()
   161	
   162	
   163	def query_state() -> str:
   164	    return send("query_state")
   165	
   166	
   167	def force_cancel() -> str:
   168	    return send("force_cancel")
   169	
   170	
   171	def force_xpc_kill() -> str:
   172	    return send("force_xpc_kill")
   173	
   174	
   175	def force_audio_xpc_kill() -> str:
   176	    return send("force_audio_xpc_kill")
   177	
   178	
   179	def force_stall(n: int) -> str:
   180	    return send(f"force_stall({n})")

   183	# ──────────────────────────── helpers ────────────────────────────

   185	
   186	def _import_wispr_eyes():
   187	    """Lazy import so this module loads even if wispr_eyes' deps are absent."""
   188	    here = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
   189	    if str(here) not in sys.path:
   190	        sys.path.insert(0, str(here))
   191	    import wispr_eyes  # noqa: E402
   192	
   193	    return wispr_eyes
   194	
   195	
   196	_TERMINAL_TOKENS = ("idle", "complete", "error", "ready")
   197	
   198	
   199	def _parse_query_state(reply: str) -> dict[str, str]:
   200	    """Parse `OK parakeet=<state> whisperkit=<state> backend=<X>` into a dict.
   201	    Returns `{}` if the reply doesn't match the expected shape."""
   202	    if not reply.startswith("OK "):
   203	        return {}
   204	    parts: dict[str, str] = {}
   205	    for token in reply[3:].split():
   206	        if "=" in token:
   207	            k, v = token.split("=", 1)
   208	            parts[k] = v
   209	    return parts
   210	
   211	
   212	def _backend_pipeline_key(backend: str) -> str:
   213	    # `backend=.parakeet` / `backend=.whisperKit` → which pipeline-state key matters
   214	    if "whisper" in backend.lower():
   215	        return "whisperkit"
   216	    return "parakeet"
   217	
   218	
   219	def assert_terminated(timeout_s: float = 5.0) -> dict:
   220	    """Poll `query_state` until the ACTIVE backend's pipeline reaches a
   221	    terminal state (.idle / .complete / .error / .ready) or the budget
   222	    expires.
   223	
   224	    The `query_state` reply always carries both pipeline states; the
   225	    inactive backend is typically idle or ready while the active one is
   226	    still running. Checking "any pipeline terminal" would always return
   227	    True immediately and silently mask Lane A regressions (Codex P1
   228	    feedback on PR #544). We must check the active backend specifically.
   229	    """
   230	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
   231	    last = ""
   232	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   233	        last = query_state()
   234	        parsed = _parse_query_state(last)
   235	        backend = parsed.get("backend", "")
   236	        active_key = _backend_pipeline_key(backend)
   237	        active_state = parsed.get(active_key, "")
   238	        if active_state and any(t in active_state for t in _TERMINAL_TOKENS):
   239	            return {"terminal": True, "state": last, "active": active_state}
   240	        time.sleep(0.1)
   241	    return {"terminal": False, "state": last}
   242	
   243	
   244	def list_xpc_service_pids() -> list[str]:
   245	    """Return PIDs of all live EnviousWispr XPC service helpers
   246	    (`EnviousWisprAudioService`, `EnviousWisprASRService`).
   247	
   248	    On macOS, XPC services are launchd-managed: invalidating the connection
   249	    does not terminate the helper process — launchd respawns it as needed.
   250	    The right "no leak" assertion is therefore a delta check: count after
   251	    the fault must not exceed count before.
   252	    """
   253	    try:
   254	        out = subprocess.check_output(
   255	            ["pgrep", "-f", "EnviousWispr.*Service"],
   256	            text=True,
   257	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   258	        )
   259	        return [p.strip() for p in out.split() if p.strip()]
   260	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba Tests/RuntimeUAT/faultInjection.py | sed -n '261,560p'" in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
   261	        return []
   262	
   263	
   264	def assert_no_xpc_leak(before: list[str]) -> dict:
   265	    """Confirm the XPC service helper count did not grow after a fault.
   266	
   267	    Returns `{"leaked": False, "before": [...], "after": [...]}` on a
   268	    pass; `leaked: True` if more service helpers exist now than before.
   269	    Identity changes (PID rotation) are expected and not a leak — only
   270	    a net increase is a leak.
   271	    """
   272	    after = list_xpc_service_pids()
   273	    return {"leaked": len(after) > len(before), "before": before, "after": after}
   274	
   275	
   276	# Backwards-compatible alias retained for existing scenarios that still call
   277	# the old name. New code should use `list_xpc_service_pids` + `assert_no_xpc_leak`.
   278	def assert_no_zombie() -> dict:
   279	    pids = list_xpc_service_pids()
   280	    return {"orphan_pids": pids}
   281

   286	_RIGHT_CMD_KEYCODE = 54
   287	_ESC_KEYCODE = 53
   288	
   289	
   290	def _import_simulate_input():
   291	    """Lazy import so this module loads even if Quartz isn't available."""
   292	    here = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
   293	    if str(here) not in sys.path:
   294	        sys.path.insert(0, str(here))
   295	    import simulate_input  # noqa: E402
   296	
   297	    return simulate_input

   299	
   300	def _active_state() -> str:
   301	    """Return the active backend's pipeline state string (e.g. `idle`,
   302	    `recording`, `transcribing`)."""
   303	    parsed = _parse_query_state(query_state())
   304	    return parsed.get(_backend_pipeline_key(parsed.get("backend", "")), "")
   305	
   306	
   307	def _tap_rcmd(hold_s: float = 0.05) -> None:
   308	    """Single Right-Cmd press+release. Hold time matches a quick PTT tap.
   309	
   310	    HotkeyService treats a single tap-and-release with a 500 ms debounce —
   311	    if no second press arrives, it fires onStopRecording. Use double-tap to
   312	    enter hands-free locked mode for sustained recording.
   313	    """
   314	    sim = _import_simulate_input()
   315	    sim.modifier_down(_RIGHT_CMD_KEYCODE)
   316	    time.sleep(hold_s)
   317	    sim.modifier_up(_RIGHT_CMD_KEYCODE)
   318	
   319	
   320	def _start_recording_locked(*, gap_s: float = 0.25, settle_s: float = 0.8,
   321	                            timeout_s: float = 4.0,
   322	                            post_lock_dwell_s: float = 0.6) -> bool:
   323	    """Enter hands-free locked recording via double-tap.
   324	
   325	    Two Right-Cmd taps within HotkeyService's 500 ms double-press window
   326	    transitions to `isRecordingLocked = true`, which suppresses the
   327	    debounced stop-on-release and keeps recording until the next single
   328	    tap. Verifies post-state and retries if the first attempt missed
   329	    (handles the first-of-process CGEvent ghost we observed 2026-05-02).
   330	
   331	    Timing safety (against HotkeyService's gestures):
   332	    - `gap_s = 250 ms` keeps both taps comfortably inside the 500 ms
   333	      double-press window without crowding the upper bound.
   334	    - `post_lock_dwell_s = 600 ms` lets HotkeyService's `lockTime`
   335	      cooldown elapse before any subsequent tap. Without this dwell, a
   336	      tap landing within 500 ms of `lockTime` is silently ignored
   337	      ('Press ignored — lock cooldown'), or worse, stacks with two
   338	      retry-loop taps to look like a triple-press cancel gesture.
   339	    """
   340	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
   341	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   342	        if "recording" in _active_state():
   343	            time.sleep(post_lock_dwell_s)
   344	            return True
   345	        _tap_rcmd()
   346	        time.sleep(gap_s)
   347	        _tap_rcmd()
   348	        time.sleep(settle_s)
   349	        if "recording" in _active_state():
   350	            time.sleep(post_lock_dwell_s)
   351	            return True
   352	        # Don't pile retry taps onto an unstable state — pause before
   353	        # the next attempt so the previous tap pair can fully settle.
   354	        time.sleep(0.4)
   355	    return "recording" in _active_state()
   356	
   357	
   358	def _stop_recording_locked(*, settle_s: float = 1.5) -> bool:
   359	    """Single tap to exit hands-free locked recording, then wait for terminal.
   360	
   361	    Returns True if pipeline reached idle/complete within `settle_s`.
   362	    """
   363	    _tap_rcmd()
   364	    deadline = time.monotonic() + settle_s
   365	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   366	        st = _active_state()
   367	        if any(t in st for t in _TERMINAL_TOKENS):
   368	            return True
   369	        time.sleep(0.1)
   370	    return any(t in _active_state() for t in _TERMINAL_TOKENS)
   371	
   372	
   373	def _activate_app() -> None:
   374	    """Bring the dev bundle to the foreground so keystrokes (ESC,
   375	    arrow keys, etc.) are routed to it.
   376	
   377	    Right-Cmd is registered by HotkeyService as a global Carbon hotkey
   378	    and fires regardless of focus, but bare-Escape is not a true
   379	    system-wide hotkey on macOS — the OS lets the foreground app
   380	    consume it first. Without explicit activation, an ESC keystroke
   381	    sent while Claude Code or another app is foreground would cancel
   382	    that app instead of the dictation. Confirmed empirically 2026-05-02.
   383	    """
   384	    subprocess.run(
   385	        ["osascript", "-e",
   386	         'tell application id "com.enviouswispr.app.dev" to activate'],
   387	        check=False, capture_output=True,
   388	    )
   389	    time.sleep(0.2)  # let WindowServer settle the focus change
   390	
   391	
   392	def _press_esc() -> None:
   393	    """Send a single Escape keystroke — the user-real cancel gesture for
   394	    an in-flight dictation. ESC is what the app binds for cancel; the
   395	    `force_cancel` debug endpoint command bypasses the keybind and would
   396	    not exercise the same code path."""
   397	    sim = _import_simulate_input()
   398	    # Escape is a regular (non-modifier) key — use the standard down/up via
   399	    # CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent rather than flagsChanged.
   400	    from Quartz import (  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
   401	        CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent,
   402	        CGEventPost,
   403	        kCGHIDEventTap,
   404	    )
   405	
   406	    down = CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent(None, _ESC_KEYCODE, True)
   407	    up = CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent(None, _ESC_KEYCODE, False)
   408	    CGEventPost(kCGHIDEventTap, down)
   409	    time.sleep(0.03)
   410	    CGEventPost(kCGHIDEventTap, up)
   411	
   412	
   413	def _assert_dictation_recovers(
   414	    *,
   415	    sentence: str = "recovery check the cat sat on the mat",
   416	    record_seconds: float = 2.5,
   417	    settle_timeout_s: float = 8.0,
   418	) -> dict:
   419	    """Run an end-to-end dictation cycle after a fault was injected, and
   420	    confirm the software actually recovered — not just that the previous
   421	    cycle reached a terminal state.
   422	
   423	    Sequence:
   424	      1. Force pipeline back to idle if it's still in error/recording
   425	         (a single rcmd tap clears most non-idle states; double-tap won't
   426	         enter lock from non-idle).
   427	      2. Double-tap to enter hands-free locked recording.
   428	      3. Play TTS audio for `record_seconds` so capture has real audio.
   429	      4. Single-tap to exit lock.
   430	      5. Wait up to `settle_timeout_s` for the pipeline to reach a non-
   431	         error terminal state (`complete` or `idle`).
   432	
   433	    Returns a dict capturing what happened so the caller can decide if
   434	    recovery passed:
   435	        {
   436	            "recovered": bool,                  # True if final state in {complete, idle} and not error
   437	            "second_cycle_reached_recording": bool,
   438	            "second_cycle_terminal_state": str,  # the active state at end
   439	            "second_cycle_elapsed_s": float,
   440	        }
   441	    """
   442	    t_start = time.monotonic()
   443	    # Step 1: clear any non-idle state so double-tap can enter lock.
   444	    state = _active_state()
   445	    if "error" in state or "recording" in state:
   446	        _tap_rcmd()
   447	        time.sleep(0.8)
   448	    # Step 2: enter locked recording.
   449	    locked = _start_recording_locked()
   450	    if not locked:
   451	        return {
   452	            "recovered": False,
   453	            "second_cycle_reached_recording": False,
   454	            "second_cycle_terminal_state": _active_state(),
   455	            "second_cycle_elapsed_s": time.monotonic() - t_start,
   456	        }
   457	    # Step 3: play TTS during the recording window.
   458	    with _TTSAudio(sentence):
   459	        time.sleep(record_seconds)
   460	    # Step 4: exit lock.
   461	    _stop_recording_locked(settle_s=0.5)
   462	    # Step 5: wait for terminal state.
   463	    deadline = time.monotonic() + settle_timeout_s
   464	    final = ""
   465	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   466	        final = _active_state()
   467	        # `complete` is the success signal; `idle` is acceptable when the
   468	        # backend short-circuits (e.g. nothing to transcribe). `error` is
   469	        # a recovery failure.
   470	        if "complete" in final or final == "idle":
   471	            break
   472	        if "error" in final:
   473	            break
   474	        time.sleep(0.1)
   475	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t_start
   476	    recovered = ("complete" in final or final == "idle") and "error" not in final
   477	    return {
   478	        "recovered": recovered,
   479	        "second_cycle_reached_recording": True,
   480	        "second_cycle_terminal_state": final,
   481	        "second_cycle_elapsed_s": elapsed,
   482	    }
   483	
   484	
   485	def _wait_for_app_exit(timeout_s: float = 10.0) -> bool:
   486	    """Poll until no `EnviousWispr Local.app` main process remains."""
   487	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
   488	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   489	        result = subprocess.run(
   490	            ["pgrep", "-f", "EnviousWispr Local.app/Contents/MacOS/EnviousWispr"],
   491	            capture_output=True, text=True,
   492	        )
   493	        if result.returncode != 0:
   494	            return True
   495	        time.sleep(0.2)
   496	    return False
   497	
   498	
   499	def _wait_for_endpoint(timeout_s: float = 10.0) -> bool:
   500	    """Poll the DebugFaultEndpoint until it responds, after a relaunch.
   501	
   502	    The token file and the endpoint listener are both written by the
   503	    running app after `applicationDidFinishLaunching`. Until that path
   504	    completes, `query_state` raises (token missing) or the connection
   505	    refuses (endpoint not listening). Tolerate both as transient.
   506	    """
   507	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
   508	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
   509	        try:
   510	            reply = query_state()
   511	            if reply.startswith("OK "):
   512	                return True
   513	        except (RuntimeError, ConnectionError, OSError):
   514	            pass
   515	        time.sleep(0.3)
   516	    return False
   517	
   518	
   519	def _relaunch_app() -> dict:
   520	    """Re-open the dev bundle with EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1 set so the
   521	    DebugFaultEndpoint comes back up. Used by scenarios that terminate
   522	    the app (A7) and still need a recovery cycle.
   523	    """
   524	    bundle_path = (
   525	        "/Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr/build/"
   526	        "EnviousWispr Local.app"
   527	    )
   528	    subprocess.run(
   529	        ["open", "--env", "EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1", bundle_path],
   530	        check=False,
   531	    )
   532	    endpoint_up = _wait_for_endpoint(timeout_s=12.0)
   533	    return {"relaunched": endpoint_up}
   534	
   535	
   536	class _TTSAudio:
   537	    """Context manager that plays TTS audio in the background while the
   538	    `with` block runs, so capture-side scenarios have real audio flowing
   539	    through the heart path during fault injection.
   540	
   541	    Usage:
   542	        with _TTSAudio("the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"):
   543	            # recording is active here, audio is being captured
   544	            force_xpc_kill()
   545	    """
   546	
   547	    def __init__(self, sentence: str = "the quick brown fox jumps over "
   548	                 "the lazy dog and the dog does not chase the fox"):
   549	        self.sentence = sentence
   550	        self._proc: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
   551	        self._wav: Optional[str] = None
   552	
   553	    def __enter__(self):
   554	        eyes = _import_wispr_eyes()
   555	        # Reuse the existing TTS helper (OpenAI echo by default, with
   556	        # macOS `say` fallback) — see Tests/RuntimeUAT/wispr_eyes.py.
   557	        self._wav = eyes.tts(self.sentence)
   558	        self._proc = subprocess.Popen(
   559	            ["afplay", self._wav],
   560	            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba Tests/RuntimeUAT/faultInjection.py | sed -n '561,860p'" in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
   561	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   562	        )
   563	        return self
   564	
   565	    def __exit__(self, *_exc):
   566	        if self._proc is not None:
   567	            try:
   568	                self._proc.terminate()
   569	                self._proc.wait(timeout=1.0)
   570	            except Exception:
   571	                self._proc.kill()
   572	        return False
   573	
   574	
   575	# ──────────────────────────── Lane A scenarios ────────────────────────────
   576	
   577	
   578	@scenario(
   579	    ScenarioMeta(
   580	        name="A1_rapid_stop_start",
   581	        lane="A",
   582	        family="timing",
   583	        backends=["both"],
   584	        runtime_budget_seconds=3.0,
   585	        founder_required=False,
   586	        negative_control="Remove the recording-start debounce; this scenario passes when it should fail",
   587	        description="Rapid stop/start fuzz at boundary (100ms) and jittered (100-500ms)",
   588	    )
   589	)
   590	def A1_rapid_stop_start(**_) -> dict:
   591	    """Spam Right-Cmd to fuzz the hotkey debounce, then verify the pipeline
   592	    can be cleanly stopped after the chaos.
   593	
   594	    Validate-then-automate (2026-05-02): menu taps ("Start Recording" /
   595	    "Stop Recording") cannot drive this scenario — macOS status-bar menu
   596	    items do not enumerate their children in the AX tree until the menu
   597	    is open. Hand-validation showed Right-Cmd CGEvent taps (keycode 54,
   598	    50 ms hold) reliably toggle recording, and that an even-tap-count
   599	    chaos phase can leave the pipeline stuck in `.recording` because
   600	    some taps are debounced. The scenario therefore needs an explicit
   601	    cleanup tap after a settle pause to guarantee `.idle`.
   602	    """
   603	    import random
   604	
   605	    # Phase 1 — boundary fuzz at 100 ms (debounce should swallow most).
   606	    for _ in range(6):
   607	        _tap_rcmd()
   608	        time.sleep(0.1)
   609	    # Phase 2 — jittered fuzz (100–500 ms).
   610	    for _ in range(6):
   611	        _tap_rcmd()
   612	        time.sleep(random.uniform(0.1, 0.5))
   613	    # Phase 3 — settle past the debounce window so the next tap is honored.
   614	    time.sleep(1.0)
   615	    # Phase 4 — cleanup tap if the chaos left us stuck recording.
   616	    if "recording" in _active_state():
   617	        _tap_rcmd()
   618	    return assert_terminated(timeout_s=3.0)
   619	
   620	
   621	@scenario(
   622	    ScenarioMeta(
   623	        name="A2_esc_cancel",
   624	        lane="A",
   625	        family="timing",
   626	        backends=["both"],
   627	        runtime_budget_seconds=4.0,
   628	        founder_required=False,
   629	        negative_control="Remove cancellation cleanup in TranscriptionPipeline.cancelRecording; scenario detects leaked task",
   630	        description="ESC mid-record (1 s into recording, audio flowing) — pipeline must reach idle within budget",
   631	    )
   632	)
   633	def A2_esc_cancel(**_) -> dict:
   634	    """User behavior: dictation in progress, audio is flowing, user hits
   635	    Escape to cancel. The keybind path (HotkeyService cancel hotkey) must
   636	    propagate to the pipeline and reach a terminal state.
   637	
   638	    Validate-then-automate notes (2026-05-02):
   639	    - Sustained recording requires double-tap → hands-free locked mode
   640	      (`_start_recording_locked`); a single tap auto-stops after the
   641	      500 ms PTT debounce window.
   642	    - ESC is the user-real cancel gesture. The `force_cancel` debug
   643	      endpoint command bypasses the keybind and would not exercise the
   644	      same wiring this scenario claims to test.
   645	    - TTS audio flows during the test so the cancel happens against an
   646	      active capture session, not silence.
   647	    """
   648	    if not _start_recording_locked():
   649	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
   650	    with _TTSAudio():
   651	        time.sleep(1.0)  # let audio flow into the pipeline
   652	        _activate_app()  # ESC must reach EnviousWispr, not whatever else is foreground
   653	        _press_esc()
   654	        result = assert_terminated(timeout_s=4.0)
   655	    esc_landed = any(t in _active_state() for t in _TERMINAL_TOKENS)
   656	    result["esc_drove_cancel"] = esc_landed
   657	    if not esc_landed:
   658	        # safety-net cleanup if ESC was somehow swallowed
   659	        _tap_rcmd()
   660	        time.sleep(1.0)
   661	    return result
   662	
   663	
   664	@scenario(
   665	    ScenarioMeta(
   666	        name="A3_asr_xpc_kill",
   667	        lane="A",
   668	        family="xpc",
   669	        backends=["parakeet"],
   670	        runtime_budget_seconds=8.0,
   671	        founder_required=False,
   672	        negative_control="Remove ASR-crash handler at WhisperKitPipeline.swift / TranscriptionPipeline equivalent; pipeline gets stuck",
   673	        description="ASR XPC connection invalidated mid-stream while TTS audio is flowing — pipeline must reach a terminal error state, no XPC helper leak",
   674	    )
   675	)
   676	def A3_asr_xpc_kill(**_) -> dict:
   677	    """User behavior: dictation in progress with audio flowing, the ASR
   678	    XPC connection drops (helper crashed, sandbox revoked, kernel
   679	    pressure). The pipeline must surface an error and recover — the user
   680	    sees a "service crashed" message instead of a stuck spinner.
   681	
   682	    Notes 2026-05-02:
   683	    - "Kill" here means `forceConnectionTerminationNow()` — invalidates
   684	      the NSXPCConnection. The launchd-managed service helper itself
   685	      stays alive; macOS XPC respawns it on next connect. So the leak
   686	      assertion compares helper counts before/after, not "any helper
   687	      survives" (that would always be true and silently pass).
   688	    - TTS audio must be flowing so the connection drops while the ASR
   689	      side is actually streaming. Without audio, the connection sits
   690	      idle and the kill exercises only the disconnect path, not the
   691	      mid-stream error wiring this scenario claims to test.
   692	    """
   693	    pre_helpers = list_xpc_service_pids()
   694	    if not _start_recording_locked():
   695	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
   696	    with _TTSAudio():
   697	        time.sleep(1.0)  # let audio stream into ASR
   698	        reply = force_xpc_kill()
   699	        terminated = assert_terminated(timeout_s=5.0)
   700	    leak = assert_no_xpc_leak(pre_helpers)
   701	    # Recovery check: a graceful failure that wedges the next dictation
   702	    # is indistinguishable from a crash. Confirm the user can actually
   703	    # keep dictating after the fault.
   704	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers()
   705	    return {"reply": reply, **terminated, **leak, **recovery}
   706	
   707	
   708	@scenario(
   709	    ScenarioMeta(
   710	        name="A4_audio_xpc_kill",
   711	        lane="A",
   712	        family="xpc",
   713	        backends=["both"],
   714	        runtime_budget_seconds=15.0,
   715	        founder_required=False,
   716	        negative_control="Remove audio-XPC-error handler in AudioCaptureProxy; pipeline gets stuck",
   717	        description="Audio capture XPC connection invalidated mid-stream while TTS audio is flowing — pipeline must reach a terminal state, no helper leak, next dictation must succeed",
   718	    )
   719	)
   720	def A4_audio_xpc_kill(**_) -> dict:
   721	    """User behavior: dictation in progress with audio flowing, the
   722	    audio-capture XPC connection drops (sandbox revoked, helper crash,
   723	    HAL reset). Pipeline must surface a terminal state, the audio helper
   724	    must respawn cleanly, and the next dictation must work end-to-end.
   725	    """
   726	    pre_helpers = list_xpc_service_pids()
   727	    if not _start_recording_locked():
   728	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
   729	    with _TTSAudio():
   730	        time.sleep(0.8)  # warm-up so capture buffers have started flowing
   731	        reply = force_audio_xpc_kill()
   732	        terminated = assert_terminated(timeout_s=5.0)
   733	    leak = assert_no_xpc_leak(pre_helpers)
   734	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers()
   735	    return {"reply": reply, **terminated, **leak, **recovery}
   736	
   737	
   738	@scenario(
   739	    ScenarioMeta(
   740	        name="A5_forced_stall",
   741	        lane="A",
   742	        family="stall",
   743	        backends=["both"],
   744	        runtime_budget_seconds=15.0,
   745	        founder_required=False,
   746	        negative_control="Remove the stall watchdog (armCaptureStallWatchdog); recording continues without audio",
   747	        description="Forced audio buffer stall: drop next 1000 capture buffers (well above stall threshold)",
   748	    )
   749	)
   750	def A5_forced_stall(**_) -> dict:
   751	    """User behavior: dictation in progress with audio flowing, audio
   752	    buffers stop arriving (BT codec switch, USB mic glitch, HAL hiccup).
   753	    The capture stall watchdog must fire, the pipeline must reach a
   754	    terminal state, and the next dictation must work.
   755	
   756	    Notes 2026-05-02:
   757	    - The watchdog measures gaps between buffer arrivals — capture has
   758	      to be warm (buffer cadence established) before injecting the stall,
   759	      otherwise the watchdog has no baseline to compare against.
   760	    - Without TTS audio, the test injects a stall against a silent
   761	      capture session — the watchdog logic still runs but the user-real
   762	      shape isn't exercised.
   763	    - Recovery: stall is recoverable in principle (user can re-record);
   764	      the second cycle confirms capture re-arms cleanly.
   765	    """
   766	    if not _start_recording_locked():
   767	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
   768	    with _TTSAudio():
   769	        time.sleep(0.5)  # warm-up: let buffer cadence establish
   770	        reply = force_stall(1000)
   771	        terminated = assert_terminated(timeout_s=12.0)
   772	    if "recording" in _active_state():
   773	        _stop_recording_locked()
   774	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers()
   775	    return {"reply": reply, **terminated, **recovery}

   778	_DEV_BUNDLE_ID = "com.enviouswispr.app.dev"
   779	_WRITING_STYLE_BUTTON_LABELS = {
   780	    "formal": "Formal — Professional tone, proper grammar",
   781	    "standard": "Standard — Clean up grammar and punctuation",
   782	    "friendly": "Friendly — Casual, conversational tone",
   783	}
   784	
   785	
   786	def _read_setting(key: str) -> Optional[str]:
   787	    """Read a UserDefaults value from the dev app's domain."""
   788	    try:
   789	        out = subprocess.check_output(
   790	            ["defaults", "read", _DEV_BUNDLE_ID, key],
   791	            text=True, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   792	        ).strip()
   793	        return out
   794	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
   795	        return None

   797	
   798	@scenario(
   799	    ScenarioMeta(
   800	        name="A6_settings_storm",
   801	        lane="A",
   802	        family="settings",
   803	        backends=["both"],
   804	        runtime_budget_seconds=30.0,
   805	        founder_required=False,
   806	        negative_control="Remove wordCorrectionEnabled live-sync from PipelineSettingsSync; setting takes no effect mid-record",
   807	        description="Toggle wordCorrectionEnabled / fillerRemovalEnabled / writingStylePreset during active recording",
   808	    )
   809	)
   810	def A6_settings_storm(**_) -> dict:
   811	    """User behavior: dictation in progress, user opens Settings and
   812	    flips heart-side toggles whose live-sync runs *during* an active
   813	    recording — `wordCorrectionEnabled` (Your Words tab) and
   814	    `fillerRemovalEnabled` (Transcription tab). These two flow through
   815	    `PipelineSettingsSync` and modify the streaming-time inline post-
   816	    process; the negative control on this scenario specifically names
   817	    `wordCorrectionEnabled live-sync from PipelineSettingsSync`.
   818	
   819	    Notes 2026-05-02:
   820	    - Toggles in the AI Polish tab (writing style, Deep reasoning) are
   821	      LIMBS — their copy explicitly says "Changes made during a recording
   822	      apply to the next recording." So they do not exercise the live-sync
   823	      hot path and are not what this scenario is for.
   824	    - `noiseSuppression` is the heaviest live-sync (cancels recording +
   825	      rebuilds engine), but its deliberate user-visible side effect is
   826	      cancelling the in-flight recording. That belongs in a separate
   827	      scenario, not the settings-storm-without-cancel claim here.
   828	    - Pre-state is captured before the storm and restored after, so
   829	      successive runs do not accumulate side effects on the user's
   830	      configured custom-words / filler-removal preferences.
   831	    """
   832	    eyes = _import_wispr_eyes()

   834	
   835	    # Capture pre-state so we can restore after the storm.
   836	    pre_word_correction = _read_setting("wordCorrectionEnabled")  # "0"/"1"/None
   837	    pre_filler_removal = _read_setting("fillerRemovalEnabled")

   847	        time.sleep(0.25)

   849	        time.sleep(0.25)
   850	        # Storm 2: flip fillerRemovalEnabled twice on the Transcription tab.

   854	        time.sleep(0.25)

   856	        time.sleep(0.25)
   857	        # Continue recording briefly after the storm so the streaming
   858	        # path sees both states with audio still flowing.
   859	        time.sleep(0.5)
   860	    # Stop recording (single tap exits hands-free lock).

   888	@scenario(
   889	    ScenarioMeta(
   890	        name="A7_app_quit",
   891	        lane="A",
   892	        family="app-quit",
   893	        backends=["both"],
   894	        runtime_budget_seconds=30.0,
   895	        founder_required=False,
   896	        negative_control="Remove applicationWillTerminate cleanup in AppDelegate; orphan helper processes survive next launch",
   897	        description="Cocoa quit mid-recording with audio flowing — no orphan helpers survive, app relaunches cleanly, next dictation works",
   898	    )
   899	)
   900	def A7_app_quit(**_) -> dict:
   901	    """User behavior: dictation in progress, user hits Cmd+Q. The app's
   902	    `applicationWillTerminate` must clean up audio + ASR helpers — no
   903	    orphan processes survive next launch. After relaunch the user must
   904	    be able to dictate again normally.
   905	
   906	    Notes 2026-05-02:
   907	    - Cocoa quit is the user-real path (osascript `quit`); SIGKILL
   908	      (`kill -9`) bypasses applicationWillTerminate and would not
   909	      exercise this scenario.
   910	    - TTS audio must be flowing so the quit happens against an active
   911	      capture session (helpers actively allocated + connected), not an
   912	      idle pipeline.
   913	    - After the quit, the harness relaunches the bundle with
   914	      EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1 so the recovery cycle can run end-to-end
   915	      through the normal dictation path.
   916	    """
   917	    pre_helpers = list_xpc_service_pids()
   918	    if not _start_recording_locked():
   919	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
   920	    with _TTSAudio():
   921	        time.sleep(0.8)  # warm-up: audio flowing into pipeline
   922	        # Cocoa quit — triggers applicationWillTerminate cleanup.
   923	        subprocess.run(
   924	            ["osascript", "-e",
   925	             'tell application id "com.enviouswispr.app.dev" to quit'],
   926	            check=False, capture_output=True,
   927	        )
   928	        exited_cleanly = _wait_for_app_exit(timeout_s=10.0)
   929	    # Give launchd a moment to reap helpers.
   930	    time.sleep(2.0)
   931	    post_helpers = list_xpc_service_pids()
   932	    # Negative-control assertion: no orphan helpers from the killed
   933	    # session. Helpers from before the test (pre_helpers) should be
   934	    # gone too — the app's lifecycle owns them.
   935	    orphans_remain = bool(post_helpers)
   936	    # Relaunch + recovery cycle.
   937	    relaunched = _relaunch_app()
   938	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers() if relaunched["relaunched"] else {
   939	        "recovered": False,
   940	        "reason": "endpoint did not come up after relaunch",
   941	    }
   942	    return {
   943	        "exited_cleanly": exited_cleanly,
   944	        "pre_helpers": pre_helpers,
   945	        "post_helpers": post_helpers,
   946	        "orphans_remain": orphans_remain,
   947	        **relaunched,
   948	        **recovery,
   949	    }

   994	@scenario(
   995	    ScenarioMeta(
   996	        name="A9_backend_switch_mid_record",
   997	        lane="A",
   998	        family="backend-switch",
   999	        backends=["both"],
  1000	        runtime_budget_seconds=2.0,
  1001	        founder_required=False,
  1002	        negative_control="Remove `if parakeetActive || whisperKitActive { break }` guard in PipelineSettingsSync; active recording aborted",
  1003	        description="Backend switch mid-record (settings UI change) — must be rejected, recording continues",
  1004	    )
  1005	)
  1006	def A9_backend_switch_mid_record(**_) -> dict:
  1007	    """User behavior: dictation in progress with audio flowing, user
  1008	    opens Settings → Transcription and taps the OTHER engine button.
  1009	    The PipelineSettingsSync guard at line 90 must drop the switch
  1010	    silently (logs 'Backend switch blocked'), the active recording
  1011	    must finalize on the original backend, and `query_state` must
  1012	    show the original backend throughout.
  1013	
  1014	    Notes 2026-05-02:
  1015	    - The UI text under the engine buttons says 'Changes made during
  1016	      a recording apply to the next recording.' That text is mildly
  1017	      misleading — the code (PipelineSettingsSync.swift:90 case) BLOCKS
  1018	      and DISCARDS the switch entirely; it does not buffer it for the
  1019	      next recording. UserDefaults `selectedBackend` does flip
  1020	      (SwiftUI binding writes through), but the asrManager never
  1021	      receives a `switchBackend(to:)` call.
  1022	    - The harness restores `selectedBackend` after the test by
  1023	      tapping the original engine button once recording has
  1024	      finalized, so successive runs do not leave UserDefaults in a
  1025	      lying state.
  1026	    """
  1027	    eyes = _import_wispr_eyes()
  1028	    eyes.connect()
  1029	
  1030	    # Pre-state: assert idle, capture starting backend.
  1031	    if "idle" not in _active_state():
  1032	        _stop_recording_locked(settle_s=2.0)
  1033	    pre_backend = _read_setting("selectedBackend") or "parakeet"
  1034	    target_button_for_switch_attempt = (
  1035	        "Multi-Language" if pre_backend.lower() == "parakeet" else "Fast (English)"
  1036	    )
  1037	    target_button_for_restore = (
  1038	        "Fast (English)" if pre_backend.lower() == "parakeet" else "Multi-Language"
  1039	    )
  1040	
  1041	    if not _start_recording_locked():
  1042	        return {"terminal": False, "reason": "could not enter recording", "state": query_state()}
  1043	
  1044	    pipeline_state_during = ""
  1045	    pipeline_state_after_settle = ""
  1046	    # Hold the switched state long enough for a human to actually
  1047	    # perceive the visual flip in the Transcription tab. Without this
  1048	    # dwell the buttons toggle and toggle back in <1s — the test passes
  1049	    # in the data but a watching reviewer can't visually confirm it.
  1050	    display_dwell_seconds = 3.0
  1051	    with _TTSAudio():
  1052	        time.sleep(0.5)  # let audio flow into the active backend
  1053	        eyes.nav("Transcription")
  1054	        time.sleep(0.4)
  1055	        # Attempt the switch — should be blocked by the guard.
  1056	        eyes.tap(target_button_for_switch_attempt)
  1057	        time.sleep(0.3)
  1058	        # Snapshot which PIPELINE is actually doing the work. The
  1059	        # `backend=` field in query_state mirrors UserDefaults, which
  1060	        # SwiftUI flips immediately. The truthful signal is which
  1061	        # pipeline reports a non-idle state.
  1062	        pipeline_state_during = query_state()
  1063	        # Dwell so the swap is visible to a human reviewer.
  1064	        time.sleep(display_dwell_seconds)
  1065	
  1066	    _stop_recording_locked(settle_s=2.0)
  1067	    pipeline_state_after_settle = query_state()
  1068	    terminated = assert_terminated(timeout_s=10.0)
  1069	
  1070	    # Restore: tap the original engine button so UserDefaults matches reality.
  1071	    eyes.nav("Transcription")
  1072	    time.sleep(0.4)
  1073	    if (_read_setting("selectedBackend") or "").lower() != pre_backend.lower():
  1074	        eyes.tap(target_button_for_restore)
  1075	        time.sleep(0.4)
  1076	
  1077	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers()
  1078	
  1079	    # The truthful "switch_was_blocked" signal: during the recording,
  1080	    # the ORIGINAL backend's pipeline state was non-idle, and the OTHER
  1081	    # backend's pipeline state was idle. (If the switch had succeeded,
  1082	    # the original would have torn down to idle and the other would
  1083	    # have started recording.)
  1084	    parsed_during = _parse_query_state(pipeline_state_during)
  1085	    pre_backend_key = "parakeet" if pre_backend.lower() == "parakeet" else "whisperkit"
  1086	    other_key = "whisperkit" if pre_backend_key == "parakeet" else "parakeet"
  1087	    pre_backend_active = parsed_during.get(pre_backend_key, "") not in {"", "idle"}
  1088	    other_idle = parsed_during.get(other_key, "") in {"idle", ""}
  1089	    switch_was_blocked = pre_backend_active and other_idle
  1090	
  1091	    return {
  1092	        **terminated,
  1093	        **recovery,
  1094	        "pre_backend": pre_backend,
  1095	        "pipeline_state_during": pipeline_state_during,
  1096	        "pipeline_state_after_settle": pipeline_state_after_settle,
  1097	        "switch_was_blocked": switch_was_blocked,
  1098	        "post_backend_userdefaults": _read_setting("selectedBackend"),
  1099	    }
  1100	
  1101

  1132	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
  1133	    if not argv or argv[0] in ("-h", "--help", "list", "list_scenarios"):
  1134	        print_scenarios()
  1135	        return 0
  1136	    cmd = argv[0]
  1137	    if cmd == "run" and len(argv) >= 2:
  1138	        result = run_scenario(argv[1])
  1139	        print(result)
  1140	        return 0
  1141	    if cmd == "query":
  1142	        print(query_state())
  1143	        return 0
  1144	    print(f"unknown: {cmd}", file=sys.stderr)
  1145	    return 2

     1	"""
     2	Run a Lane A gauntlet sequentially against the running debug bundle, with
     3	a between-scenario health check. If any scenario or its post-check leaves
     4	the app in a non-idle / non-recovering state, stop immediately and report
     5	which scenario was the last to run cleanly. Without this check a single
     6	bad scenario silently invalidates every subsequent "pass" because the app
     7	is already broken.
     8	
     9	Usage:
    10	    python3 Tests/RuntimeUAT/run_gauntlet.py --backend parakeet
    11	    python3 Tests/RuntimeUAT/run_gauntlet.py --backend whisperKit
    12	
    13	The bundle must already be running with EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1 set
    14	(via the wispr-rebuild-debug skill).
    15	"""
    16	from __future__ import annotations
    17	
    18	import argparse
    19	import sys
    20	import time
    21	from pathlib import Path
    22	
    23	HERE = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    24	if str(HERE) not in sys.path:
    25	    sys.path.insert(0, str(HERE))
    26	
    27	from faultInjection import (  # noqa: E402
    28	    _assert_dictation_recovers,
    29	    _parse_query_state,
    30	    list_xpc_service_pids,
    31	    query_state,
    32	    run_scenario,
    33	)
    34	
    35	
    36	PARAKEET_GAUNTLET = [
    37	    "A1_rapid_stop_start",
    38	    "A2_esc_cancel",
    39	    "A3_asr_xpc_kill",
    40	    "A4_audio_xpc_kill",
    41	    "A5_forced_stall",
    42	    "A6_settings_storm",
    43	    "A7_app_quit",
    44	    "A9_backend_switch_mid_record",
    45	]
    46	
    47	WHISPERKIT_GAUNTLET = [
    48	    "A1_rapid_stop_start",
    49	    "A2_esc_cancel",
    50	    # A3 (ASR XPC kill) is Parakeet-only — WhisperKit ASR is in-process.
    51	    "A4_audio_xpc_kill",
    52	    "A5_forced_stall",
    53	    "A6_settings_storm",
    54	    "A7_app_quit",
    55	    "A9_backend_switch_mid_record",
    56	]
    57	
    58	
    59	def health_check(backend_key: str) -> dict:
    60	    """After a scenario, verify the app is in a state where the next
    61	    scenario can meaningfully run.
    62	
    63	    Checks:
    64	      1. Active backend's pipeline state is idle/complete/ready (NOT
    65	         error — issue #555 means error states leave the overlay stuck).
    66	      2. XPC helper count is sane (1-3).
    67	      3. A recovery dictation cycle reaches a non-error terminal state.
    68	    """
    69	    parsed = _parse_query_state(query_state())
    70	    pipeline_state = parsed.get(backend_key, "")
    71	    helpers = list_xpc_service_pids()
    72	
    73	    state_ok = pipeline_state in {"idle", "complete", "ready"}
    74	    helpers_ok = 1 <= len(helpers) <= 3
    75	
    76	    if not state_ok:
    77	        return {
    78	            "healthy": False,
    79	            "reason": f"pipeline state {pipeline_state!r} not idle/complete/ready",
    80	            "pipeline_state": pipeline_state,
    81	            "helpers": helpers,
    82	        }
    83	    if not helpers_ok:
    84	        return {
    85	            "healthy": False,
    86	            "reason": f"helper count {len(helpers)} out of expected range",
    87	            "pipeline_state": pipeline_state,
    88	            "helpers": helpers,
    89	        }
    90	
    91	    recovery = _assert_dictation_recovers()
    92	    if not recovery.get("recovered"):
    93	        return {
    94	            "healthy": False,
    95	            "reason": "post-scenario recovery dictation failed",
    96	            "pipeline_state": pipeline_state,
    97	            "helpers": helpers,
    98	            "recovery": recovery,
    99	        }
   100	    return {
   101	        "healthy": True,
   102	        "pipeline_state": pipeline_state,
   103	        "helpers": helpers,
   104	        "recovery_terminal_state": recovery.get("second_cycle_terminal_state"),
   105	    }
   106	
   107	
   108	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
   109	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
   110	    parser.add_argument("--backend", choices=["parakeet", "whisperKit"],
   111	                        default="parakeet")
   112	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   113	
   114	    backend_key = "parakeet" if args.backend == "parakeet" else "whisperkit"
   115	    scenarios = (PARAKEET_GAUNTLET if args.backend == "parakeet"
   116	                 else WHISPERKIT_GAUNTLET)
   117	
   118	    print(f"=== Lane A gauntlet — {args.backend} ===")
   119	    print(f"pre-state: {query_state()}")
   120	
   121	    results = []
   122	    for i, scenario in enumerate(scenarios, 1):
   123	        print(f"\n--- [{i}/{len(scenarios)}] {scenario} ---")
   124	        t0 = time.monotonic()
   125	        try:
   126	            scenario_result = run_scenario(scenario)
   127	            elapsed = scenario_result.get("elapsed_seconds", time.monotonic() - t0)
   128	            inner = scenario_result.get("result", {})
   129	            print(f"  elapsed={elapsed:.1f}s")
   130	            print(f"  result={inner}")
   131	        except Exception as e:
   132	            print(f"  scenario raised: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
   133	            results.append({
   134	                "scenario": scenario, "passed": False,
   135	                "reason": "scenario raised exception", "exception": str(e),
   136	            })
   137	            print(f"\n!!! gauntlet aborted at {scenario} (raised exception) !!!")
   138	            break
   139	
   140	        print(f"  health check (active backend = {backend_key})...")
   141	        health = health_check(backend_key)
   142	        results.append({
   143	            "scenario": scenario,
   144	            "elapsed_s": elapsed,
   145	            "scenario_result": inner,
   146	            "health": health,
   147	        })
   148	        if not health["healthy"]:
   149	            print(f"  health FAIL: {health['reason']}")
   150	            print(f"  pipeline_state={health['pipeline_state']}, "
   151	                  f"helpers={health['helpers']}")
   152	            print(f"\n!!! gauntlet aborted at {scenario} — app no longer healthy !!!")
   153	            print(f"    {len(scenarios) - i} scenarios skipped: {scenarios[i:]}")
   154	            break
   155	        print(f"  health OK  pipeline={health['pipeline_state']}  "
   156	              f"helpers={len(health['helpers'])}  "
   157	              f"recovery={health['recovery_terminal_state']}")
   158	
   159	    print("\n=== Summary ===")
   160	    for r in results:
   161	        scen = r["scenario"]
   162	        if r.get("health", {}).get("healthy"):
   163	            print(f"  PASS  {scen}")
   164	        else:
   165	            reason = r.get("health", {}).get("reason") or r.get("reason") or "?"
   166	            print(f"  FAIL  {scen}  ({reason})")
   167	    print(f"\nfinal-state: {query_state()}")
   168	    return 0 if all(r.get("health", {}).get("healthy") for r in results) else 1
   169	
   170	
   171	if __name__ == "__main__":
   172	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

   121	Backends: both. Budget: 15s. Mechanism: bt-route.
   122	
   123	Founder physically toggles AirPods (or other BT input) power off/on during an active recording. Pipeline either continues with new route or terminates cleanly via the existing capture-session-interruption path. No pipeline lockup.
   124	
   125	**Founder action required:** the harness prompts `"Founder action: toggle AirPods power off, wait 2s, power on. Press Enter when done."` — physically toggle the device. Re-run with `founder_present=True`.
   126	
   127	**Optional Lane B' (programmatic device flip):** `AudioObjectSetPropertyData` on `kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultInputDevice` may exercise the same code path. Pending the spike documented in the V2 plan §3.2; if feasibility is confirmed, B1' joins Lane A and B1 becomes release-time-only.
   128	
   129	**Negative control:** remove the BT route handler in `AudioDeviceManager` / capture-session-interruption flow. Recording behaves incorrectly on real BT toggle (audio cuts out, pipeline stuck).
   130	
   131	## Wire protocol
   132	
   133	The DEBUG endpoint accepts text, line-delimited:
   134	
   135	```
   136	<token>\n
   137	<command>\n
   138	```
   139	
   140	Reply: `OK\n`, `OK <state>\n` (for `query_state`), or `ERR <reason>\n`.
   141	
   142	Token: per-launch random hex written by the app to `~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/fault-token-<pid>` with `0600` perms. The app deletes this file on `applicationWillTerminate`.
   143	
   144	Fixed command set (no arbitrary RPC):
   145	
   146	| Command | Effect |
   147	|---|---|
   148	| `force_stall(N)` | Drop next N captured buffers via `AudioCaptureProxy.forceStallRemainingBuffers` |
   149	| `force_cancel` | Invoke `forceCancelNow()` on the active backend's pipeline |
   150	| `force_xpc_kill` | Invalidate `ASRManagerProxy` connection mid-stream |
   151	| `force_audio_xpc_kill` | Invalidate `AudioCaptureProxy` connection mid-stream |
   152	| `query_state` | Return current pipeline + backend state (one line, no side effects) |
   153	
   154	## Adding a new scenario
   155	
   156	1. Add a `package`-access DEBUG seam on the type that owns the behavior. Gate everything in `#if DEBUG`.
   157	2. Add a command to the fixed set in `DebugFaultEndpoint.handle(request:)`.
   158	3. Add a `@scenario(...)`-decorated function in `faultInjection.py`. Document its negative control.
   159	4. Add a row to "Index by scenario name" above. Add an entry to "Index by symptom" if the scenario maps to a known production failure mode.
   160	5. Demonstrate red/green at PR time: revert the negative control, scenario fails. Restore, scenario passes.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	@preconcurrency import AVFoundation
     2	import EnviousWisprASR

     9	@testable import EnviousWisprPipeline
    10	
    11	/// V2 fault-injection — Lane C invariant C1 (issue #291).
    12	///
    13	/// Asserts that the per-session stall dedup in `HeartPathTelemetryEmitter` is
    14	/// truly per-session: a stall fires in session 1 (state flips to `.error`),
    15	/// and after `cancelRecording` + `startRecording`, a fresh stall in session 2
    16	/// fires again rather than getting suppressed by stale dedup state. Dedup
    17	/// must not leak across recordings.
    18	///
    19	/// Uses a `NeverFinishingAudioCapture` stub so the pipeline parks in
    20	/// `.recording` without racing against the audio path's auto-completion.
    21	/// Does NOT exercise the `AudioCaptureProxy.forceStallRemainingBuffers`
    22	/// proxy seam — that's Lane A scenario A5's responsibility.
    23	@MainActor
    24	@Suite("V2 Lane C — dedup state survives recording restart")
    25	struct DedupSurvivesStallTests {
    26	
    27	  @Test(
    28	    "stall fires in session 1, fires again in fresh session 2 — dedup is per-session, not global"
    29	  )
    30	  func testDedupSurvivesStallRestart() async throws {
    31	    let audioCapture = NeverFinishingAudioCapture()
    32	    let asrManager = NoOpASRManagerV2()
    33	
    34	    let pipeline = TranscriptionPipeline(
    35	      audioCapture: audioCapture,
    36	      asrManager: asrManager,
    37	      transcriptStore: TranscriptStore()
    38	    )
    39	
    40	    let config = DictationSessionConfig.testDefault(
    41	      autoPasteToActiveApp: false,
    42	      vadSensitivity: 0.5,
    43	      languageMode: .auto,
    44	      llmProvider: .openAI,
    45	      llmModel: "gpt-test"
    46	    )
    47	
    48	    // ─── Session 1 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    49	    await pipeline.startRecording(config: config)
    50	    let reachedRecording1 = await pollUntil(timeout: .seconds(1)) {
    51	      pipeline.state == .recording
    52	    }
    53	    #expect(reachedRecording1, "session 1 must reach .recording")
    54	
    55	    // First stall in session 1 — emitter fires (no prior dedup), pipeline
    56	    // guard advances state to .error.
    57	    pipeline.handleCaptureStall(Self.stallContext(sessionID: 1))
    58	    #expect(
    59	      pipeline.state == .error("No audio detected — try again."),
    60	      "first stall in session 1 must flip state to .error")
    61	
    62	    // Reset to idle so we can start session 2. `cancelRecording()` is a
    63	    // no-op once the pipeline is in `.error` (its guard checks for
    64	    // `.recording` / `.loadingModel`); `reset()` is the path that takes the
    65	    // pipeline back to `.idle` from any state.
    66	    pipeline.reset()
    67	    #expect(pipeline.state == .idle, "reset() must return to .idle")
    68	
    69	    // ─── Session 2 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    70	    await pipeline.startRecording(config: config)
    71	    let reachedRecording2 = await pollUntil(timeout: .seconds(1)) {
    72	      pipeline.state == .recording
    73	    }
    74	    #expect(reachedRecording2, "session 2 must reach .recording — dedup must not block restart")
    75	
    76	    // Stall in session 2 (fresh sessionID). Emitter must NOT consider this
    77	    // deduped from session 1's prior fire — the pipeline guard must advance
    78	    // to .error. If dedup state had leaked across recordings, state would
    79	    // remain .recording and this test would fail.
    80	    pipeline.handleCaptureStall(Self.stallContext(sessionID: 2))
    81	    #expect(
    82	      pipeline.state == .error("No audio detected — try again."),
    83	      "stall in fresh session 2 must fire — dedup state must not survive restart")
    84	
    85	    await pipeline.cancelRecording()
    86	  }
    87	
    88	  // MARK: - Helpers
    89	
    90	  private static func stallContext(sessionID: UInt64) -> CaptureStallContext {
    91	    CaptureStallContext(
    92	      sessionID: sessionID,
    93	      armedAtUptimeNs: 1_000,
    94	      firedAtUptimeNs: 2_000,
    95	      route: "built_in_mic",
    96	      sourceType: "av_audio_engine",
    97	      engineStartedSuccessfully: true,
    98	      tapInstalled: true,
    99	      formatMismatchObserved: false,
   100	      inputDeviceUIDPreferred: nil,
   101	      inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault: nil
   102	    )
   103	  }
   104	}
   105	
   106	@MainActor
   107	private func pollUntil(
   108	  timeout: Duration,
   109	  interval: Duration = .milliseconds(10),
   110	  condition: @escaping @MainActor () -> Bool
   111	) async -> Bool {
   112	  let deadline = ContinuousClock.now + timeout
   113	  while ContinuousClock.now < deadline {
   114	    if condition() { return true }
   115	    try? await Task.sleep(for: interval)
   116	  }
   117	  return condition()
   118	}
   119	
   120	// MARK: - Test stubs
   121	
   122	/// `AudioCaptureInterface` stub that never delivers buffers and never
   123	/// finishes its capture stream — the pipeline parks in `.recording` so we
   124	/// can fire `handleCaptureStall` deterministically without racing the
   125	/// audio path's auto-completion. Architecture rule "duplication is allowed
   126	/// when it protects independence" — V2 tests stay decoupled from
   127	/// `HeartPathTelemetryWiringTests`'s private stubs.
   128	@MainActor
   129	private final class NeverFinishingAudioCapture: AudioCaptureInterface {
   130	  var isCapturing: Bool = false
   131	  var audioLevel: Float = 0
   132	  var capturedSamples: [Float] = []
   133	  var currentAudioRoute: String = "synthetic-fixture"
   134	  var onBufferCaptured: (@Sendable (AVAudioPCMBuffer) -> Void)?
   135	  var onEngineInterrupted: (() -> Void)?
   136	  var onVADAutoStop: (() -> Void)?
   137	  var onCaptureStalled: ((CaptureStallContext) -> Void)?
   138	  var onCaptureSessionInterruption: ((CaptureSessionInterruptionContext) -> Void)?
   139	  var onXPCServiceError: ((XPCErrorContext) -> Void)?
   140	  var onXPCReplyFailed: ((XPCReplyFailureContext) -> Void)?
   141	  var onRouteResolved: ((CaptureRouteDecision, _ sourceTypeChanged: Bool) -> Void)?
   142	  var currentCaptureSessionID: UInt64 = 0
   143	  var isActivelyCapturing: Bool = false
   144	  var captureSourceType: String = "fixture_mock"
   145	  var noiseSuppressionEnabled: Bool = false
   146	  var selectedInputDeviceUID: String = ""
   147	  var preferredInputDeviceIDOverride: String = ""
   148	  var warmEnginePolicy: WarmEnginePolicy = .off
   149	
   150	  /// Hold the continuation indefinitely so the AsyncStream never completes.
   151	  /// The pipeline's read loop stays parked, leaving state at `.recording`.
   152	  private var continuation: AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer>.Continuation?
   153	
   154	  func startEnginePhase() async throws {}
   155	  func beginCapturePhase() async throws -> AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> {
   156	    isCapturing = true
   157	    isActivelyCapturing = true
   158	    currentCaptureSessionID += 1
   159	    return AsyncStream { cont in
   160	      self.continuation = cont
   161	      // Intentionally never yield, never finish.
   162	    }
   163	  }
   164	  func startCapture() async throws -> AsyncStream<AVAudioPCMBuffer> {
   165	    try await startEnginePhase()
   166	    return try await beginCapturePhase()
   167	  }
   168	  func stopCapture() async -> CaptureResult {
   169	    continuation?.finish()
   170	    continuation = nil
   171	    isCapturing = false
   172	    isActivelyCapturing = false
   173	    return CaptureResult(samples: [])
   174	  }
   175	  func rebuildEngine() {}
   176	  func buildEngine(noiseSuppression: Bool) {}
   177	  func preWarm() async throws {}
   178	  func abortPreWarm() {
   179	    continuation?.finish()
   180	    continuation = nil
   181	    isCapturing = false
   182	    isActivelyCapturing = false
   183	  }
   184	  func waitForFormatStabilization(maxWait: TimeInterval, pollInterval: TimeInterval) async -> Bool {
   185	    true
   186	  }
   187	  func configureVAD(autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool) {}
   188	  func getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: Int) async -> (samples: [Float], totalCount: Int) { ([], 0) }
   189	  func getVADSegments() async -> [SpeechSegment] { [] }
   190	}
