.claude/knowledge/archived-memories/wisprflow-competitive-intel-wisprflow-v1-4-517-electron.md:5:wisprflow-competitive-intel WisprFlow v1.4.517: Electron 39.5.2 + native Swift helper sidecar (NOT XPC). ASR 100% server-side proprietary (no on-device). Swift helper handles accessibility/paste via localhost HTTP port 8969. ~437MB bundle, ~800MB RAM. LLM polish: fine-tuned Llama on Baseten TensorRT-LLM, <200ms. Custom words: ASR-level boosting server-side + post-ASR string replacement. Full competitive analysis at docs/feature-requests/smart-polish-v2.md. BT strategy: defaults to built-in mic (avoids HFP), but manages HFP when BT mic forced via codec detect + 500ms wait + mute before record + A2DP recovery after. Our gap: we don't default to built-in and don't recover A2DP cleanly after recording stops.

.claude/knowledge/archive/beads-export-2026-04-05/beads-full-export.jsonl:68:{"id":"ew-8y3","title":"XPC audio isolation: move AVAudioEngine to separate process","description":"Move audio capture (AVAudioEngine, VP, device management) to a separate XPC service process. CoreAudio's BT device management corrupts memory in the main process — manifests as EXC_BAD_ACCESS (0x1e) in unrelated SwiftUI/event callbacks. Wispr Flow solves this with multi-process Electron architecture. Our monolith needs the same isolation. Scope: (1) XPC service with AVAudioEngine + capture, (2) main app communicates via XPC protocol for start/stop/samples, (3) if XPC crashes, main app stays alive and can restart the service. This is the architectural fix — the short-term setInputDevice skip (4774ad1) only prevents recording-time crashes, not BT hot-swap crashes.","notes":"Step 6b COMPLETE (2026-03-16). AVCaptureSession capture backend solves BT audio degradation. When BT headphones connected as output, CaptureRouteResolver selects AVCaptureSessionSource which captures from built-in mic via AVCaptureSession — no A2DP→SCO switch, no aggregate device crash. Tested: music stays clear during recording, transcription works, 0.8s pipeline. Non-BT path uses AVAudioEngineSource (extracted from AudioCaptureManager, supports VP). AudioCaptureManager reduced from 843→247 lines. New files: AudioInputSource.swift, CaptureRouteResolver.swift, AVAudioEngineSource.swift, AVCaptureSessionSource.swift. 6 commits across Steps 6b.1-6b.4. docs/launch-assets/reference/speed-and-heart-path.md:35:**Why it matters.** Cold-starting the macOS audio engine takes ~100ms for the built-in mic and longer for Bluetooth. That's the dead air at the start of your dictation where the first word gets lost. Because we keep the engine warm between recordings, your next hotkey press captures audio *instantly*, and a 500ms ring buffer means we've even been listening to the ambient silence before you pressed the key.
docs/launch-assets/reference/speed-and-heart-path.md:120:3. **Bet on heart-and-limbs architecture.** We accept that LLMs fail, networks drop, and Bluetooth headphones misbehave. The heart path has defensive fallbacks so the user never loses a dictation.

.claude/skills/wispr-incident-response/SKILL.md:67:### Bluetooth codec switch or device dropout
.claude/skills/wispr-incident-response/SKILL.md:69:Symptoms: audio drops mid-recording, device vanishes, AirPods codec flip, "audio stopped working".
.claude/skills/wispr-incident-response/SKILL.md:71:- Go to `incident-response.md` → **Bluetooth Codec Switch / Device Dropout**.
.claude/knowledge/architecture.md:294:  - **BT output active** → `AVCaptureSessionSource` captures from built-in mic via `AVCaptureSession`. No A2DP→SCO codec switch — AVCaptureDevice on macOS never returns BT devices. Framework handles 16kHz mono Float32 conversion via `audioSettings`. Noise suppression unavailable (VP requires AVAudioEngine to own input).
.claude/knowledge/gotchas.md:177:`AVAudioEngine` auto-stops when the audio device is disconnected (e.g., USB mic unplugged), but it does **not** clean up internal state. On `AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange` notification, first check `kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceIsAlive`. **Alive** = graceful recovery via `recoverFromCodecSwitch()` (reconfigure engine in-place, e.g., BT A2DP→SCO codec switch). **Dead** = full teardown via `emergencyTeardown()` (stop engine, remove all taps, `engine.reset()`, reconstruct from scratch). `onEngineInterrupted` callback propagates the event to `TranscriptionPipeline` to cancel in-progress work and transition to error state.

./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-1-4.json:47:              "content": "<p>Push-to-Talk (PTT) is the default recording mode. Hold your hotkey to record, release it to stop and transcribe.</p><h3>How it works</h3><ol><li>Press and hold your designated hotkey.</li><li>Speak naturally while holding the key.</li><li>Release the key when you are done speaking.</li><li>EnviousWispr transcribes your speech, applies any enabled processing (filler removal, custom words, AI polish), and pastes the result.</li></ol><h3>Pre-warm on key-down</h3><p>When you press the hotkey, EnviousWispr immediately begins pre-warming the audio input. If you are using a Bluetooth device, this triggers the codec switch so the microphone is ready by the time you start speaking. This keeps perceived latency sub-second.</p><h3>Hands-free extension</h3><p>While in Push-to-Talk mode, you can <strong>double-press</strong> the hotkey to lock the recording into hands-free mode. This lets you release the key and continue speaking for longer dictations. See the <em>Hands-Free Mode</em> article for details.</p><h3>Setting</h3><p>Open the settings window, go to the <strong>Shortcuts</strong> tab, and select <strong>Push-to-Talk</strong> as the recording mode.</p>"
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-5-9.json:62:              "content": "<h2>Choosing Your Microphone</h2><p>EnviousWispr supports two modes for audio input device selection, configurable in <strong>Settings &gt; Microphone</strong>.</p><h3>Auto Mode</h3><p>In Auto mode, EnviousWispr uses smart mic selection to pick the best available input device. This is the default and works well for most setups. The app handles device changes (plugging in a USB mic, connecting Bluetooth headphones) automatically.</p><h3>Manual Selection</h3><p>If you prefer to lock to a specific microphone, switch to manual mode and choose your device from the list. EnviousWispr will always use that device regardless of other inputs that become available.</p><h3>Device Disconnect Recovery</h3><p>If your selected microphone is disconnected during a recording (USB unplugged, Bluetooth drops), EnviousWispr detects this via CoreAudio device-alive checks and performs an emergency teardown to salvage any audio captured so far. The partial recording is transcribed rather than discarded.</p>"
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-5-9.json:65:              "title": "Bluetooth and AirPods",
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-5-9.json:66:              "description": "How EnviousWispr handles Bluetooth audio routing and codec switching.",
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-5-9.json:67:              "content": "<h2>Bluetooth and AirPods</h2><p>Bluetooth audio devices require special handling because of codec switching. When a Bluetooth device (like AirPods) switches from the high-quality playback codec (A2DP) to the bidirectional voice codec (SCO/HFP), there is a brief audio disruption. EnviousWispr has Bluetooth-aware routing to handle this gracefully.</p><h3>Pre-warm on PTT Key-Down</h3><p>When you press the push-to-talk key, EnviousWispr immediately fires a pre-warm call that triggers the Bluetooth codec switch. The actual recording start waits for format stabilization (up to 1.5 seconds, polling every 200ms) so the codec switch has time to settle before audio capture begins.</p><h3>Warm Engine Between Recordings</h3><p>After a recording ends, the audio engine stays running (in pre-roll mode) for a configurable timeout. This means the Bluetooth codec stays in voice mode, and your next recording starts instantly without waiting for a codec switch. The warm engine timeout is configurable in Settings under Microphone (options: Off, 10s, 30s, 60s, Always; default is 30s).</p><h3>Route Change Detection</h3><p>If a Bluetooth device connects or disconnects between recordings while the engine is warm, EnviousWispr detects the route change, rebuilds the audio source, and starts fresh with the correct device.</p>"
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-5-9.json:77:              "content": "<h2>Noise Suppression</h2><p>EnviousWispr offers Apple Voice Processing noise suppression to produce cleaner audio input, especially useful in noisy environments.</p><h3>How It Works</h3><p>When enabled, the AVAudioEngine is configured with Apple's Voice Processing I/O audio unit, which applies noise suppression at the system level before audio reaches the speech recognition model.</p><h3>Configuration</h3><p>Noise suppression can be toggled in <strong>Settings &gt; Microphone</strong>.</p><h3>Bluetooth Limitation</h3><p>Noise suppression is unavailable when Bluetooth audio output is active (e.g., AirPods). When Bluetooth output is detected, EnviousWispr routes audio capture through a different path that does not support Voice Processing. This is a system-level constraint, not a bug. If you need noise suppression, use your Mac's built-in speakers for output or a wired headset.</p><h3>Default State</h3><p>Noise suppression is off by default.</p><h3>Important Note</h3><p>Changing the noise suppression setting requires rebuilding the audio engine internally. EnviousWispr handles this automatically, but the change takes effect on the next recording, not mid-recording.</p><h3>Troubleshooting</h3><ul><li><strong>Noise suppression has no effect with Bluetooth headphones:</strong> This is expected. Noise suppression requires the standard audio engine path, which is not used when Bluetooth output is active. See the Bluetooth and AirPods article for details.</li><li><strong>Toggle does not seem to change anything:</strong> The change applies on your next recording, not the current one.</li></ul>"
.claude/knowledge/competitor-fazm.md:51:> Uses CoreAudio IOProc directly on the default input device to avoid AVAudioEngine's implicit aggregate device creation, which degrades system audio output quality (especially Bluetooth A2DP → SCO switch).
.claude/knowledge/competitor-fazm.md:178:| 2 | (new) | Virtual/aggregate device avoidance in `AudioDeviceManager`: reject Wispr Flow / BlackHole / Loopback as default input, fall back to ranked physical device. Port from `Desktop/Sources/AudioCaptureService.swift:400-454`. | SMALL | Low | Prevents mysterious "no audio captured" when user has Wispr Flow or BlackHole installed. |
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-10-13.json:42:          "name": "Audio and Bluetooth",
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-10-13.json:45:              "title": "Bluetooth Audio Issues",
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-10-13.json:46:              "description": "How EnviousWispr handles Bluetooth headsets and codec switching.",
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-10-13.json:47:              "content": "<h2>The Bluetooth Codec Problem</h2><p>When a Bluetooth headset (like AirPods) is used for audio output, macOS switches the codec from A2DP (high-quality playback) to SCO (lower-quality bidirectional) when an app requests microphone input. This codec switch causes a brief audio disruption and can degrade recording quality.</p><h2>Automatic BT-Aware Routing</h2><p>EnviousWispr has built-in Bluetooth-aware audio routing via the <strong>Bluetooth-aware audio router</strong>. When it detects that a Bluetooth device is active for audio output:</p><ul><li>It automatically routes audio capture to your Mac's <strong>built-in microphone</strong> instead of the Bluetooth device.</li><li>This avoids the A2DP-to-SCO codec switch entirely.</li><li>Your Bluetooth headset continues playing audio without interruption.</li></ul><h2>Pre-Warm on Push-to-Talk</h2><p>When using Push-to-Talk mode, pressing the hotkey triggers a pre-warm phase that resolves the audio route and settles any codec switching before capture begins. This happens in parallel with other setup tasks.</p><h2>Device Disconnect During Recording</h2><p>If a Bluetooth device disconnects during recording, EnviousWispr detects this via CoreAudio device property monitoring. If the device is still alive (codec switch), it recovers gracefully in place. If the device is gone (disconnect), it performs an emergency teardown and salvages any audio captured so far.</p>"
./workers/crisp-kb/articles/categories-10-13.json:117:              "content": "<h2>Warm Engine Policy</h2><p>The warm engine keeps the audio engine and microphone tap running between recordings so the next recording starts instantly. You can configure this in <strong>Settings &gt; Microphone</strong>.</p><p>Available policies:</p><ul><li><strong>Off:</strong> Engine shuts down immediately after each recording. Lowest memory usage, but each recording has a cold start.</li><li><strong>10 seconds:</strong> Engine stays warm for 10 seconds after recording stops.</li><li><strong>30 seconds (default):</strong> Good balance between responsiveness and resource usage.</li><li><strong>60 seconds:</strong> Longer warm window for frequent dictators.</li><li><strong>Always:</strong> Engine never shuts down. Fastest possible start time, highest idle resource usage.</li></ul><h2>Pre-Warm on Push-to-Talk Key-Down</h2><p>When using Push-to-Talk mode, pressing the hotkey triggers two parallel actions: (1) the audio input is pre-warmed (audio engine started, Bluetooth codec settled), and (2) recording setup begins. By the time you start speaking, the engine is ready and the 500ms pre-roll ring buffer has been draining, capturing those critical first milliseconds of audio.</p><h2>Model Unload Policy</h2><p>The ASR model can be unloaded from memory after a period of inactivity. This is separate from the warm engine (which controls audio hardware). Available policies include: never unload, immediately, 2 minutes, 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 15 minutes, and 60 minutes. Keeping the model loaded means zero cold-start latency on the next dictation but uses more memory.</p><h2>Background Model Pre-Loading</h2><p>On launch, EnviousWispr pre-loads the ASR model in the background so your first dictation has zero cold-start delay. The model download includes progress tracking and SHA-256 checksum verification.</p>"

./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/02-system-requirements.md:5:* **Microphone:** Built-in microphone or any external mic. AirPods and Bluetooth headsets are supported with automatic audio routing.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/48-bluetooth-and-airpods.md:1:Bluetooth audio devices require special handling because of codec switching. When a Bluetooth device (like AirPods) switches from the high-quality playback codec (A2DP) to the bidirectional voice codec (SCO/HFP), there is a brief audio disruption. EnviousWispr has Bluetooth-aware routing to handle this gracefully.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/48-bluetooth-and-airpods.md:5:When you press the push-to-talk key, EnviousWispr immediately fires a pre-warm call that triggers the Bluetooth codec switch. The actual recording start waits for format stabilization (up to 1.5 seconds, polling every 200ms) so the codec switch has time to settle before audio capture begins.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/48-bluetooth-and-airpods.md:9:After a recording ends, the audio engine stays running (in pre-roll mode) for a configurable timeout. This means the Bluetooth codec stays in voice mode, and your next recording starts instantly without waiting for a codec switch. The warm engine timeout is configurable in Settings under Microphone (options: Off, 10s, 30s, 60s, Always; default is 30s).
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/48-bluetooth-and-airpods.md:13:If a Bluetooth device connects or disconnects between recordings while the engine is warm, EnviousWispr detects the route change, rebuilds the audio source, and starts fresh with the correct device.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/49-noise-suppression.md:11:### Bluetooth Limitation
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/49-noise-suppression.md:13:Noise suppression is unavailable when Bluetooth audio output is active (e.g., AirPods). When Bluetooth output is detected, EnviousWispr routes audio capture through a different path that does not support Voice Processing. This is a system-level constraint, not a bug. If you need noise suppression, use your Mac's built-in speakers for output or a wired headset.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/49-noise-suppression.md:25:* **Noise suppression has no effect with Bluetooth headphones:** This is expected. Noise suppression requires the standard audio engine path, which is not used when Bluetooth output is active. See the Bluetooth and AirPods article for details.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/06-push-to-talk-mode.md:12:When you press the hotkey, EnviousWispr immediately begins pre-warming the audio input. If you are using a Bluetooth device, this triggers the codec switch so the microphone is ready by the time you start speaking. This keeps perceived latency sub-second.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:1:When a Bluetooth headset (like AirPods) is used for audio output, macOS switches the codec from A2DP (high-quality playback) to SCO (lower-quality bidirectional) when an app requests microphone input. This codec switch causes a brief audio disruption and can degrade recording quality.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:5:EnviousWispr has built-in Bluetooth-aware audio routing via the **Bluetooth-aware audio router**. When it detects that a Bluetooth device is active for audio output:
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:7:* It automatically routes audio capture to your Mac's **built-in microphone** instead of the Bluetooth device.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:8:* This avoids the A2DP-to-SCO codec switch entirely.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:9:* Your Bluetooth headset continues playing audio without interruption.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/28-bluetooth-audio-issues.md:17:If a Bluetooth device disconnects during recording, EnviousWispr detects this via CoreAudio device property monitoring. If the device is still alive (codec switch), it recovers gracefully in place. If the device is gone (disconnect), it performs an emergency teardown and salvages any audio captured so far.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/47-choosing-your-microphone.md:5:In Auto mode, EnviousWispr uses smart mic selection to pick the best available input device. This is the default and works well for most setups. The app handles device changes (plugging in a USB mic, connecting Bluetooth headphones) automatically.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/47-choosing-your-microphone.md:13:If your selected microphone is disconnected during a recording (USB unplugged, Bluetooth drops), EnviousWispr detects this via CoreAudio device-alive checks and performs an emergency teardown to salvage any audio captured so far. The partial recording is transcribed rather than discarded.
./workers/crisp-kb/markdown/36-warm-engine-and-performance-tuning.md:13:When using Push-to-Talk mode, pressing the hotkey triggers two parallel actions: (1) the audio input is pre-warmed (audio engine started, Bluetooth codec settled), and (2) recording setup begins. By the time you start speaking, the engine is ready and the 500ms pre-roll ring buffer has been draining, capturing those critical first milliseconds of audio.

This is the default and works well for most setups. The app handles device changes (plugging in a USB mic, connecting Bluetooth headphones) automatically.</p><h3>Manual Selection</h3><p>If you prefer to lock to a specific microphone, switch to manual mode and choose your device from the list. EnviousWispr will always use that device regardless of other inputs that become available.</p><h3>Device Disconnect Recovery</h3><p>If your selected microphone is disconnected during a recording (USB unplugged, Bluetooth drops), EnviousWispr detects this via CoreAudio device-alive checks and performs an emergency teardown to salvage any audio captured so far. docs/feature-requests/issue-319-2026-04-18-q2-hardening-epic.md:2915:- **2026-05-02 v1.30 · #553 closed as not-a-bug + #559 filed for V2 harness fix — real-world testing disproved V2's wedge claim** — Real-world test session 2026-05-02 across 7 distinct interruption shapes (BT-output connect mid-recording, Zoom mic-grab on join, Zoom mic-release on end, Zoom mute toggling × multiple, Spotify playback start, Discord voice channel join with concurrent EW capture, Discord disconnect with BT codec switch HFP→A2DP) on Bose QC headphones, fresh-launched build, M4 Pro / macOS 26. **Production heart path recovered cleanly in every scenario.** Six of seven scenarios fired ZERO `onEngineInterrupted` / `wasInterrupted` / `ConfigurationChange` / btCrash log entries; the seventh (BT-output connect) surfaced a cosmetic overlay error but next dictation worked at 1.080s pipeline. The Discord-disconnect test is the strongest negative-control: a real Bluetooth codec renegotiation traversed an active 13.25s recording with 91.1% VAD voiced and zero log signal. **Sentry production-only events API (`environment=production`, 14d):** `Audio engine interrupted` (ENVIOUSWISPR-A) fires 0× in window — the issue's lifetime count of 3/1-user has lastSeen 2026-04-07, outside 14d; `Audio XPC interrupt (capturing=true)` (ENVIOUSWISPR-N) fires 1×; no `asr_empty_result` or `audio_capture_failed` follow-up clusters within minutes of either, meaning even when the rare interruption fires, recovery succeeds. **Diagnosis:** V2's `force_stall(1000)` injects via `AudioServiceHandler` XPC bridge directly, bypassing `AVAudioEngineSource.handleEngineConfigurationChange()` — the production recovery routine that re-arms the engine on real interruption. Real-world interruptions arrive via `.AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange` notification (`AVAudioEngineSource.swift:250–261`) or `AVCaptureSession.wasInterruptedNotification` (`AVCaptureSessionSource.swift:360–384`); V2 skips both. **#553 closed not-planned with full test matrix in close-out comment.** **#559 filed** — V2 harness must replace direct XPC stall injection with `NotificationCenter.post(.AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange)` and/or simulated `wasInterruptedNotification` to produce realistic results. After harness fix, re-run V2 Lane A on A5 + A6/A7/A9 (which were SKIPPED only because A5 wedged the session ahead of them); also re-evaluate **#555** (overlay stuck on error terminal) and **#556** (WhisperKit LID cancelled by Settings tap mid post-recording) against real-world repro before treating either as production defects. §6.1 V2 row updated PLANNED → PARTIAL with #559 dependency note. **Pivot direction:** ENVIOUSWISPR-B `asr_empty_result` is the actually-impactful real-user heart-path issue (7 production events / 5 distinct users / 14d window, last seen 2026-04-30) — different code path entirely (ASR returns empty despite VAD detecting speech), worth Gate 0 on next session.
docs/feature-requests/issue-319-2026-04-18-q2-hardening-epic.md:2919:- **2026-04-30 v1.28 · V2 plan finalized + ready for Gate 2 sign-off** — Plan at `docs/feature-requests/issue-291-2026-04-30-v2-fault-injection-toolkit.md`. Three-lane fault-injection toolkit: Lane A (9 wispr_eyes-driven runtime scenarios for capture stall, cancel, XPC kill, settings storms, app quit, model-load cancel, backend-switch guard, rapid-toggle fuzz), Lane B (1 founder-required Bluetooth scenario, optional B1' programmatic device-flip if 30-min spike confirms feasibility), Lane C (4 Swift `@Test` invariants on actual owners — 2 on pipeline, 1 on PipelineSettingsSync, 1 on HotkeyService). DEBUG-only seams (`internal + #if DEBUG + @testable import`) on `AudioCaptureProxy`, `ASRManagerProxy`, both pipelines. DEBUG-only localhost endpoint (`DebugFaultEndpoint` type retained by `AppDelegate`, env-gated `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1`, per-launch token at `~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/fault-token-<pid>` with `0600` perms + atomic write). **Policy:** Lane A/B on-demand only (no CI, no nightly); Lane C runs on PR CI as standard Swift tests. **Self-test discipline:** per-mechanism red/green at PR demonstration time, NOT per-scenario regression+revert busywork ongoing. **Two rounds of Codex grounded review** (`docs/audits/2026-04-30-v2-grounded-review.txt` + `docs/audits/2026-04-30-v2-grounded-review-round2.txt`) + council from GPT 5.5 + Gemini 3.1 Pro. Round 1 absorbed 5 PIVOTs: A5 dropped (WhisperKit ASR is in-process), stall injection moved from pipelines → AudioCaptureProxy (capture-side concern, WhisperKit batch has no chunk handler), `package` → `internal+@testable` (SPM exposes `package` to app target), §10 file paths corrected throughout, Lane C reduced from 6 to 4 invariants on actual owners. Round 2 absorbed 3 precision revisions: C1 mechanism scope clarified (pipeline fixture covers pipeline dedup, NOT proxy seam — those are separate test surfaces), DEBUG endpoint owner pivoted from `EnviousWisprApp.swift` → small `DebugFaultEndpoint` type retained by AppDelegate, A7 narrowed to Cocoa terminate (no SIGTERM handler in code), A8 split into A8a Parakeet (true cancel propagation) + A8b WhisperKit (state-unwind only — no held task to cancel). Open Q1 fault-trigger mechanism: DEBUG-only localhost endpoint with tight gating selected (UserDefaults polling + DistributedNotificationCenter rejected). Two new scenarios added: A7 app-quit, A8 cancel-during-model-load. Out of V2 scope (filed as follow-ups): paste cascade fault injection (NG7 — heart-path-adjacent paste_failed cluster from V1a), fake `WhisperKitBackend` for testing (NG10 — needed for true zombie recovery test), V4 prompt adversarial (separate Track 2 phase). Tier LARGE confirmed (~1175 LOC across `Sources/{EnviousWisprAudio,EnviousWisprASR,EnviousWisprPipeline,EnviousWispr/App}` + `Tests/EnviousWisprTests/{Pipeline,App,Services}/V2/` + `Tests/UITests/`). Bible §6.1 V2 row stays PLANNED until V2 PR ships. Build deferred to next session per session-behavior §6 (one-release-per-session discipline + cognitive-mode-shift between planning and building).

     2	# Session Log
     3	
     4	## 2026-05-02 (Saturday morning) — #553 closed as harness artifact via real-world BT/Zoom/Discord testing; #559 filed for V2 fault-injector fix; pivot direction = ENVIOUSWISPR-B asr_empty_result
     5	
     6	Resumed from yesterday's V2 Lane A bugs queue (#553 / #555 / #556). Founder asked the load-bearing question before plan/build: **"how would a real user trigger this bug?"** — surfaced trigger paths from `AVAudioEngineSource.swift:250–261` (`AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange`) and `AVCaptureSessionSource.swift:360–384` (`wasInterruptedNotification`), then pivoted from planning-the-fix to testing-the-premise on the running app.
     7	
     8	**Real-world test session, Bose QC headphones, fresh-launched build, M4 Pro / macOS 26:**
     9	
    10	| Trigger | Recording | VAD voiced | Pipeline | Interrupt events |
    11	|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
    12	| BT-output connect mid-recording | spans connect | n/a | 1.080s next | 1× cosmetic overlay only |
    13	| Zoom mic-grab on join | 29.55s | 97.7% | 1.411s | 0 |
    14	| Zoom mic-release on end-call | 23.67s | 93.9% | 1.244s | 0 |
    15	| Zoom mute toggling × multiple | 20.82s | 96.8% | 1.393s | 0 |
    16	| Spotify playback start | spanned in 34.62s | 96.4% | 1.371s | 0 |
    17	| Discord voice channel concurrent capture | 6.69s | 86.2% | 0.980s | 0 |
    18	| Discord disconnect + BT codec switch HFP→A2DP | 13.25s | 91.1% | 1.144s | 0 |
    19	
    20	The Discord-disconnect test is the strongest negative-control: a real Bluetooth codec renegotiation (call-quality → music-quality) traversed an active recording with zero log signal anywhere. Production code path is dramatically more resilient than V2's synthetic test claimed.
    21	
    22	**Sentry production-only events API (`environment=production`, 14d), the load-bearing nuance the founder flagged mid-session ("look at dev vs production"):**
    23	
    24	- `Audio engine interrupted` (ENVIOUSWISPR-A): **0 events in 14d.** Issue lifetime count of 3 / 1 user has lastSeen 2026-04-07, outside the window. Issue-API `count` is lifetime; events-API with explicit env filter is the correct 14d signal — distinction matters because the issues-API number alone overstates real-user impact.
    25	- `Audio XPC interrupt (capturing=true)` (ENVIOUSWISPR-N): 1 event in 14d.
    26	- `asr_empty_result` (production-filtered events): 7 events / 5 distinct users / Apr 20 → Apr 30 / mix of ASR + WhisperKit ASR variants. **The actually-impactful real-user heart-path issue.**
    27	- Issue-API lifetime `asr_empty_result` count of 23 / 4 users includes events outside the 14d window — useful for "ever happens" but not for "active today."
    28	
    29	**Diagnosis:** V2's `force_stall(1000)` injects via `AudioServiceHandler` XPC bridge directly. That path bypasses `handleEngineConfigurationChange()` — the production recovery routine that re-arms the engine on real interruption. Real-world interruptions arrive via `.AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange` notification or `AVCaptureSession.wasInterruptedNotification`, both of which trigger recovery. V2 skips both. The wedge it produced is unreachable from any real user action.
    30	
    31	**Actions taken:**
    32	- **#553 closed not-planned** with full real-world test matrix + corrected production-only Sentry numbers in the close-out comment.
    33	- **#559 filed:** V2 harness must replace direct XPC stall injection with `NotificationCenter.post(.AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange)` and/or simulated `wasInterruptedNotification`. After harness fix, re-run V2 Lane A on A5 + A6/A7/A9 (which were SKIPPED only because A5 wedged the session ahead of them); also re-evaluate **#555** and **#556** against real-world repro before treating either as production defects.
    34	- **Bible §6.1 V2 row** updated PLANNED → PARTIAL with #559 dependency; **§30 Changelog** new v1.30 entry capturing the testing methodology and Sentry-by-environment correction.
    35	
    85	**Lessons captured (durable):**
    86	- **Ask "is there an existing path?" before designing a new one.** Re-gate on `isDebugModeEnabled` was a real plan with full council review; it took the founder's "wait, didn't we build infrastructure for this?" challenge to surface that `wispr-rebuild-debug` already covered 95% of the requirement. The grounded-review template's premise check should explicitly include "does the canonical run-the-thing path support running this thing?" — which is exactly what #548 will codify.
    87	- **`pkill -f "<DevAppName>"` is a footgun in multi-worktree setups.** Added to gotchas.md.
    88	- **`git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/<branch>`** is the maintenance escape for branch-label changes in the main worktree when Rule 8 forbids `git checkout`. Pure ref-table operation, doesn't touch files, doesn't trip the hook regex.
    89	- **The `--options runtime` + `--entitlements` flags in dev codesign break Sparkle library validation.** `bundle-dev.sh` and `distribution.md` deliberately omit them. Don't add them when replicating the bundling.
    90	
    91	**Open from this session (not blocking wind-down):**
    92	- #548 (P0 weekend remediation) — plan-template canonical-bundle-path field, council preamble runtime-path check, grounded-review Q3, push-hook third-option skip-note rejection, auto-mode prompt clarification. None shipped tonight; all queued for the weekend per the issue body.
    93	- Lane B1 Bluetooth scenario — V2 toolkit is now exercisable, but Lane B requires a real human flipping a real BT device. Independent task, not blocking anything.
    94	
    95	
    96	
    97	**V1 closure (PR #541 merged).** Bible §18 originally planned a 3-hr metronome dictation profile + 25-min cold bench. Founder rule mid-session: no synthetic test longer than 5 min unless explicitly requested for a one-off. The marathon designs were built around a "users idle, then re-use causes cold-path slowdown" hypothesis traced to one #272 Gemma4 incident. **V1a tested that hypothesis against 30 days of production telemetry** (16 active installs, 12 dictating users, 238 dictations, 150 polish events) and the hypothesis does not generalize: 100% of production polish is Apple Intelligence (no cloud providers in window — the cache-rotation mechanism the rule guarded against does not exist in `FoundationModels.LanguageModelSession`); polish latency vs gap-since-previous-polish is flat (p50 1.0–1.2s across all gaps from <30s to >4hr; p99 max never crosses 3.4s; no knee); Sentry corroborates with 0 polish failures, 0 network failures, 0 cold-path-shaped events in 14 days. **Performance & latency downgraded to Low Risk** for the upcoming audit rerun. Evidence: `docs/audits/2026-04-30-v1a-cold-path-telemetry.md` (local-only). Bible §18 rewritten to retain a slim on-demand V1 (V1a telemetry refresh + optional V1b cold-launch single dictation + optional V1c 50-dictation stress, all <5 min). Marathon designs retired. Companion amendment to `validation-discipline.md §9` (drop "5-min idle between samples" prescription) drafted as a separate PR per zero-blast-radius split.
    98	
    99	**Four V1a follow-ups filed for separate triage** (none blocking V1 close): (1) `cold_start=true` never registers in production `asr.completed` — likely telemetry bug; (2) e2e dictation tail past 2-min gaps (p90 jumps from 2.1s → 5–7s, but ASR p50 = 100ms and polish p50 = 1.0s — unattributed time lives in audio engine spinup or recording-duration variance); (3) `paste_failed` rolling-window production count ~14 events (issues ENVIOUSWISPR-8 + ENVIOUSWISPR-M, heart-path-adjacent); (4) Sentry env-tag knowledge note for `observability-operations.md` (REST `/issues/` returns combined-environment counts; production-only requires per-issue tags fetch).
   100	
   101	**Sentry env-filter mistake during V1a.** First read of Sentry returned 117 paste_failed events headline → looked like the "largest open production cluster." Founder caught it: "was that me or production?" Drilled into per-issue tags: 87 of 101 events on 2026-04-25 cluster were on dev builds (`v1.9.4-31-g6dd361e-dualmode-logs2-dev` — founder's dual-mode polish work). Real production count is ~14, downgraded from "urgent triage" to "small steady trickle worth understanding." `observability-operations.md` says explicitly "always filter by environment=production" — same lesson surfaced before, applies to Sentry REST too.
   102	
   103	**V2 plan finalized (PR #542 merged).** Plan at `docs/feature-requests/issue-291-2026-04-30-v2-fault-injection-toolkit.md`. Hybrid toolkit per founder direction (and independently per @m13v's split recommendation): runtime harness via wispr_eyes for everything Claude can drive autonomously, founder-required only for Bluetooth, Swift `@Test` for pure state-machine invariants. **Policy:** Lane A/B never run in CI (impossible — no audio device, no GUI, no microphone on hosted runners; even if possible, blocking PRs on a runtime suite for unrelated changes is anti-velocity). Lane C runs on PR CI as standard Swift tests. **Self-test discipline reframed** from per-scenario regression+revert to per-mechanism red/green at PR time only (GPT review surfaced the original was overkill).
   104	
   105	**V2 plan went through two rounds of Codex grounded review + GPT 5.5 + Gemini 3.1 Pro council.** Round 1 absorbed 5 PIVOTs: A5 dropped (WhisperKit ASR is in-process — `WhisperKitPipeline.swift:801-817` calls `backend.transcribe()` directly, no ASRManagerProxy involvement); stall injection moved from pipelines → AudioCaptureProxy (capture-side concern, WhisperKit batch sets `onBufferCaptured = nil` so pipeline-level stall would miss WhisperKit batch entirely); access modifier `package` → `internal+@testable` (SPM puts pipeline + app + test target in same package, so `package` exposes seam to app target); §10 file paths corrected throughout (real XPC clients are `Sources/EnviousWisprAudio/AudioCaptureProxy.swift` + `Sources/EnviousWisprASR/ASRManagerProxy.swift`, NOT the nonexistent `XPCServiceClient.swift`; test directory is `Tests/EnviousWisprTests/{Pipeline,App,Services}/V2/`, NOT the nonexistent `Tests/EnviousWisprPipelineTests/`); Lane C reduced from 6 to 4 invariants on actual owners (C3 backend-switch on `PipelineSettingsSync` not pipeline; C4 hands-free lock on `HotkeyService`/AppState not pipeline; "session-id mismatch" + "zombie recovery" removed — latter requires fake `WhisperKitBackend` that doesn't exist per `PreWarmThrowsTests.swift:7-13`). Round 2 absorbed 3 precision revisions: C1 mechanism scope clarified (pipeline fixture covers pipeline dedup, NOT proxy seam — those are separate test surfaces); DEBUG endpoint owner pivoted from `EnviousWisprApp.swift` (only does observability init + scene building) → small `DebugFaultEndpoint` type retained by `AppDelegate` (the real startup hook with appState access at `applicationDidFinishLaunching` lines 37-86 + cleanup in `applicationWillTerminate` lines 408-420); A7 narrowed to Cocoa terminate (no SIGTERM/signal handler exists in `Sources/`); A8 split into A8a Parakeet (true cancel propagation via held `modelLoadTask` cancelled at `TranscriptionPipeline.swift:1089-1096`) + A8b WhisperKit (state-unwind only — `backend.prepare()` awaited directly with no held task to cancel).
   106	
   107	**Open Q1 trigger mechanism.** UserDefaults polling rejected (Gemini concern: pollutes heart path with hot-path config reads + perturbs timing measurement). DistributedNotificationCenter rejected (deprecating). Selected: tightly-gated DEBUG-only localhost endpoint — env-gated `EW_FAULT_INJECTION=1`, binds 127.0.0.1, per-launch token at `~/Library/Logs/EnviousWispr/fault-token-<pid>` with 0600 perms + atomic write before accepting commands, fixed command set (no arbitrary RPC), dispatched to owning actor via `Task { @MainActor in ... }`.

     1	@preconcurrency import AVFoundation
     2	import EnviousWisprAudio

     6	/// XPC service handler — composes an AudioCaptureManager and bridges its lifecycle
     7	/// to the XPC protocol. All capture logic runs in the service process; the host app
     8	/// receives buffers and state changes via AudioServiceClientProtocol callbacks.
     9	final class AudioServiceHandler: NSObject, AudioServiceProtocol, @unchecked Sendable {
    10	  /// The XPC connection back to the host — set by AudioServiceDelegate.
    11	  weak var connection: NSXPCConnection?  // periphery:ignore - XPC connection lifecycle; set by delegate, prevents premature release
    12	  /// Client proxy for sending callbacks to the host app.
    13	  /// Resolved from connection.remoteObjectProxy — set by AudioServiceDelegate.
    14	  var clientProxy: (any AudioServiceClientProtocol)?
    15	
    16	  /// The in-process audio capture engine. Runs entirely within this XPC service process.
    17	  /// @MainActor — the XPC service's main run loop serves as the MainActor executor.
    18	  /// Created lazily on MainActor when first needed.
    19	  private var _captureManager: AudioCaptureManager?
    20	
    21	  /// Dedicated serial queue for XPC sends — keeps XPC messaging off the RT audio thread.
    22	  private let xpcSendQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.enviouswispr.audioservice.xpc-send")
    23	
    24	  // MARK: - Service-side VAD state (Step 5)
    25	
    26	  /// Stored VAD configuration — applied when capture begins, replayed after crash.
    27	  private var vadAutoStop: Bool = false
    28	  private var vadSilenceTimeout: Double = 1.5
    29	  private var vadSensitivity: Float = 0.5
    30	  private var vadEnergyGate: Bool = false
    31	
    32	  /// Service-owned SilenceDetector — runs VAD in the service process where samples live.
    33	  private var silenceDetector: SilenceDetector?
    34	
    35	  /// VAD monitoring task — started after beginCapture, cancelled on stopCapture/abortPreWarm.
    36	  private var vadMonitorTask: Task<Void, Never>?
    37	
    38	  /// Get or create the capture manager on MainActor, wiring callbacks on first creation.
    39	  @MainActor
    40	  private var captureManager: AudioCaptureManager {
    41	    if let existing = _captureManager { return existing }
    42	    let manager = AudioCaptureManager()
    43	    wireCallbacks(on: manager)
    44	    _captureManager = manager
    45	    return manager
    46	  }
    47	
    48	  /// Wire AudioCaptureManager callbacks to XPC client proxy calls.
    49	  @MainActor
    50	  private func wireCallbacks(on manager: AudioCaptureManager) {
    51	    // Buffer callback: fires on CoreAudio's real-time audio thread.
    52	    // RT discipline: only arithmetic + bounded memcpy on the RT thread.
    53	    // XPC send dispatched to xpcSendQueue.
    54	    manager.onBufferCaptured = { [weak self] buffer in
    55	      guard let floatData = buffer.floatChannelData?[0] else { return }
    56	      let count = Int(buffer.frameLength)
    57	      guard count > 0 else { return }
    58	
    59	      // RMS: tight arithmetic loop — no heap allocation, RT-safe.
    60	      // Matches AudioBufferProcessor.calculateRMS formula: -60dB..0dB → 0..1
    61	      var sum: Float = 0
    62	      for i in 0..<count { sum += floatData[i] * floatData[i] }
    63	      let rms = sqrt(sum / Float(count))
    64	      let dBFS = 20 * log10(max(rms, 1e-6))
    65	      let level = max(0, min(1, (dBFS + 60) / 60))
    66	
    67	      // Capture buffer to keep its memory alive across the queue hop.
    68	      // Data(bytes:count:) does malloc — moved off RT thread to xpcSendQueue.
    69	      nonisolated(unsafe) let safeBuffer = buffer
    70	      self?.xpcSendQueue.async { [weak self] in
    71	        guard let floats = safeBuffer.floatChannelData?[0] else { return }
    72	        let data = Data(bytes: floats, count: count * MemoryLayout<Float>.size)
    73	        self?.clientProxy?.audioBufferCaptured(data, frameCount: count, audioLevel: level)
    74	      }
    75	    }
    76	
    77	    // Engine interruption callback: fires on @MainActor.
    78	    manager.onEngineInterrupted = { [weak self] in
    79	      self?.xpcSendQueue.async { [weak self] in
    80	        self?.clientProxy?.engineInterrupted()
    81	      }
    82	    }
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  // MARK: - Diagnostics
    86	
    87	  func ping(reply: @escaping (String) -> Void) {
    88	    reply("pong")
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  func checkMicPermission(reply: @escaping (Int, String) -> Void) {
    92	    let status = AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .audio)
    93	    let name: String
    94	    switch status {
    95	    case .notDetermined: name = "notDetermined"
    96	    case .restricted: name = "restricted"
    97	    case .denied: name = "denied"
    98	    case .authorized: name = "authorized"
    99	    @unknown default: name = "unknown(\(status.rawValue))"
   100	    }
   101	    reply(status.rawValue, name)
   102	  }
   103	
   104	  // MARK: - Configuration
   105	
   106	  func buildEngine(noiseSuppression: Bool) {
   107	    Task { @MainActor in
   108	      captureManager.buildEngine(noiseSuppression: noiseSuppression)
   109	    }
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  func setNoiseSuppressionEnabled(_ enabled: Bool) {
   113	    Task { @MainActor in
   114	      captureManager.noiseSuppressionEnabled = enabled
   115	    }
   116	  }
   117	
   118	  func setPreferredInputDeviceUID(_ uid: String) {
   119	    Task { @MainActor in
   120	      captureManager.preferredInputDeviceIDOverride = uid
   121	    }
   122	  }
   123	
   124	  func setSelectedInputDeviceUID(_ uid: String) {
   125	    Task { @MainActor in
   126	      captureManager.selectedInputDeviceUID = uid
   127	    }
   128	  }
   129	
   130	  func setWarmEnginePolicy(_ rawValue: String) {
   131	    let policy = WarmEnginePolicy(rawValue: rawValue) ?? .seconds30
   132	    Task { @MainActor in
   133	      captureManager.warmEnginePolicy = policy
   134	    }
   135	  }

   140	    preferredDeviceUID: String,

   209	    let fired = telemetry.stallFired(
   210	      ctx: ctx,
   211	      isActivelyCapturing: audioCapture.isActivelyCapturing
   212	    )
   213	
   214	    // Issue #289: flip terminal state SYNCHRONOUSLY before any await so a
   215	    // racing PTT key-up can't slip into `stopAndTranscribe` against a live
   216	    // `.recording` state. The emitter's per-session dedup already
   217	    // suppressed the captureError on re-entry; we still must guard the
   218	    // state flip the same way.

   220	    guard state == .recording else { return }

   222	    isPreWarmed = false

   230	    // gate while still pre-`beginCapturePhase`. The token is cleared by any
   231	    // path that takes ownership back, so fast-retry + cross-pipeline races
   232	    // both bail cleanly here.
   233	    Task { @MainActor [weak self, sessionID = ctx.sessionID] in
   234	      await self?.finishStallRecovery(for: sessionID)
   235	    }
   236	  }

   242	  /// would leak it in the committed-recovery case.

   140	    preferredDeviceUID: String,
   141	    selectedDeviceUID: String,
   142	    reply: @escaping (NSError?) -> Void
   143	  ) {

   173	  func beginCapture(reply: @escaping (NSError?) -> Void) {
   174	    nonisolated(unsafe) let safeReply = reply
   175	    Task { @MainActor in
   176	      do {
   177	        _ = try await self.captureManager.beginCapturePhase()
   178	        self.startVADMonitoring()
   179	        safeReply(nil)
   180	      } catch {
   181	        // XPC error sanitization boundary.
   182	        safeReply(XPCErrorSanitizer.sanitizeForXPC(error))
   183	      }
   184	    }
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  func stopCapture(reply: @escaping (Data, Data) -> Void) {
   188	    nonisolated(unsafe) let safeReply = reply
   189	    Task { @MainActor in
   190	      self.cancelVADMonitoring()
   191	
   192	      // Stop capture first: freezes the tap, returns all accumulated samples,
   193	      // clears the internal buffer. No new samples can arrive after this.
   194	      let captureResult = await self.captureManager.stopCapture()
   195	
   196	      // Finalize VAD using the EXACT count of returned samples. This guarantees
   197	      // segment endpoints align perfectly with the sample array the caller receives.
   198	      // Using captureResult.samples.count (not capturedSamples.count) avoids both:
   199	      // - The original bug: capturedSamples already cleared -> count=0 -> endSample=0
   200	      // - The race window: samples arriving during await -> count drift -> tail trim
   201	      var vadData = Data()
   202	      if let detector = self.silenceDetector {
   203	        await detector.finalizeSegments(totalSampleCount: captureResult.samples.count)
   204	        let segments = await detector.speechSegments
   205	        vadData = Data(capacity: segments.count * MemoryLayout<Int32>.size * 2)
   206	        for seg in segments {
   207	          var start = Int32(seg.startSample)
   208	          var end = Int32(seg.endSample)
   209	          vadData.append(Data(bytes: &start, count: MemoryLayout<Int32>.size))
   210	          vadData.append(Data(bytes: &end, count: MemoryLayout<Int32>.size))
   211	        }
   212	      }
   213	
   214	      let sampleData = captureResult.samples.withUnsafeBytes { Data($0) }
   215	      safeReply(sampleData, vadData)
   216	    }
   217	  }
   218	
   219	  func abortPreWarm() {
   220	    Task { @MainActor in
   221	      self.cancelVADMonitoring()
   222	      self.captureManager.abortPreWarm()
   223	    }
   224	  }

   230	  }

   234	  func configureVAD(autoStop: Bool, silenceTimeout: Double, sensitivity: Float, energyGate: Bool) {
   235	    self.vadAutoStop = autoStop
   236	    self.vadSilenceTimeout = silenceTimeout
   237	    self.vadSensitivity = sensitivity
   238	    self.vadEnergyGate = energyGate
   239	
   240	    // If VAD is already running mid-session, rebuild the detector with new config.
   241	    if let detector = silenceDetector {
   242	      let config = SmoothedVADConfig.fromSensitivity(sensitivity, energyGate: energyGate)
   243	      Task { await detector.updateConfig(config) }
   244	    }
   245	  }
   246	
   247	  func getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: Int, reply: @escaping (Data, Int) -> Void) {
   248	    nonisolated(unsafe) let safeReply = reply
   249	    Task { @MainActor in
   250	      let (samples, totalCount) = await self.captureManager.getSamplesSnapshot(fromIndex: fromIndex)
   251	      let data = samples.withUnsafeBytes { Data($0) }
   252	      safeReply(data, totalCount)
   253	    }
   254	  }
   255	
   256	  func getVADSegments(reply: @escaping (Data) -> Void) {
   257	    nonisolated(unsafe) let safeReply = reply
   258	    Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   259	      guard let self, let detector = self.silenceDetector else {
   260	        safeReply(Data())
   261	        return
   262	      }
   263	      // Finalize any open segment before returning.
   264	      let totalCount = self.captureManager.capturedSamples.count
   265	      await detector.finalizeSegments(totalSampleCount: totalCount)
   266	      let segments = await detector.speechSegments
   267	
   268	      // Encode as packed [Int32 start, Int32 end] pairs.
   269	      var packed = Data(capacity: segments.count * MemoryLayout<Int32>.size * 2)
   270	      for seg in segments {
   271	        var start = Int32(seg.startSample)
   272	        var end = Int32(seg.endSample)
   273	        packed.append(Data(bytes: &start, count: MemoryLayout<Int32>.size))
   274	        packed.append(Data(bytes: &end, count: MemoryLayout<Int32>.size))
   275	      }
   276	      safeReply(packed)
   277	    }
   278	  }

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba Sources/EnviousWisprServices/CaptureTelemetryState.swift | sed -n '1,120p'" in /Users/m4pro_sv/Developer/EnviousLabs/EnviousWispr
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import Foundation
     2	
     3	/// App-wide telemetry state for audio capture diagnostics. Owns dedupe state
     4	/// for zombie-engine zero-peak events (#302) and the monotonic counter of
     5	/// `AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange` notifications since launch (used as the
     6	/// smoking-gun diagnostic for #294).
     7	///
     8	/// Shared across both pipelines so a Parakeet-to-WhisperKit switch does not
     9	/// reset dedupe, and so `timeSinceLastSuccessfulRecordingMs` is an app-level
    10	/// baseline rather than pipeline-local.
    11	@MainActor
    12	public final class CaptureTelemetryState {
    13	  private var lastZombieEmittedAt: ContinuousClock.Instant?
    14	  private var lastZombieRoute: String?
    15	  private var lastSuccessfulRecordingAt: ContinuousClock.Instant?
    16	  private(set) public var configurationChangeCount: Int = 0
    17	
    18	  public init() {}
    19	
    20	  /// Called at transcript save (not paste end) so clipboard-only users are
    21	  /// covered. Resets zombie dedupe so a successful recording sandwiched
    22	  /// between two zombie events still surfaces the second event.
    23	  public func recordSuccessfulRecording() {
    24	    lastSuccessfulRecordingAt = .now
    25	    lastZombieEmittedAt = nil
    26	    lastZombieRoute = nil
    27	  }
    28	
    29	  /// Returns true if a zombie event on `route` should emit to Sentry now.
    30	  /// False when a prior event fired less than `window` ago on the same route
    31	  /// with no intervening successful recording.
    32	  public func shouldEmitZombie(route: String, window: Duration) -> Bool {
    33	    guard let last = lastZombieEmittedAt, lastZombieRoute == route else {
    34	      return true
    35	    }
    36	    return .now - last >= window
    37	  }
    38	
    39	  /// Marks that a zombie event was observed (regardless of whether it emitted
    40	  /// to Sentry). The 30s window is relative to the most recent observation,
    41	  /// not the most recent emission, so rapid retries stay suppressed.
    42	  public func markZombieEmitted(route: String) {
    43	    lastZombieEmittedAt = .now
    44	    lastZombieRoute = route
    45	  }
    46	
    47	  public func incrementConfigChange() {
    48	    configurationChangeCount += 1
    49	  }
    50	
    51	  /// Milliseconds since the last successful recording. Nil if the app session
    52	  /// has never produced a successful recording yet.
    53	  public func timeSinceLastSuccessfulRecordingMs() -> Int? {
    54	    guard let last = lastSuccessfulRecordingAt else { return nil }
    55	    let elapsed = ContinuousClock.now - last
    56	    let (seconds, attoseconds) = elapsed.components
    57	    return Int(seconds) * 1000 + Int(attoseconds / 1_000_000_000_000_000)
    58	  }
    59	}

codex
One important code-grounded finding: the default app uses `AudioCaptureProxy`, so real engine/session ownership is in `EnviousWisprAudioService`, not the host app. The current debug endpoint is host-app only, so a realistic notification seam has to run in the service process or use direct in-process audio mode; otherwise it can only poke the proxy/XPC surface.

    80	    self.captureTelemetry = captureTelemetry

    85	  // MARK: - Events
    86	
    87	  /// Emit `audio_capture_stalled` once per session. Subsequent calls within
    88	  /// the same session are dropped silently (dedup).
    89	  /// Returns true if the event fired (caller may chain terminal-state work).
    90	  @discardableResult
    91	  func stallFired(
    92	    ctx: CaptureStallContext,
    93	    isActivelyCapturing: Bool
    94	  ) -> Bool {
    95	    resetIfNewSession(ctx.sessionID)
    96	    guard !stallEventAlreadyCaptured else { return false }
    97	    stallEventAlreadyCaptured = true
    98	
    99	    let extras = SentryAudioExtras.buildCaptureExtras(
   100	      route: ctx.route,
   101	      sourceType: ctx.sourceType,
   102	      sessionID: ctx.sessionID,
   103	      isActivelyCapturing: isActivelyCapturing,
   104	      inputDeviceUIDPreferred: ctx.inputDeviceUIDPreferred,
   105	      inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault: ctx.inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault,
   106	      failureMode: "stall_window_elapsed",
   107	      stallContext: ctx
   108	    )
   109	    captureError(
   110	      HeartPathError.audioCaptureStalled(sessionID: ctx.sessionID, ctx: ctx),
   111	      .audioCaptureStalled,
   112	      "recording",
   113	      extras
   114	    )
   115	    return true
   116	  }
   117	
   118	  /// Emit `xpc_service_error`. Marks the session so a subsequent rawSamples-empty
   119	  /// branch dedups to a breadcrumb instead of a duplicate captureError.
   120	  func xpcReplyFailed(ctx: XPCReplyFailureContext) {
   121	    resetIfNewSession(ctx.sessionID)
   122	    xpcReplyFailedThisSession = true
   123	    captureError(
   124	      HeartPathError.xpcReplyFailed(ctx: ctx),
   125	      .xpcServiceError,
   126	      "audio",
   127	      [
   128	        "xpc.reply_stage": ctx.replyStage,
   129	        "xpc.error_domain": ctx.errorDomain,
   130	        "xpc.error_code": ctx.errorCode,
   131	        "capture_session_id": Int(ctx.sessionID),
   132	      ]
   133	    )
   134	  }
   135	
   136	  /// Emit `audio_capture_failed` for a capture-session interruption. Backend
   137	  /// extra preserved exactly as it was per pipeline (WhisperKit included it,
   138	  /// Parakeet did not).
   139	  func captureSessionInterrupted(ctx: CaptureSessionInterruptionContext) {
   140	    var extra: [String: Any] = [
   141	      "capture_session.kind": ctx.kind.rawValue,
   142	      "capture_session.reason_code": ctx.reasonCode.map { $0 } ?? NSNull(),
   143	      "capture_session.reason_label": ctx.reasonLabel ?? NSNull(),
   144	      "capture_session.error_domain": ctx.errorDomain ?? NSNull(),
   145	      "capture_session.error_code": ctx.errorCode.map { $0 } ?? NSNull(),
   146	      "capture_session.error_description": ctx.errorDescription ?? NSNull(),
   147	      "capture.is_actively_capturing": ctx.isActivelyCapturing,
   148	      "capture_session_id": Int(ctx.sessionID),
   149	    ]
   150	    // Historical asymmetry — only WhisperKit's interruption carried the
   151	    // backend extra. Preserve to keep Sentry grouping/triage stable.
   152	    if backend == .whisperKit {
   153	      extra["backend"] = backend.rawValue
   154	    }
   155	    captureError(
   156	      HeartPathError.captureSessionInterrupted(ctx: ctx),
   157	      .audioCaptureFailed,
   158	      "audio",
   159	      extra
   160	    )
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  /// Emit either a deduped breadcrumb (if a stall or XPC failure already
   164	  /// fired for this session) or a terminal `no_audio_captured` captureError.
   165	  /// Mirrors the prior in-pipeline `emitNoAudioCapturedEvent` call sites.
   166	  func noAudioCaptured(ctx: NoAudioContext) {
   167	    resetIfNewSession(ctx.sessionID)
   168	    if stallEventAlreadyCaptured || xpcReplyFailedThisSession {
   169	      let dedupedFrom =
   170	        stallEventAlreadyCaptured ? "audio_capture_stalled" : "xpc_reply_failed"
   203	  /// Emit `zombie_engine_zero_peak` when the VAD gate gets a full recording
   204	  /// of exactly-zero audio with non-empty samples (matches the zombie-engine
   205	  /// failure described in `gotchas.md`). Dedupes via shared
   206	  /// `CaptureTelemetryState`. Returns true iff an event fired.
   207	  ///
   208	  /// Invariant: `markZombieEmitted` is called UNCONDITIONALLY before the
   209	  /// `shouldEmit` guard so the 30s dedup window slides forward on every
   210	  /// observation, not just on emissions. Rapid retries from the same route
   211	  /// stay suppressed instead of slipping through after the first emit's
   212	  /// window expires. See `CaptureTelemetryState.markZombieEmitted` doc
   213	  /// comment.
   214	  @discardableResult
   215	  func zombieZeroPeak(ctx: ZeroPeakContext) -> Bool {
   216	    let shouldEmit = captureTelemetry.shouldEmitZombie(
   217	      route: ctx.route, window: .seconds(30))
   218	    captureTelemetry.markZombieEmitted(route: ctx.route)
   219	    guard shouldEmit else { return false }
   220	
   221	    let err = HeartPathError.zombieEngineZeroPeak(
   222	      sessionID: ctx.sessionID,
   223	      durationMs: ctx.durationMs,
   224	      route: ctx.route,
   225	      sampleCount: ctx.sampleCount
   226	    )
   227	    captureError(
   228	      err,
   229	      .audioCaptureFailed,
   230	      "recording",
   231	      SentryAudioExtras.buildCaptureExtras(
   232	        route: ctx.route,
   233	        sourceType: ctx.captureSourceType,
   234	        sessionID: ctx.sessionID,
   235	        isActivelyCapturing: ctx.isActivelyCapturing,
   236	        inputDeviceUIDPreferred: ctx.inputDeviceUIDPreferred,
   237	        inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault: ctx.inputDeviceUIDSystemDefault,
   238	        failureMode: "zombie_engine_zero_peak",
   239	        timeSinceLastSuccessfulRecordingMs:
   240	          captureTelemetry.timeSinceLastSuccessfulRecordingMs(),
   241	        configChangeCountSinceLaunch: captureTelemetry.configurationChangeCount
   242	      )
   243	    )
   244	    return true
   245	  }

     1	import CoreAudio

     4	/// Represents an audio input device discovered via CoreAudio.
     5	public struct AudioInputDevice: Sendable, Identifiable, Hashable {
     6	  public let id: AudioDeviceID
     7	  public let name: String
     8	  public let uid: String
     9	}
    10	
    11	/// Enumerates audio input devices using CoreAudio HAL.
    12	public enum AudioDeviceEnumerator {
    13	  /// Returns all audio input devices currently connected.
    14	  public static func allInputDevices() -> [AudioInputDevice] {
    15	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
    16	      mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices,
    17	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
    18	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
    19	    )
    20	
    21	    var dataSize: UInt32 = 0
    22	    var status = AudioObjectGetPropertyDataSize(
    23	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
    24	      &propertyAddress,
    25	      0,
    26	      nil,
    27	      &dataSize
    28	    )
    29	    guard status == noErr, dataSize > 0 else { return [] }
    30	
    31	    let deviceCount = Int(dataSize) / MemoryLayout<AudioDeviceID>.size
    32	    var deviceIDs = [AudioDeviceID](repeating: 0, count: deviceCount)
    33	
    34	    status = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
    35	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
    36	      &propertyAddress,
    37	      0,
    38	      nil,
    39	      &dataSize,
    40	      &deviceIDs
    41	    )
    42	    guard status == noErr else { return [] }
    43	
    44	    return deviceIDs.compactMap { deviceID -> AudioInputDevice? in
    45	      let channelCount = inputChannelCount(for: deviceID)
    46	      guard channelCount > 0 else { return nil }
    47	
    48	      let name =
    49	        stringProperty(for: deviceID, selector: kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceNameCFString) ?? "Unknown"
    50	      let uid = stringProperty(for: deviceID, selector: kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceUID) ?? ""
    51	
    52	      return AudioInputDevice(
    53	        id: deviceID,
    54	        name: name,
    55	        uid: uid
    56	      )
    57	    }
    58	  }
    59	
    60	  /// Returns the default system input device ID, or nil if unavailable.
    61	  public static func defaultInputDeviceID() -> AudioDeviceID? {
    62	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
    63	      mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultInputDevice,
    64	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
    65	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
    66	    )
    67	
    68	    var deviceID: AudioDeviceID = 0
    69	    var dataSize = UInt32(MemoryLayout<AudioDeviceID>.size)
    70	
    71	    let status = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
    72	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
    73	      &propertyAddress,
    74	      0,
    75	      nil,
    76	      &dataSize,
    77	      &deviceID
    78	    )
    79	
    80	    guard status == noErr, deviceID != kAudioObjectUnknown else { return nil }
    81	    return deviceID
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  /// Finds a device ID by its persistent UID string.
    85	  public static func deviceID(forUID uid: String) -> AudioDeviceID? {
    86	    let devices = allInputDevices()
    87	    return devices.first(where: { $0.uid == uid })?.id
    88	  }
    89	
    90	  /// Returns the UID of the current system-default input device, or nil if
    91	  /// none. Used by Sentry extras (`capture.input_device_uid_system_default`)
    92	  /// so divergence vs the preferred device is measured correctly.
    93	  public static func defaultInputDeviceUID() -> String? {
    94	    guard let id = defaultInputDeviceID() else { return nil }
    95	    return stringProperty(for: id, selector: kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceUID)
    96	  }
    97	
    98	  // MARK: - Bluetooth & Smart Device Selection
    99	
   100	  /// Returns true if the given device uses Bluetooth transport (Classic or LE).
   101	  public static func isBluetoothDevice(_ deviceID: AudioDeviceID) -> Bool {
   102	    let transport = transportType(for: deviceID)
   103	    return transport == kAudioDeviceTransportTypeBluetooth
   104	      || transport == kAudioDeviceTransportTypeBluetoothLE
   105	  }
   106	
   107	  /// Returns the AudioDeviceID of the built-in microphone, if one exists.
   108	  public static func builtInMicrophoneDeviceID() -> AudioDeviceID? {
   109	    let devices = allInputDevices()
   110	    return devices.first { transportType(for: $0.id) == kAudioDeviceTransportTypeBuiltIn }?.id
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  /// Returns the default system output device ID, or nil if unavailable.
   114	  public static func defaultOutputDeviceID() -> AudioDeviceID? {
   115	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   116	      mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultOutputDevice,
   117	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   118	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   119	    )
   120	
   121	    var deviceID: AudioDeviceID = 0
   122	    var dataSize = UInt32(MemoryLayout<AudioDeviceID>.size)
   123	
   124	    let status = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
   125	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
   126	      &propertyAddress,
   127	      0,
   128	      nil,
   129	      &dataSize,
   130	      &deviceID
   131	    )
   132	
   133	    guard status == noErr, deviceID != kAudioObjectUnknown else { return nil }
   134	    return deviceID
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  /// Returns true if the device's I/O cycle is active (audio is flowing somewhere).
   138	  public static func isDeviceRunningSomewhere(_ deviceID: AudioDeviceID) -> Bool {
   139	    var isRunning: UInt32 = 0
   140	    var size = UInt32(MemoryLayout<UInt32>.size)
   141	    var addr = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   142	      mSelector: kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceIsRunningSomewhere,
   143	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   144	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   145	    )
   146	    AudioObjectGetPropertyData(deviceID, &addr, 0, nil, &size, &isRunning)
   147	    return isRunning != 0
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  /// Recommended input device given current output device and media-playing state.
   151	  /// Returns the built-in mic if Bluetooth output is active and media is playing;
   152	  /// nil otherwise (meaning "use whatever is currently selected").
   153	  public static func recommendedInputDevice() -> AudioDeviceID? {
   154	    guard let outputDeviceID = defaultOutputDeviceID() else { return nil }
   155	    guard isBluetoothDevice(outputDeviceID) else { return nil }
   156	    guard isDeviceRunningSomewhere(outputDeviceID) else { return nil }
   157	    return builtInMicrophoneDeviceID()
   158	  }
   159	
   160	  // MARK: - Private Helpers
   161	
   162	  static func transportType(for deviceID: AudioDeviceID) -> UInt32 {
   163	    var transport: UInt32 = 0
   164	    var size = UInt32(MemoryLayout<UInt32>.size)
   165	    var addr = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   166	      mSelector: kAudioDevicePropertyTransportType,
   167	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   168	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   169	    )
   170	    AudioObjectGetPropertyData(deviceID, &addr, 0, nil, &size, &transport)
   171	    return transport
   172	  }
   173	
   174	  private static func inputChannelCount(for deviceID: AudioDeviceID) -> Int {
   175	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   176	      mSelector: kAudioDevicePropertyStreamConfiguration,
   177	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeInput,
   178	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   179	    )
   180	
   181	    var dataSize: UInt32 = 0
   182	    let status = AudioObjectGetPropertyDataSize(deviceID, &propertyAddress, 0, nil, &dataSize)
   183	    guard status == noErr, dataSize > 0 else { return 0 }
   184	
   185	    // AudioBufferList has a variable-length trailing array of AudioBuffer entries.
   186	    // Allocate the exact byte count reported by CoreAudio to avoid heap corruption
   187	    // on multi-stream devices where dataSize > MemoryLayout<AudioBufferList>.size.
   188	    let rawPointer = UnsafeMutableRawPointer.allocate(
   189	      byteCount: Int(dataSize),
   190	      alignment: MemoryLayout<AudioBufferList>.alignment
   191	    )
   192	    defer { rawPointer.deallocate() }
   193	
   194	    let bufferListPointer = rawPointer.bindMemory(to: AudioBufferList.self, capacity: 1)
   195	
   196	    let getStatus = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
   197	      deviceID, &propertyAddress, 0, nil, &dataSize, bufferListPointer)
   198	    guard getStatus == noErr else { return 0 }
   199	
   200	    let bufferList = UnsafeMutableAudioBufferListPointer(bufferListPointer)
   201	    return bufferList.reduce(0) { $0 + Int($1.mNumberChannels) }
   202	  }
   203	
   204	  private static func stringProperty(
   205	    for deviceID: AudioDeviceID, selector: AudioObjectPropertySelector
   206	  ) -> String? {
   207	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   208	      mSelector: selector,
   209	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   210	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   211	    )
   212	
   213	    // CoreAudio string properties return CFStringRef — use withUnsafeMutablePointer
   214	    // to avoid the warning about forming UnsafeMutableRawPointer to a reference type.
   215	    var dataSize = UInt32(MemoryLayout<CFString>.size)
   216	    var result: CFString? = nil
   217	
   218	    let status = withUnsafeMutablePointer(to: &result) { ptr in
   219	      AudioObjectGetPropertyData(deviceID, &propertyAddress, 0, nil, &dataSize, ptr)
   220	    }
   221	    guard status == noErr, let name = result else { return nil }
   222	    return name as String
   223	  }
   224	}
   225	
   226	/// Monitors audio device connect/disconnect events via CoreAudio property listener.
   227	public final class AudioDeviceMonitor: Sendable {
   228	  private let onDevicesChanged: @Sendable () -> Void
   229	  /// Stored listener block — CoreAudio requires the same reference for removal.
   230	  nonisolated(unsafe) private var listenerBlock: AudioObjectPropertyListenerBlock?
   231	
   232	  public init(onDevicesChanged: @escaping @Sendable () -> Void) {
   233	    self.onDevicesChanged = onDevicesChanged
   234	    startListening()
   235	  }
   236	
   237	  deinit {
   238	    stopListening()
   239	  }

   226	/// Monitors audio device connect/disconnect events via CoreAudio property listener.
   227	public final class AudioDeviceMonitor: Sendable {
   228	  private let onDevicesChanged: @Sendable () -> Void
   229	  /// Stored listener block — CoreAudio requires the same reference for removal.
   230	  nonisolated(unsafe) private var listenerBlock: AudioObjectPropertyListenerBlock?
   231	
   232	  public init(onDevicesChanged: @escaping @Sendable () -> Void) {
   233	    self.onDevicesChanged = onDevicesChanged
   234	    startListening()
   235	  }
   236	
   237	  deinit {
   238	    stopListening()
   239	  }
   240	
   241	  private func startListening() {
   242	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   243	      mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices,
   244	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   245	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   246	    )
   247	
   248	    let callback = self.onDevicesChanged
   249	    let block: AudioObjectPropertyListenerBlock = { _, _ in
   250	      callback()
   251	    }
   252	    self.listenerBlock = block
   253	
   254	    AudioObjectAddPropertyListenerBlock(
   255	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
   256	      &propertyAddress,
   257	      nil,
   258	      block
   259	    )
   260	  }
   261	
   262	  private func stopListening() {
   263	    guard let block = listenerBlock else { return }
   264	
   265	    var propertyAddress = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   266	      mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices,
   267	      mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   268	      mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   269	    )
   270	
   271	    AudioObjectRemovePropertyListenerBlock(
   272	      AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
   273	      &propertyAddress,
   274	      nil,
   275	      block
   276	    )
   277	    listenerBlock = nil
   278	  }
   279	}
